name: Combat Mind
description: The psionicist gains an unusually keen understanding of his enemies and their fighting tactics. As a result, the psionicist's side in combat gains a -1 bonus to initiative die rolls (cumulative with other modifiers). On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist also gains a +1 AC bonus.
augments: Maintain for 4 PP/round to continue the combat awareness.
darkSunNotes: Gladiatorial psionicists in the arenas of Athas frequently employ combat mind to anticipate their opponents' attacks, thrilling the crowds with seemingly impossible dodges.

name: Danger Sense
description: The psionicist experiences a tingling sensation at the back of his neck when a hazard or threat is near. The power reveals the general direction of the threat but not its nature or timing. High check results provide a full round of warning before danger strikes, while lower results give only moments' notice. In all cases, the psionicist and companions gain a +2 bonus on surprise rolls. On a minimal success, the direction may be misleading.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/turn to keep danger sense active.
darkSunNotes: Athasian wastelanders prize this power above almost all others—in a world where predators lurk beneath every sand dune, a moment's warning can save an entire caravan.

name: Detect Psionics
description: You detect psionic auras. A psionic aura is given off by any active or permanent power, or during the use of any psionic feat. Characters who have levels in a psionic class, creatures with the psionic subtype, and creatures with the Wild Talent feat possess psionic auras. The amount of information revealed depends on how long you study a particular area. 1st Round: Presence or absence of psionic auras. 2nd Round: Number of different psionic auras and the strength of the most potent aura. 3rd Round: The strength and location of each aura. If the items or creatures bearing the auras are in line of sight, you can make Psicraft checks to determine the discipline involved in each aura (DC 15 + power level, or 15 + one-half manifester level for a non-power effect). Each round, you can turn to detect psionics in a new area. The power can penetrate barriers, but 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 feet of wood or dirt blocks it.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where psionics pervade every level of society from the lowliest slave to the mightiest sorcerer-king, this power reveals the invisible web of mental energy that saturates every crowded marketplace and templar checkpoint. Even wild talents flare like candle flames to the trained seer.

name: Detect Teleportation
description: You sense the use of any effects of the teleportation subdiscipline within the area. You sense the use of these powers whether or not you have line of sight or line of effect (although a force effect prevents this detection). When you sense the use of an appropriate power, you know the direction in which the power was used, though not the distance or the exact effect.
augments: If you spend 2 additional power points, this power's range increases to Medium (100 ft. + 10 ft./level).
darkSunNotes: Templars stationed at city gates use this power to catch smugglers and fugitives who attempt to bypass checkpoints through psychoportation. In the paranoid courts of the sorcerer-kings, unauthorized teleportation is often punishable by death — or worse, the obsidian mines.

name: Feel Sound
description: The psionicist's body becomes sensitive to sound waves, allowing him to continue hearing even when his ears are disabled. The power does not detect sound where there is none, nor is it effective inside magical silence. The psionicist gains a +2 bonus against sonic attacks or effects, including a siren's song.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/round to continue feeling sound.
darkSunNotes: In the obsidian mines beneath Urik, where the crack of an overseer's whip can mean death, enslaved psionicists use feel sound to monitor their captors even when deafened by the relentless hammering. The vibrations of Athas itself become their ears.

name: Know Course
description: This power allows the psionicist to hold an accurate course without deviation. To use this power, the psionicist must know where he is going—either a place he has been before or a location he can reference from a known location. The character must also know his current position. Once initiated, the character receives a strong feeling of which direction to go, and the directional sense continually updates as long as the power is maintained, ensuring arrival at the destination. On an exceptional manifestation, the character can retrace his steps as if using radial navigation.
augments: Maintain for 4 PP/hour to keep the course active.
darkSunNotes: Navigating the featureless Athasian wastes between city-states is notoriously treacherous. Know course has saved many a caravan master from wandering into the Sea of Silt or a field of deadly cacti.

name: Know Direction
description: The psionicist becomes his own compass. Upon successful manifestation, he instantly knows which direction is north. On an exceptional success, the power is automatically maintained for one full day without additional cost.
darkSunNotes: In the featureless Sea of Silt or the endless Athasian desert, knowing which way is north can be the difference between reaching an oasis and dying of thirst.

name: Know Direction and Location
description: You generally know where you are. This power is useful to characters who end up at an unknown location, such as those who have teleported astray, ## ## ## traveled through a portal, or been knocked unconscious and moved. The power tells you what direction you face and roughly how far you are from the nearest community or landmark your mind can reference.
darkSunNotes: Lost in the endless wastes between Tyr and Urik, with water running low and no stars visible through the dust haze — this is when a psion earns their keep in any caravan. On Athas, where a wrong turn means death by dehydration, this humble power saves more lives than any combat discipline.

name: Poison Sense
description: The psionicist detects the presence and location of any poison within 1 yard of his body, including animal venom. The type of poison is not revealed, only its presence and location. This power also functions through clairvoyance or while traveling astrally. On an exceptional manifestation, the exact type of poison is identified. On a critical failure, the sense of the poison mentally overwhelms the psionicist, producing effects matching actual exposure to the poison.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where assassins lace everything from water skins to ceramic daggers with poison, this power is a staple survival tool for any cautious psionicist.

name: Precognition, Defensive
description: Your precognitive abilities allow you to see a fraction of a second into the future, giving you an insight bonus of +1 to AC and saving throws for the duration of this power.
augments: For every 3 additional power points you spend, the insight bonus increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Defensive precognition is the psychic equivalent of a sixth sense — a constant, low-grade awareness of incoming threats that lets the manifester dodge strikes before they are thrown. On Athas, where ambush is a way of life and treachery is expected, this power has saved countless lives. Caravan guards who manifest defensive precognition are worth triple wages, their ability to sense danger before it arrives invaluable in the lawless wastes.

name: Precognition, Offensive
description: Your precognitive abilities allow you to see a fraction of a second into the future, giving you an insight bonus of +1 on your attack rolls for the duration of this power.
augments: For every 3 additional power points you spend, the insight bonus increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Even a heartbeat's warning can decide life or death in Athasian combat. Offensive precognition lets a warrior see where their opponent will be when the blow lands, guiding obsidian blades and bone spears with preternatural accuracy. Arena fighters who manifest this power are feared for their uncanny precision — every strike lands exactly where it will hurt most, as though the fight has already been decided.

name: Prescience, Offensive
description: Your awareness of the future grants you an advantage in combat. You gain a +2 insight bonus on your damage rolls for the duration of this power.
augments: You can augment this power in one or both of the following ways. 1. For every 3 additional power points you spend, the insight bonus on your damage rolls increases by 1. 2. If you spend 6 additional power points, you can manifest this power as a swift action.
darkSunNotes: In the blood-soaked arenas of Draj, even a slight edge in combat means the difference between glory and a shallow grave in the boneyard. Gladiator-psions learn this power early, channeling fleeting visions of their opponent's vulnerabilities into devastating strikes.

name: Radial Navigation
description: As long as this power is maintained, the psionicist knows exactly where he is in relation to a fixed starting point—the exact direction and distance, no matter how he moves. While this does not automatically allow retracing steps through a maze, the DM makes checks at decision points to determine if the character knows which way he came. Radial navigation enhances teleportation (providing a fix on the starting location), astral travel (reaching the fixed point in minimum time of seven hours), and dimension walking (no chance of getting lost). It also aids telepathy by eliminating range penalties when the target's location is fixed. If the power lapses, the fix can be recovered within six hours by resuming the power. Only one location can be fixed at a time unless maintenance cost is paid separately for each.
augments: Maintain for 7 PP/hour to keep the navigational fix active.
darkSunNotes: Athasian traders crossing the vast wastes between city-states rely on radial navigation to maintain their bearings when sandstorms erase all landmarks.

name: See Ethereal
description: The character extends his vision to the Border Ethereal with a range of 120 feet, detecting any ethereal characters, objects, or monsters. While in use, the character sees both the Ethereal Plane and the Prime Material Plane simultaneously. In addition to ethereal creatures, the psionicist can see phased or displaced creatures. Dimension walkers are revealed, and dimensional doors show as bright threads linking two portals. This power is useful when fighting ethereal or phased monsters such as ghosts and phase spiders. On an exceptional manifestation, the character can see out to the full range of his normal vision.
augments: Maintain for 2 PP/round to continue seeing the ethereal.
darkSunNotes: The Gray is never far on Athas, and ethereal undead haunt many ancient sites. See ethereal allows Athasian psionicists to spot threats from the spirit world before they can strike.

name: Sensitivity to Observation
description: The psionicist has the uncanny ability to know when he is being watched. This power is triggered by the intense attention of another person or creature and is always active unless consciously suppressed. A successful manifestation confirms the character's suspicion that he is being observed, but does not reveal the watcher's location. If the observer is using clairvoyance or clairaudience, the psionicist may attempt to obscure the scrying by winning a psychic contest. If successful, the watcher cannot scry against the psionicist for 1d4 hours. On an exceptional manifestation, the character immediately determines the location of his watcher.
darkSunNotes: In the paranoid courts of the sorcerer-kings, where templar spies use clairvoyance to monitor potential threats, sensitivity to observation is a vital early warning system for Athasian psionicists.

name: All-Round Vision
description: The psionicist gains the ability to see in all directions simultaneously, as if he had eyes in the back and sides of his head. No one can sneak up on the character without magical concealment. When combined with clairvoyance, this power provides complete visual coverage of a remote area. However, the psionicist suffers a -4 penalty against all gaze attacks while using this power.
augments: Maintain for 4 PP/round to continue all-round vision.
darkSunNotes: In the treacherous streets of Tyr or Nibenay, all-round vision can mean the difference between spotting an assassin's approach and taking a bone dagger between the shoulder blades.

name: Clairvoyant Sense
description: You can see and hear a distant location almost as if you were there. You don't need line of sight or line of effect, but the locale must be known — a place familiar to you or an obvious one, such as behind a door, around a corner, or in a grove of trees. Once you have selected the locale, the focus of your clairvoyant sense doesn't move, but you can rotate it in all directions to view the area as desired. Unlike other scrying powers, this power does not allow psionically or supernaturally enhanced senses to work through it. If the chosen locale is magically or psionically dark, you see nothing. If it is naturally pitch black, you can see in a 10-foot radius around the center of the power's effect or out to the extent of your natural darkvision. The power does not work across planes.
darkSunNotes: Essential tradecraft for Athasian seers. Veiled Alliance cells use this power to scout templar patrols from hidden cellars, while paranoid nobles employ it to spy on their own households. In a world where trust is a luxury no one can afford, seeing without being seen is priceless.

name: Darkvision, Psionic
description: As the darkvision spell, except as noted here. You gain the ability to see 60 feet even in total darkness. Darkvision is black and white only but otherwise like normal sight. Darkvision does not grant one the ability to see in magical or psionic darkness.
darkSunNotes: Beneath the Tablelands lie endless warrens — ancient dwarven ruins, thri-kreen egg chambers, and the tunnels connecting the underground portions of city-states. Gladiators forced to fight in lightless pits beneath the arenas of Tyr and Urik prize this power above almost any other.

name: Detect Hostile Intent
description: While the duration of this power lasts, you become aware of the presence of any creatures with hostile intent within 30 feet of you, and their direction from you (but not their specific location). The power detects active aggression, as opposed to vigilance. In addition, while this power is active you cannot be surprised or caught flat-footed by creatures that are susceptible to mind-affecting powers. While under the effect of this power, you can make Sense Motive checks as a free action against anyone within 30 feet of you. The power can penetrate barriers, but 3 feet of stone, 3 inches of common metal, 1 inch of lead, or 6 feet of wood or dirt blocks it.
darkSunNotes: In the cutthroat bazaars of Nibenay and the slave pens of Balic, everyone is a potential enemy. This power has saved countless merchants from assassination and allowed gladiatorial champions to sense ambushes in the darkened tunnels beneath the arena floor.

name: Feel Light
description: The psionicist's entire body becomes a receiver for light waves, allowing him to experience light through tactile sensations. His body effectively replaces his eyes, enabling him to see what his eyes would normally reveal (field of vision unchanged). This power does not allow him to see in darkness, since there must be light to feel, nor does it counter magical darkness. The character gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against gaze attacks.
augments: Maintain for 5 PP/round to continue feeling light.
darkSunNotes: Under the dark sun's blazing crimson light, a blinded psionicist can still navigate the desert by feeling Athas's relentless radiance on his skin.

name: Hear Light
description: A blinded character can 'see' with his ears by converting light waves to sound. Any light waves that reach the psionicist are converted to auditory impulses, and he 'hears' the image. The perceived image is identical to normal sight, and the character suffers no penalties for anything requiring vision. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist can 'hear' in the dark as if he had infravision.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/round to continue hearing light.
darkSunNotes: Blind gladiators in the arenas of Draj have astonished crowds by fighting as though sighted, their psionically attuned hearing translating the blazing Athasian sun into a symphony of combat awareness that no blindfold can defeat.

name: Know Location
description: This power reveals general information about the psionicist's current location, as if asking a local farmer 'Where am I?' Typical answers might include relative distance and direction to a known landmark, the name of a building or settlement, or the body of water the character is adrift upon. Higher check results yield more precise information (within a mile on high rolls, within 10 miles otherwise). The psionicist can ask follow-up questions for less specific information without making additional checks.
darkSunNotes: Useful for psionicists who travel the Gray or use psychoportation across the Tablelands—Athas's shifting sands and featureless wastes make mundane navigation nearly impossible.

name: Read Thoughts
description: As the detect thoughts spell, except as noted here. This power detects surface thoughts. The amount of information revealed depends on how long you study a particular area or subject. 1st Round: Presence or absence of thoughts (from conscious creatures with Intelligence scores of 1 or higher). 2nd Round: Number of thinking minds and the Intelligence score of each. If the highest Intelligence is 26 or higher (and at least 10 points higher than your own Intelligence score), you are stunned for 1 round and the power ends. 3rd Round: Surface thoughts of any mind in the area. A target's Will save prevents you from reading its thoughts, and you must cast the power anew to check the same target again.
darkSunNotes: Templars of every city-state employ this power during interrogations and at gate checkpoints. The merchant houses of Athas consider it the single greatest threat to their trade secrets, investing heavily in psionic countermeasures. In the courts of Nibenay, thinking the wrong thought at the wrong moment can be a death sentence.

name: Safe Path
description: The psionicist uses her danger sense abilities to guide her steps through hazardous situations. By listening for the warning tingle of danger sense, she knows when to duck, dodge, and move forward. A character using this power for dangerous feats of climbing, balance, or coordination gains a +4 bonus to ability checks and saving throws required. The character can walk blindly across a room full of traps and still receive the bonus to avoid them. In combat, the psionicist gains a -2 bonus to Armor Class and a +4 bonus to saving throws each round the power is maintained. On an exceptional manifestation, the character automatically succeeds on her first saving throw or ability check.
augments: Maintain for 5 PP/round to continue the safe path effect.
darkSunNotes: In the trap-laden ruins of Giustenal and the deadly obstacle courses of gladiatorial arenas, safe path gives an Athasian psionicist an edge in navigating lethal environments by instinct alone.

name: See Magic
description: The psionicist can perceive wizardly magic, including magical items, effects, and potential. Any enchanted items, scrolls, spellbooks, or potions glow faintly in the psionicist's vision. Continuing spell effects such as fire traps, walls of stone, or charm effects on characters can be seen. The power does not identify the type of magic being used—only that the item or person radiates magical energy. On a superior manifestation, the psionicist can also see the spell potential of a magic-using character, perceiving how many memorized spells remain. This power cannot detect priestly magic. On an exceptional manifestation, the character discerns the general type of enchantment on an item or area.
augments: Maintain for 6 PP/round to continue seeing magic.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where defiler magic is reviled and preserver magic is hidden, the ability to see magic gives a psionicist crucial intelligence about who wields arcane power and what enchantments lurk nearby.

name: See Sound
description: The psionicist can perceive sound waves visually by converting them to light impulses. Only a character who can see normally can use this power. The psionicist can see sound even in darkness, because sound waves do not require light. However, magical silence can effectively 'blind' the character's sound-sight.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/round to continue seeing sound.
darkSunNotes: The thri-kreen of the Hinterlands, already gifted with extraordinary senses, regard this power as the mark of a truly accomplished clutch-mind—one who can perceive a sandstorm's roar as a wall of visible force and read the whispered plots of elven traders as glowing script upon the air.

name: Spirit Sense
description: The psionicist can sense the presence of spirits—ghosts, banshees, wraiths, haunts, heucuvas, and revenants—within 15 yards. If a spirit frequently haunts the location, the psionicist will know it. He can detect when a spirit is within range but cannot pinpoint its exact location under normal circumstances. On an exceptional manifestation, the exact locations of all sensed spirits are revealed.
darkSunNotes: The Gray, Athas's plane of the dead, bleeds into the material world more readily than on other worlds. Spirit sense is essential for detecting the restless dead that lurk in ancient ruins and defiled lands.

name: Watcher's Ward
target: Up to 60 ft. radius centered on psionicist
description: The psionicist attunes herself to her surroundings and becomes extremely sensitive to any disturbances within the warded area, which can range from a single door to a circle of 60 feet radius centered on herself. If the psionicist moves out of the area, she must re-initiate the power. While active, the psionicist cannot be surprised by the approach of any creature or force. She is instantly aware of any changes in the area, but does not automatically spot the intruders—she only knows that something is coming. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist instantly determines the exact location and type of any intruders.
augments: Maintain for 2 PP/hour to keep the ward active.
darkSunNotes: Desert travelers on Athas use watcher's ward to secure their campsites against the deadly nocturnal predators that prowl the wastes. A psionicist on watch with this power is nearly impossible to ambush.

name: Aura Sight
description: The psionicist can perceive the luminous aura that surrounds all living creatures. An aura reflects both the subject's alignment and experience level. Each round, the psionicist can examine up to two auras, learning either alignment or experience level (not both) per reading. The subject's experience level imposes a -1 penalty per three levels on the attempt. The psionicist must gaze intently at the subject, which may be noticed at close range but can be done discreetly from a distance.
augments: Maintain for 9 PP/round to continue reading auras.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where deception is a way of life, aura sight is invaluable for detecting the true nature of templar agents and defiler sorcerer-kings' servants.

name: Bone Reading
description: The psionicist can use a creature's remains to form an impression of who it was and what it was doing when it perished. Fragments of bone are usually used, but the power works on any corpse or portion of a corpse. The amount of information gained depends on the success of the manifestation: the deceased's race, sex, age, identity, appearance and alignment, date of death, and method of death can all be determined, with more information gained on better rolls. The information is cumulative. This power is more difficult to use on very old remains, with increasing penalties for remains older than one day. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist can view the last 10 minutes of the deceased's life from the departed's point of view.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the desert sands hide countless bones of the fallen, bone reading lets a psionicist piece together the violent history of the Tablelands one fragment at a time. Templars and auditors use this power to investigate deaths and uncover hidden crimes.

name: Clairaudience
description: The psionicist can hear sounds from a distant location as if he were standing there. Enhanced senses also apply to the remote listening. Background noise around the psionicist is not screened out and may interfere. The power does not grant understanding of foreign languages or help interpret ambiguous sounds. Range modifiers apply: 100 yards (no penalty), 1,000 yards (-2), 10 miles (-4), 100 miles (-6), 1,000 miles (-8), 10,000 miles (-10), interplanetary (-12). The power works only within a given crystal sphere or plane.
augments: Maintain for 4 PP/round to continue listening.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the wind carries secrets across the Tablelands, clairaudience lets a psionicist eavesdrop on distant merchant caravans or templar patrols.

name: Clairvoyance
description: The psionicist can see images from a distant location as if standing there, with a normal field of vision that can be scanned by turning his head. The distant scene is superimposed over normal sight, so most clairvoyants close their eyes to avoid confusion. The power does not reveal invisible or hidden objects, nor does it provide sound. Once a viewing spot is chosen, it is fixed; viewing another location requires a new use of the power. The clairvoyant presence is undetectable by normal means and cannot be dispelled or repelled by magic. Range modifiers apply: 100 yards (no penalty), 1,000 yards (-2), 10 miles (-4), 100 miles (-6), 1,000 miles (-8), 10,000 miles (-10), interplanetary (-12).
augments: Maintain for 4 PP/round to continue viewing.
darkSunNotes: Athasian psionicists use clairvoyance to scout the wastes for raider bands and locate hidden oases from the safety of a city-state.

name: Detection
description: The psionicist can find substances or items by their psychic vibrations. This power can be used to dowse for water, find lost items, or prospect for precious minerals. To locate a substance, the psionicist must have a sample of what he is searching for. To locate a specific item, he must have seen it himself or successfully used object reading on its owner. The initial search radius is 30 yards, and by maintaining the power the psionicist can expand the radius by 10 yards per round. At first, only the nearest location of the desired substance is detected. By maintaining the power, additional sources are detected at a rate of one per round, from nearest to farthest.
augments: Maintain for 6 PP/round to continue expanding the search radius.
darkSunNotes: On water-scarce Athas, detection is perhaps the most immediately life-saving power a clairsentient can possess. Dowsing for water beneath the burning sands has saved countless caravans from death by dehydration.

name: Escape Detection
description: You (plus all your gear and any objects you carry) become difficult to detect by clairsentience powers such as clairvoyant sense, remote viewing, and psionic true seeing. If a clairsentience power or similar effect is attempted against you, the manifester of the power must succeed on a manifester level check (1d20 + manifester level, or caster level if the opponent is not a manifester) against a DC of 13 + your manifester level (maximum +10).
darkSunNotes: The Veiled Alliance's most guarded secret is not a spell or a hideout — it is the network of psions trained to shroud their members from the all-seeing eyes of templar seers. Without this power, resistance movements on Athas would have been crushed generations ago.

name: Object Reading
description: The psionicist detects psychic impressions left on an object by its previous owners. Depending on the success of the manifestation, the psionicist can learn the last owner's race, sex, age, alignment, how the owner gained and lost the item, or all such information about every previous owner. An object can be read only once per experience level of the psionicist; additional readings at that level reveal no new information.
darkSunNotes: In a world where metal is scarce and every weapon has a storied history, object reading can reveal the bloody provenance of an ancient steel sword.

name: Sensitivity to Psychic Impressions
description: The psionicist gains a sense of history, perceiving the residue of powerful emotions experienced in a given area. Only events that elicited strong emotional or psychic energy—battles, betrayals, marriages, murders, great pain—leave impressions; everyday occurrences do not. The number of strong events the psionicist can sense depends on the success of the manifestation (half the check result, rounded up), with only one event perceived per round, beginning with the most recent and proceeding backward through time. The understanding is vague and dreamlike, though the dominant emotion comes through clearly.
augments: Maintain for 2 PP/round to continue sensing impressions.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, nearly every ruin echoes with centuries of suffering and defiler magic. A psionicist using this power in the ruins of Giustenal or Yaramuke may be overwhelmed by the sheer volume of psychic anguish.

name: Touchsight
description: You generate a subtle telekinetic field of mental contact, giving you blindsight out to 60 feet. You can perceive and pinpoint all creatures and objects within 60 feet, even if they are invisible or obscured. This includes creatures and objects you could not otherwise see or detect. Your touchsight does not function in areas of psionic suppression.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, the range of your touchsight increases by 10 feet.
darkSunNotes: When the crimson dust storms roll across the Tablelands and blot out the dark sun itself, touchsight becomes the only reliable sense. Thri-kreen hunting packs instinctively understand the concept — their antennae provide a natural analogue — and they respect humanoid psions who replicate it through sheer mental discipline.

name: True Sight
description: The psionicist can pierce any normal or magical obscurement to a range of 60 feet. Invisible creatures or objects are detected, unless someone is using psionic invisibility against the psionicist. The character can pierce illusions and detect anything phased, displaced, or ethereal. Unlike the clerical spell true seeing, this power does not confer the ability to see alignments, enchantments, polymorphed items, or secret doors. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist gains the full benefits of the true seeing spell as well.
augments: Maintain for 9 PP/round to continue the true sight effect.
darkSunNotes: In a world where defiler illusions and psionic invisibility are common tools of assassins and templars, true sight is an invaluable defense for any Athasian clairsentient.

name: Ubiquitous Vision
description: You have psychic sensitivity to your surroundings that is so great that your senses extend around corners and through walls up to a certain distance. You have all-around vision, eliminating your flanking bonus for enemies and allowing you to make Spot checks in any direction. You also gain a +4 enhancement bonus on Spot and Search checks, and you do not take penalties from being flanked.
darkSunNotes: No back alleys in the warrens of Tyr's Caravan District are safe for assassins when their target has manifested this power. Mul bodyguards who combine ubiquitous vision with their natural toughness become nearly impossible to ambush — they see every angle, every shadow, every blade drawn behind them.

name: Anchored Navigation
description: You know where you are in relation to a fixed starting point, which is essential for setting up a mishap-free teleport beacon. While the duration lasts, you are aware of your exact distance and route (physical or psychoportive) back to a fixed starting point. The 'anchored' starting point is your exact location when you manifest the power. To designate other anchored starting points, you must manifest this power multiple times and be present at the desired locations when you do so. You can also retrace your steps through a maze automatically while the power lasts, without resorting to a map. Anchored navigation grants you a mindlink with one designated creature who remains within a 60-foot radius of the starting point, regardless of the distance between you and the creature. The use of anchored navigation is confined to the plane of existence where you manifest it.
augments: If you spend 6 additional power points, the effect of this power extends across all planar boundaries.
darkSunNotes: Navigators in the Sea of Silt swear by this power, using it to maintain unerring bearings through the blinding grey storms that swallow entire merchant caravans. The mind-anchor holds fast even when sandstorms scour away every landmark for miles.

name: Detect Remote Viewing
description: You immediately become aware of any attempt to observe you by means of a clairsentience (scrying) power or divination (scrying) spell. The power's effect radiates from you and moves as you move. You know the location of every psionic or magical sensor within the power's area. If the viewing attempt originates within the area, you also know the viewer's location. Otherwise, you and the remote viewer immediately make opposed manifester level checks (1d20 + manifester level, or viewer's caster level as appropriate). If you at least match the remote viewer's result, you get a visual image of the remote viewer and an accurate sense of the remote viewer's direction and distance from you.
darkSunNotes: The psionic cold war between the sorcerer-kings makes this power indispensable. Hamanu's seers scry on Nibenay's shadow consorts while Nibenay's own watchers hunt for prying eyes. In this endless game of mental espionage, detecting the watcher is often more valuable than the watched secret itself.

name: Divination, Psionic
description: As the divination spell, except as noted here. You gain useful advice in reply to a question concerning a specific goal, event, or activity that is to occur within one week. The power provides a meaningful piece of advice that can help guide future actions. The chance for a correct divination is 70% + 1% per manifester level (maximum 90%).
darkSunNotes: The seers of Athas peer through the crimson haze of the future, seeking guidance among the tangled threads of fate. Psionic divination draws on Clairsentience to glimpse what lies ahead — but on a world as chaotic and brutal as Athas, the future is a treacherous landscape. The answers received are often cryptic, filtered through the interference of the Gray and the psychic noise of a billion desperate minds.

name: Precognition
description: The psionicist foresees the probable outcome of a described course of action, limited to the near future (no more than several hours ahead). The course of action must be described in some detail to establish the chain of events. On success, the psionicist sees the most likely outcome, with higher rolls yielding more detail. However, precognition offers no guarantees—deviations from the described actions, die rolls, and random chance can all alter the outcome. On a critical failure, the psionicist sees a terrifying vision of his own death and must make a saving throw vs. petrification or suffer a -3 penalty to all psionic power scores for 1d6 hours. After using precognition, the psionicist must rest for at least one turn before using any other clairsentient power.
darkSunNotes: Athasian seers who master precognition are prized advisors to merchant houses and sorcerer-kings alike, though the visions of Athas's brutal future can shatter even the strongest mind.

name: Remote Viewing
description: As the scrying spell, except as noted here. You can observe a creature at any distance. If the subject succeeds on a Will save, the remote viewing attempt fails, and you can't attempt to view that creature again for 24 hours. The difficulty of the save depends on how well you know the subject and what sort of physical connection (if any) you have to that creature. The DC of the Will save is modified based on knowledge and connection, similar to scrying.
darkSunNotes: The great psionic spymasters of Athas wage invisible wars through remote viewing, tracking rival agents across the Tablelands. Lalali-Puy's seers in Gulg peer through the forest canopy to monitor elven raiding parties, while Andropinis's viewers once watched the very borders of the Black before his imprisonment.

name: Spirit Lore
description: The psionicist communicates with spirits to gain information about deeply-hidden secrets or unravel mysteries. The psionicist must ask very specific questions; the spirits never volunteer information and often seek to distort or confuse the truth. The quality of the spirit's response depends on the success of the manifestation: the spirit may lie outright, embellish partial truths, admit ignorance, disguise the truth in riddles, or answer truthfully. The psionicist may ask one question per round. A clever character can cross-examine a spirit to determine its truthfulness. On an exceptional manifestation, a particularly knowledgeable and helpful spirit is contacted. On a critical failure, the psionicist contacts a malicious entity that attempts to possess him.
augments: Maintain for 8 PP/round to continue questioning spirits.
darkSunNotes: The Gray bleeds close to the material world on Athas, making spirit lore both more accessible and more dangerous. The restless dead of the Cleansing Wars harbor secrets spanning millennia, but their bitterness makes them treacherous informants.

name: Steadfast Perception
description: Your flawed perceptions of reality are bolstered against deception. You gain a +6 insight bonus on Spot checks and Search checks. Additionally, you are immune to all illusory effects for the duration of this power.
darkSunNotes: In the mirage-haunted wastes of Athas, where heat shimmer and defiler illusions conspire to mislead travelers, steadfast perception cuts through every deception. Rangers who cross the salt flats between Raam and Draj consider it more essential than water — you can survive thirst for a day, but a mirage-trap kills instantly.

name: Clairtangent Hand
description: You can emulate a far hand effect at any distance while simultaneously emulating clairvoyant sense to target your far hand; see the appropriate power descriptions. Clairtangent hand's duration is up to 1 minute per level when used with a far hand effect.
augments: If you spend 8 additional power points, you can emulate clairvoyant sense in conjunction with either telekinetic force, telekinetic maneuver, or telekinetic thrust, but this power's duration expires as soon as any of the noted telekinetic powers deals damage.
darkSunNotes: Sorcerer-king spymasters use this power to manipulate objects in distant chambers — turning locks, planting evidence, or poisoning goblets — all while observing through the veil of clairsentience from the safety of their obsidian towers.

name: Cosmic Awareness
description: The High Science of Clairsentience. The psionicist perceives everything occurring within the area of effect with extraordinary clarity. She gains X-ray vision extending into the astral and ethereal planes, detecting all inanimate features including hidden caves, secret doors, traps, and unusual mineral deposits. All forces—winds, water currents, spell effects such as glyphs of warding and areas of reversed gravity—are perceived. All living creatures larger than a microbe are detected; the power automatically detects invisibility, evil/good, and magic. Illusions are seen for what they are, shapechanged or polymorphed creatures are recognized in their true forms, and ethereal, astral, phased, ectoplasmic, or gaseous creatures are seen clearly. Equipment details down to the number of coins in a purse are detected. All sounds and smells within the area are also perceived. On the first round, the psionicist perceives everything within a 10-foot radius. For each additional round maintained, the perception radius extends by another 10 feet.
augments: Maintain for 10 PP/round to extend the perception radius by 10 ft. each round.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where danger lurks beneath every dune and ancient ruins hold hidden terrors, cosmic awareness transforms a psionicist into an all-seeing sentinel. Only the most dedicated clairsentients of the Tyr region master this High Science, and those who do are prized by sorcerer-kings and merchant houses alike.

name: Remote View Trap
description: When others use clairsentience powers to view you from afar, your prepared trap gives them a nasty surprise. If a remote viewer attempts to observe you with a scrying power or spell, the viewer must make a Will save. If the save fails, you are undetected by the remote viewing, and the viewer is instead stunned for 1 round. Furthermore, you can choose to observe the remote viewer as if through a window for the remainder of their scrying attempt. You can choose not to spring the trap and instead allow the remote viewing to function normally.
darkSunNotes: In the psionic arms race between the city-states, this power represents the ultimate countermeasure. A well-placed remote view trap can incapacitate an enemy seer and expose their location, turning the hunter into the hunted. Shadow brides of Nibenay are rumored to keep this power active at all times.

name: Second Chance
description: You can reroll one attack roll, one saving throw, one ability check, or one skill check. You must take the result of the reroll, even if it's worse than the original roll. You do not have to make another roll if satisfied with your original roll.
darkSunNotes: The future is not fixed on Athas — or so the seers claim. This power lets a psion reach back through the timestream and seize a different thread of fate. Arena bookmakers in Tyr have been known to hire seers just to detect when a gladiator uses this power, as it makes gambling outcomes maddeningly unpredictable.

name: Moment of Prescience, Psionic
description: As the moment of prescience spell, except as noted here. This power grants you a powerful sixth sense in relation to yourself. Once during the power's duration, you may choose to use its effect. This grants you an insight bonus equal to your manifester level (maximum +25) on any single attack roll, opposed ability or skill check, or saving throw. Alternatively, you can apply the insight bonus to your AC against a single attack (even if flat-footed). Activating the effect doesn't take an action; you can even activate it on another character's turn if needed. You must choose to use the effect before the roll is made. Once used, the power ends.
darkSunNotes: Gladiatorial legends speak of arena champions who seemed to move before their opponents struck — dodging killing blows by a hair's breadth, landing impossible strikes. These fighters were not merely skilled; they had seers in the stands feeding them prescient visions, or possessed the talent themselves.

name: Bend Reality
description: Bend reality lets you create nearly any type of effect. It can duplicate any psion power of 6th level or lower (provided the power is not of a prohibited discipline), any other power (but not a spell) of 5th level or lower, or any psion power of 5th level or lower even if it's of a prohibited discipline. It can also undo the harmful effects of many powers such as psionic dominate, geas/quest, or insanity. A duplicated power allows saving throws and power resistance as normal (but the save DC is for an 8th-level power). XP Cost: 300 XP or more.
darkSunNotes: Bend reality represents the terrifying ability to rewrite the laws of the universe through sheer psychic will. On Athas, where the fabric of reality has already been stretched thin by millennia of defiling magic and the imprisonment of the elemental lords, bend reality carries additional risks — the manifester's desires can ripple outward through the wounded metaphysics of the world in unpredictable ways.

name: Hypercognition
manifestingTime: 1 standard action or 1 immediate action; see text
description: You make lightning-fast deductions based on only the slightest clue, pattern, or scrap of memory resident in your mind. You can make reasonable statements about a person, place, or object, seemingly from very little knowledge. However, your knowledge is in fact the result of a rigorously logical process that you force your mind to undertake, digging up and correlating every possible piece of knowledge bearing on the topic (possibly even extracting echoes of knowledge from the Astral Plane). The nature of the knowledge you gain might include the answer to a riddle, the way out of a maze, stray bits of information about a person, legends about a place or an object, or even a conclusion concerning a dilemma that your conscious mind is unable to arrive at. An Intelligence check may be required to obtain the desired information. If so, you can manifest hypercognition as an immediate action prior to making the check and receive a +20 bonus for doing so.
darkSunNotes: The great seer-advisors of Athas command staggering fees for this power. A single manifestation can unravel a templar conspiracy, decode an ancient dwarven inscription, or deduce the location of a hidden oasis from the flight patterns of erdlu. The sorcerer-kings themselves are said to use it when plotting against each other.

name: Metafaculty
description: You can learn the answer to any question about a specific creature, provided you have at least one piece of information about the creature to begin with (even a general description is enough). You learn the creature's name, where it is at that moment, some details about the creature's current activities, a general knowledge of the creature's current mental state, and any detail about its present physical condition. This power can defeat any attempt at concealment by magical or psionic means (including mind blank) and can even reach across planar boundaries. It can defeat any attempt at deception, including alter self, polymorph, and similar effects. The only way to prevent the operation of this power is through the use of an artifact or the direct intervention of a deity.
darkSunNotes: This is the power that makes even sorcerer-kings pause. To know exactly where Rajaat's last champion hides, or to pierce the veils surrounding the Pristine Tower — such knowledge reshapes the political landscape of Athas. Those who manifest it become targets themselves, hunted by every power that fears exposure.

name: Reality Revision
description: As bend reality, but with more far-reaching effects. Reality revision can duplicate any psion power of 8th level or lower, any other power of 6th level or lower, any psion power of 7th level or lower even if it is prohibited, undo harmful effects of powers such as microcosm or insanity, create nonpsionic items of up to 25,000 gp in value, create or add to psionic items, grant inherent ability score bonuses (+1 to +5), remove injuries and afflictions, revive the dead, transport travelers across planes, or undo recent misfortune. The minimum XP cost is 5,000 XP.
darkSunNotes: Reality revision is the province of the sorcerer-kings and the most powerful psionicists of the Order — those who can literally reshape the world through the force of their will. On Athas, where reality itself has been scarred by the Pristine Tower and the Dark Lens, this power carries cosmic weight. It is said that Rajaat himself used a version of reality revision to create the Champions who would become the sorcerer-kings, rewriting the fundamental nature of mortals to serve his genocidal vision.

name: Astral Construct
description: This power creates one 1st-level astral construct of solidified ectoplasm that attacks your enemies. It appears where you designate and acts immediately, on your turn. It attacks your opponents to the best of its ability. As a free action, you can mentally direct it not to attack, to attack particular enemies, or to perform other actions. The astral construct acts normally on the last round of the power's duration and dissipates at the end of its turn. Astral constructs are not combatants and cannot be targeted as such.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power creates a construct of one level higher (to a maximum of a 9th-level construct at 17 additional power points).
darkSunNotes: On magic-starved Athas, astral constructs are among the most visible demonstrations of psionic might. In the gladiatorial arenas, shapers who summon shimmering ectoplasmic warriors draw roars from the crowd. Some constructs are shaped to resemble extinct creatures — the great lizards that roamed Athas in the Green Age.

name: Bolt
description: You create a shimmering bolt of crystallized ectoplasm that you can hurl at a target as a ranged attack. The bolt deals 1d10 points of damage. The bolt is created in your hand and must be thrown or fired from a crossbow within 1 round of being created, or it dissipates harmlessly.
darkSunNotes: Psychic warriors among the slave tribes forge these crystalline missiles from raw will, bypassing the metal scarcity that cripples conventional archers. A bolt of solidified thought costs nothing but mental effort — a currency even the poorest Athasian possesses in abundance.

name: Call Weaponry
description: You create a nonmagical, nonpsionic melee weapon of any type that you are proficient with. The weapon appears in your hands and is made of ectoplasm that looks, feels, and behaves in most respects like a normal weapon of its type but dissipates when the power ends. The weapon deals normal damage for a weapon of its type. It can be broken or sundered as normal, and it can also be enhanced with a personal weapon augmentation.
augments: For every 4 additional power points you spend, this power creates a weapon with a +1 enhancement bonus. The maximum enhancement bonus you can create is +5.
darkSunNotes: In a world where metal is worth more than life, the ability to conjure a weapon from nothing is revolutionary. Escaped slaves with this talent need never be unarmed again. The weapon vanishes when the power fades, leaving no evidence for templar inspectors who search for contraband arms.

name: Crystal Shard
description: Upon manifesting this power, you propel a razor-sharp crystal shard at your target. You must succeed on a ranged touch attack to hit. The ray deals 1d6 points of piercing damage.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by 1d6 points.
darkSunNotes: The most basic attack in a shaper's arsenal, yet deadly effective. The crystalline missile appears from nothing, a shard of pure psychic will hardened to razor sharpness. In Athas's metal-poor economy, a psion who can hurl crystal needles at will is as dangerous as any archer — and far cheaper to supply.

name: Ecto Protection
range: Personal or close (25 ft. + 5 ft./2 levels)
description: This power grants an astral construct or other ectoplasmic creature you created a measure of protection. The subject gains a +1 deflection bonus to AC. If manifested on yourself while you are wearing ectoplasmic armor, you gain the +1 deflection bonus instead.
augments: For every 4 additional power points you spend, the deflection bonus increases by 1 (maximum +5 at 17 additional power points).
darkSunNotes: Shapers on Athas treat their constructs like prized war beasts, reinforcing them with additional layers of ectoplasmic hardening. In the gladiatorial pits of Draj, where shapers are sometimes forced to fight alongside their creations, a well-protected construct can mean the difference between triumph and the bone pile.

name: Entangling Ectoplasm
description: You draw forth a glob of ectoplasm that adheres to the target on a successful ranged touch attack. The subject is entangled for the duration. An entangled creature takes a -2 penalty on attack rolls and a -4 penalty to Dexterity. An entangled creature that attempts to manifest a power or cast a spell must make a Concentration check (DC 15 + power or spell level) or lose the power or spell.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power can affect a target one size category larger.
darkSunNotes: This sticky glob of ectoplasm is the shaper's answer to an opponent trying to flee across the open desert. Many a thri-kreen raider has been brought low by this power, their powerful legs tangled in strands of quasi-real matter that hardens like resin in the brutal Athasian heat.

name: Metaphysical Claw
description: If you have a claw attack (either from an actual claw or from a power such as claws of the beast), you can use this power to provide your claws with an enhancement bonus of +1 on attack rolls and damage rolls.
augments: If you spend 4 additional power points, this power's duration increases to 1 hour per level. In addition, for every 4 additional power points you spend, the enhancement bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Thri-kreen psychic warriors channel this power through their natural claws, wreathing them in a faint crystalline sheen that hardens the chitin beyond its natural limits. Among the mantis warriors of the Hinterlands, this is considered a rite of passage — a young hunter's first successful manifestation marks their transition to adulthood.

name: Metaphysical Weapon
description: Metaphysical weapon gives a weapon you touch a +1 enhancement bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls. An enhancement bonus does not stack with a masterwork weapon's +1 bonus on attack rolls.
augments: If you spend 4 additional power points, this power's duration increases to 1 hour per level. In addition, for every 4 additional power points you spend, the enhancement bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: In a world where most weapons are made from bone, obsidian, or hardened wood, this power bridges the gap between crude materials and masterwork steel. A psychic warrior who touches an obsidian macahuitl and infuses it with metacreative energy transforms a brittle club into a weapon that strikes like forged iron.

name: Minor Creation, Psionic
target: Nonmagical, unattended object of nonliving vegetable matter, up to 1 cu. ft./level
description: As the minor creation spell, except as noted here. You create a nonmagical, unattended object of nonliving, vegetable matter. The volume of the item created cannot exceed 1 cubic foot per manifester level.
darkSunNotes: Even the humblest creation — a wooden splint, a length of rope, a waterskin — can save a life in the Athasian wastes. Shapers who travel with merchant caravans earn their keep by conjuring replacement wagon parts, emergency shelters, and other necessities from the raw stuff of thought.

name: Concealing Amorpha
description: You form an ill-defined mass of ectoplasm 1 inch thick that hovers near your body. The concealing amorpha grants you concealment (20% miss chance) from all attacks directed at you, though you can still be targeted normally.
darkSunNotes: A shimmering veil of half-real matter clings to the shaper like desert heat haze, blurring their outline against the scorched landscape. In the alleys of Under-Tyr, this rippling shroud of ectoplasm has deflected many an assassin's poisoned blade.

name: Psionic Repair Damage
description: When laying your hands upon a construct that has at least 1 hit point remaining, you reknit its structure to repair damage it has taken. The power repairs 3d8 points of damage +1 point per manifester level (maximum +10).
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power repairs an additional 1d8 points of damage.
darkSunNotes: Astral constructs on Athas are valued like war beasts — expensive to create and devastating to lose. A shaper who can repair their constructs mid-battle extends their combat effectiveness enormously. In the siege warfare between city-states, construct repair crews are considered as vital as water supplies.

name: Swarm of Crystals
description: Thousands of tiny crystal shards spray forth in a cone from your hand, dealing 3d4 points of slashing damage to every creature in the area.
darkSunNotes: A psychic warrior's hands erupt with a spray of razor-thin crystal fragments, shredding exposed flesh like a sandstorm concentrated into a single devastating blast. In the gladiatorial arenas, this crowd-pleasing power is known as 'the shaper's breath' — beautiful, deadly, and over in an instant.

name: Concealing Amorpha, Greater
description: As concealing amorpha, except the ectoplasm is denser and provides total concealment (50% miss chance). The ectoplasm does not impede your vision.
darkSunNotes: The dense ectoplasmic shroud envelops the psion like a personal sandstorm, rendering them nearly invisible. Agents of House Stel have been known to employ shapers with this power for high-stakes smuggling runs across templar-patrolled territories, where being seen means death.

name: Dismiss Ectoplasm
description: You dismiss an ectoplasmic creature or object, causing it to dissipate back into the Astral Plane. An astral construct or other ectoplasmic creature that fails its save dissipates in a flash. A character who is wrapped or encased in ectoplasm (such as by ectoplasmic cocoon) can be freed by this power. If the ectoplasm was created by a power that allows power resistance, this power can dismiss it with a successful manifester level check.
darkSunNotes: When rival shapers clash in the streets of Raam's crumbling districts, dismiss ectoplasm is the great equalizer — a single manifestation can unravel an opponent's carefully constructed ectoplasmic fortress or banish their summoned constructs back to the gray nothingness from which they came.

name: Ectoplasmic Cocoon
description: You draw writhing strands of ectoplasm from the Astral Plane that wrap up the subject like a mummy. The subject is helpless, unable to move, speak, or take any physical actions. The subject's eyes are covered, rendering the subject blind. The subject can breathe but cannot use powers that require verbal components. The cocoon has hardness 8 and 20 hit points. If the cocoon loses all its hit points, it is destroyed and the creature is freed.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power can affect a target one size category larger.
darkSunNotes: Slavers and bounty hunters prize this power for capturing targets alive. The ectoplasmic cocoon wraps around a fugitive slave or political dissident like a second skin, immobilizing them completely for transport back to the city-state. Some templar interrogators leave subjects cocooned for days, softening their will before questioning.

name: Fabricate, Psionic
target: Up to 10 cu. ft./level; see text
description: As the fabricate spell, except as noted here. You convert material of one sort into a product that is of the same material. You can create items that require a high degree of craftsmanship with a successful appropriate Craft check.
darkSunNotes: On metal-starved Athas, a shaper who can fabricate bone, chitin, or obsidian into finished goods — weapons, armor, tools — commands enormous respect and wealth. Entire villages have been built around a single psion's ability to reshape raw materials into survival essentials without forge or flame.

name: Quintessence
description: You collapse a bit of time from the Astral Plane into a 1-inch-diameter sphere of quintessence, a substance that freezes time for any nonliving matter it touches. Quintessence can be used to preserve food, stabilize a rapidly deteriorating material, or in countless other ways. Objects sealed in quintessence are protected from all forms of deterioration and from the passage of time itself. Living matter sealed in quintessence appears to be in stasis; the creature does not age, breathe, grow hungry, or regain spells. A layer of quintessence can be removed by physical means (dealing 1 point of damage to the layer for each 1-foot square), whereupon the released object returns to normal.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where food spoils in hours under the crimson sun and water evaporates from sealed containers, quintessence is liquid gold. Shapers who can freeze time itself around precious supplies are the most sought-after members of any caravan. Rumors persist of ancient treasure vaults sealed in quintessence since the Green Age, their contents perfectly preserved.

name: Wall of Ectoplasm
target: An opaque sheet of ectoplasm up to 20 ft. long/level or a ring of ectoplasm with a radius of up to 5 ft./2 levels; either form 20 ft. high
description: You fashion a wall of ectoplasm. The wall cannot be seen through, blocking line of sight. Creatures can attack through the wall by making a melee attack against it. The wall has hardness 0 and a number of hit points equal to 10 per inch of thickness. A section of wall whose hit points drop to 0 is breached. Creatures can walk through the gap. The wall can be formed as a flat surface or shaped into a half-circle or full circle to enclose creatures within it.
darkSunNotes: In the open-field battles that rage across the Tablelands, a wall of ectoplasm provides instant fortification where none existed. Shaper-generals have turned the tide of engagements by splitting enemy formations with these translucent barriers, or by encircling their own troops with defensive rings of solidified thought during desperate last stands against gith hordes.

name: Ectoplasmic Shambler
description: You fashion an ephemeral, manylegged mass of pseudo-living ectoplasm called an ectoplasmic shambler. You can direct the shambler as a free action. It has a speed of 10 feet. It can engulf and digest opponents. The shambler is amorphous and can squeeze through holes or openings as small as 2 inches in diameter. The shambler's dimensions are roughly 5 feet by 5 feet, and it has a +5 bonus on grapple checks. Any creature failing to resist the shambler's grapple is drawn inside the shambler's body and takes 1 point of damage each round it remains there.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's duration increases by 2 rounds.
darkSunNotes: In the undercity tunnels of Tyr, where cramped passages negate the advantage of conventional troops, shapers deploy these amorphous horrors to flush out escaped slaves and Veiled Alliance operatives. The shambler oozes through cracks and crevices, an unstoppable mass of semi-living ectoplasm that devours everything in its path.

name: Hail of Crystals
description: A tiny crystal emanates from you and grows rapidly, exploding in a deadly burst of razor-sharp crystal shards in a 20-foot-radius burst. The power deals 9d4 points of slashing damage to every creature and unattended object in the area.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by 1d4 points.
darkSunNotes: Battlefield shapers unleash this power against massed infantry formations — elven raiders, gith war parties, and slave soldier columns alike. The burst of crystalline shrapnel scythes through ranks like an obsidian thresher through grain, leaving the sand glittering with blood-red crystal fragments.

name: Incarnate
description: This power makes certain other powers permanent. Depending on the power to be affected, you must be of a minimum manifester level and must expend a number of XP. You can make the following powers permanent in regard to yourself: aura sight, conceal thoughts, danger sense, detect psionics, detect teleportation, elfsight, know direction and location, my light, or ubiquitous vision.
darkSunNotes: To make a power permanent is to weave it into the very fabric of one's being — a commitment few Athasian psions take lightly. Those who incarnate detect psionics walk through the world with their inner eye perpetually open, seeing the psionic auras that blaze from every sentient creature on this power-saturated world.

name: Major Creation, Psionic
target: Nonmagical, unattended object of nonliving matter, up to 1 cu. ft./level
description: As the major creation spell, except as noted here. You create a nonmagical, unattended object of nonliving matter. The volume of the item created cannot exceed 1 cubic foot per manifester level. The duration of the created item varies with its relative hardness and rarity.
darkSunNotes: Imagine creating a barrel of fresh water in the middle of the desert, or a block of obsidian for emergency weapons. On Athas, where every resource is precious and scarcity kills, the ability to conjure matter from the mind's own substance is power beyond price. Merchant houses would kill — and have — for a shaper with this talent.

name: Crystallize
description: You seed the subject's body with rapidly growing crystal. The subject must make a Fortitude save or be entirely encased in a sheath of crystals. A crystallized creature is considered unconscious and helpless. The crystal has hardness 8 and 20 hit points per inch of thickness. A crystallized creature who is shattered, then subjected to an effect that restores life, is restored whole.
darkSunNotes: A grisly punishment favored by templar inquisitors: the victim's body is transmuted into living crystal, a grotesque statue displayed as a warning in public squares. The crystallized prisoners lining the processional in Urik are said to still be alive within their prisons, dreaming endlessly beneath their mineral shells.

name: Fabricate, Greater Psionic
target: Up to 100 cu. ft./level; see text
description: As psionic fabricate, except you can fabricate up to 100 cubic feet of material per level. You can also work with mineral materials to produce stone, crystal, or metal items. You must make an appropriate Craft check to fabricate articles requiring a high degree of craftsmanship.
darkSunNotes: A power that could reshape the economies of entire city-states. With greater psionic fabricate, a master shaper can construct buildings from raw stone, forge obsidian weapons by the hundreds, or — most precious of all — work the rare iron deposits of the Ringing Mountains into finished steel without ever lighting a forge.

name: Ectoplasmic Cocoon, Mass
description: As ectoplasmic cocoon, except you can cocoon several creatures in a 20-foot-radius burst. All creatures in the area that fail their saves are encased in ectoplasmic cocoons.
darkSunNotes: When the slave revolts erupt — and on Athas, they always do — this is the power that templars call for. A single manifestation can pacify an entire rioting square, cocooning dozens of rebels in suffocating ectoplasm. The sight of an entire mob frozen mid-charge, wrapped in translucent shells, is enough to break any uprising.

name: True Creation
target: Nonmagical, unattended object of nonliving matter, up to 1 cu. ft./level
description: As psionic major creation, except items created are permanent and you do not have to track duration. Unlike psionic major creation, you can create items of mineral nature: stone, crystal, metal, or the like. The item cannot have a value greater than 2,000 gp. The volume of the item created cannot exceed 1 cubic foot per manifester level. You must succeed on an appropriate Craft check to make a complex item.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, the item created can have a value of up to an additional 1,000 gp.
darkSunNotes: To create permanent matter from nothing is to rival the gods themselves — or so the templars would have you believe. A shaper who masters true creation can forge iron from will alone, an act so valuable on metal-starved Athas that such individuals are hunted relentlessly by the merchant houses and sorcerer-kings alike, each seeking to control this irreplaceable resource.

name: Astral Seed
description: This power weaves strands of astral energy into a small crystal that contains the seed of your living mind. You can have only one astral seed at any one time. If the crystal is destroyed, you may create a new one. If you die, your soul transfers into the crystal, which then grows into a new body over 10 days. The new body has all the features and abilities of your original body at the time the seed was created, minus one level. You can have only one astral seed in existence at any one time.
darkSunNotes: The ultimate contingency for Athasian psions — a crystallized echo of the self, hidden in some remote cave or buried beneath the sands. It is whispered that Oronis of Kurn maintains multiple such seeds, each secreted in a different ruin of the Green Age, ensuring that his mission of redemption can never truly be extinguished.

name: Genesis
target: A demiplane coterminous with the Astral Plane, centered on your location
description: You create a finite plane with limited access: a demiplane. Demiplanes created by this power are very small, very minor planes. A character can only manifest this power on the Astral Plane. When manifested, the power creates a local density fluctuation that precipitates the creation of a demiplane. At first, the demiplane grows at a rate of 1 foot in radius per day to an initial maximum radius of 180 feet. You determine the environment of the demiplane when you manifest genesis, such as atmosphere, water, temperature, and the general shape of the terrain.
darkSunNotes: The ultimate expression of metacreative power — to birth an entirely new world. On dying Athas, where the land itself is being consumed by defiler magic, the dream of creating a pristine demiplane free from the ravages of the sorcerer-kings is the deepest aspiration of many master shapers. Some believe the Pristine Tower itself was built through a similar act of creation.

name: Cannibalize
description: You cannibalize your own body for extra power points. You may convert any number of Constitution points directly into power points at a ratio of 1:8 (one Constitution point yields eight power points). The Constitution reduction is debilitating and long-lasting: you immediately lose bonus hit points, your system shock and resurrection survival chances are reduced, and all psionic power scores based on Constitution decrease accordingly. You recover one cannibalized Constitution point per week of complete rest (staying safely indoors with no adventuring). On a critical success, you gain 8 PP without reducing your Constitution. On a critical failure, you lose 1d4 Constitution points but may recover them through rest.
augments: Each additional Constitution point sacrificed yields 8 more power points.
darkSunNotes: Under the burning Athasian sun, many desperate psionicists have cannibalized themselves to survive encounters with the horrors of the wastes, risking permanent debilitation for a few more moments of psionic power.

name: Cognitive Trance
description: The psionicist places himself in a trance that sharpens his mind for calm, contemplative thought. While in the trance, anything seen, heard, or read can be committed to memory verbatim for precise recall later. The player can use this to remember detailed instructions, conversations, or complicated passwords even if they themselves don't recall the exact content. The psionicist can also enter the trance to consider problems, puzzles, or riddles, gaining a +2 bonus on Intelligence checks for solutions. If used for photographic memory, the trance lasts until all information is absorbed; entire books can be memorized. While in the trance, the psionicist suffers -1 to surprise checks, and combat or conversation breaks it. On an exceptional manifestation, Intelligence check bonuses increase to +4.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where written records are rare and libraries rarer still, cognitive trance allows a psionicist to become a living repository of knowledge—memorizing entire trade manifests, legal codes, or secret Alliance communications.

name: Fighting Trance
description: The psionicist focuses his attention into razor-sharp combat awareness. Reflexes and agility reach their absolute peak, and he unconsciously tracks all combatants nearby. While in fighting trance: rear attacks become flank attacks, flank attacks become frontal attacks, and the psionicist gains +1 to all initiative rolls, Armor Class, saving throws, and attack rolls. However, the psionicist's focus on physical action makes initiating psionic powers more difficult—he suffers -1 to all power checks while in fighting trance. On an exceptional manifestation, all bonuses increase to +2 and no rear or flank attacks can be made against the psionicist.
augments: Maintain for 1 PP/round to sustain the trance.
darkSunNotes: Fighting trance is the signature power of Athasian mercenary psionicists and gladiatorial fighters who combine mental discipline with martial prowess—a common and deadly combination in the arenas.

name: Gird
target: One power you are currently maintaining
duration: Special (until dismissed or power points exhausted)
description: You lock a maintained power into automatic operation, freeing your mind from the need to consciously concentrate on it. While girded, you can sleep or fall unconscious without disrupting the affected power. The cost of maintaining the girded power is doubled (or a minimum of 1 PP per hour). You must first initiate and maintain the target power, then successfully manifest gird. To remove the girding, you must consciously decide to do so (no power check required). Otherwise, the gird remains in place until your power points are exhausted — meaning you could awaken to find all your psionic energy consumed. If you wish to reestablish a gird you discontinued, you must make a new power check.
augments: Each girded power costs twice its normal maintenance rate, or a minimum of 1 PP per hour.
darkSunNotes: Desert travelers on Athas frequently gird defensive powers before sleeping in the open wastes, accepting the drain on their reserves in exchange for unbroken protection through the dangerous Athasian night.

name: Intensify
description: You improve one of your mental ability scores — Constitution, Intelligence, or Wisdom — for psionic applications by weakening the other two for all purposes. Each point of increase in the targeted ability decreases the other two by the same amount. Each point of increase costs 5 power points. For example, raising your Intelligence by 4 costs 20 PP and reduces both Constitution and Wisdom by 4. The increase affects psionic powers only; the decrease affects everything applicable except psionic strength points (hit points, saving throws, spell bonuses, etc.). You can raise an ability score to a maximum of 25, provided you have enough power points and no other score is reduced below zero.
augments: For every 5 power points spent, you increase the targeted ability by 1 and decrease the other two by 1. Maintenance costs 1 PP/round.
darkSunNotes: Athasian gladiatorial psionicists use intensify to temporarily become savants of a single mental attribute, accepting crippling weakness in other areas for a decisive edge in arena combat.

name: Prolong
description: While this power is in effect, the range of all your other powers is increased by 50%, as is the radius of their areas of effect. This has no effect on powers with a range of personal or touch, nor does it alter powers that target a single creature or item. It does affect powers that target a quantity of material — for example, prolonged disintegration destroys up to 12 cubic feet of material instead of 8.
augments: Maintenance costs 2 PP/round. On a critical success, the increase is 100% instead of 50%. On a critical failure, all ranges and areas of effect are halved for 1d4 hours.
darkSunNotes: Athasian psionicists who must project their powers across the vast desert distances find prolong indispensable for extending their reach beyond normal limits.

name: Psionic Sense
description: You can detect psionic activity anywhere within 200 yards. Any expenditure of power points constitutes psionic activity, including maintaining a power or using mind blank. On the first successful round, you learn whether or not psionic activity is occurring within range. On the second consecutive successful round, you also learn how many power points are being spent and the direction and distance of all psionic activity within range.
augments: Maintenance costs 1 PP/round. On a critical success, second-round detail is gained in the first round.
darkSunNotes: In a world where nearly every sentient being has some psionic talent, psionic sense is essential for Athasian psionicists navigating the mental landscape of crowded city-states.

name: Receptacle
target: One empowered item or valuable gem
description: You store psionic energy in a specially prepared receptacle for later use. Two types of containers work: a vessel prepared with the empower science, or a valuable gem prepared using this power. An empowered vessel can hold power points equal to your psionic potential multiplied by your level. A gem holds 1 PP per 100 gp of its worth (a 1,000 gp gem holds 10 PP). Gems require preparation by slowly filling them with PP at 1 per turn until full, then making a power check (failure means only half capacity is usable). You can store 1 PP per round into a prepared container. You cannot use stored points when your current total is at maximum — only when running low. Only you can retrieve points from your receptacle, and it must be in contact with your skin. You can never store more total PP across all receptacles than your maximum. If a receptacle is damaged or destroyed, its stored PP are instantly subtracted from your total possible points, and you recover PP at half speed until fully restored.
darkSunNotes: Gems are precious on resource-scarce Athas, making psionic receptacles doubly valuable. Psionicists who carry gem receptacles are both envied and targeted by thieves and raiders.

name: Convergence
target: Any number of willing psionicists within 10 yards
description: You link your mind with other willing psionicists, forming a synergetic entity more powerful than the sum of its parts. There is no limit to the number of psionicists who can join, but each participant must know convergence and make a successful power check in the same round. Once linked, all participants' power points flow into a single shared pool. If one participant knows a power, anyone in the convergence can use it. Each participant can still use only one defense and one other power per round. If the group is attacked psionically, the attack must overcome every active defense; if it does, it affects every character in the convergence. When the convergence ends, remaining power points are divided evenly among all participants (fractions rounded down, no one exceeds their usual maximum).
darkSunNotes: Convergence is the foundation of Athasian psionic warfare. The Order teaches this power early, and convergence circles of trained psionicists can challenge even the might of a sorcerer-king's templars.

name: Enhancement
description: When you learn this power, you select one psionic discipline to enhance. As long as you maintain this power, all of your power scores within the chosen discipline are increased by 2.
