At the same time, all of your power scores in other disciplines are reduced by 1. This provides the same benefit as the Meditative Focus proficiency but in far less time.
augments: For every 8 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the enhancement. On a critical success, other discipline scores are not reduced.
darkSunNotes: Athasian psionicists who specialize deeply in a single discipline often use enhancement to push their focus to its absolute limits before critical encounters.

name: Iron Will
description: The psionicist can focus his willpower to extraordinary levels, persevering through conditions that would fell lesser characters. While maintained, the psionicist can temporarily ignore wounds that would reduce him below 0 hit points, remaining fully active until reaching -10 (death). He does not lose hit points each round while at negative hit points, but will resume losing them when iron will ends. The power also grants +4 to saving throws against effects that cause unconsciousness or control (vampire charm, psionic domination). For effects that normally allow no save (command, sleep), the psionicist gains a save vs. spells at -4. Iron will acts as a reflexive power—it can be initiated as a reaction when needed, though doing so may replace the psionicist's planned action for the round.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/round to sustain the iron will.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where death can come from a poisoned blade or a templar's decree, iron will has kept many a psionicist fighting long past the point where any normal person would have collapsed. The power is named for the rarest metal on the world.

name: Magnify
target: One power you are about to use
duration: Concentration (until the affected power is used)
description: You magnify the effects of another power in all conceivable ways — double damage, double range, double modifiers, and so on. The affected power's initial cost and maintenance cost are proportionately magnified as well. You initiate magnify first and designate which power you intend to improve. You must then either use the affected power immediately (in the same round) or maintain magnify until you do (a costly endeavor). Once the power has been used, magnify must be reinitiated to be used again. The magnification factor depends on your level: levels 6-10 grant x2, levels 11-15 grant x3, and levels 16-20 grant x4.
augments: Magnification factor scales with level: x2 at 6th, x3 at 11th, x4 at 16th. Initial cost equals 25 times the magnification factor.
darkSunNotes: The sorcerer-kings of Athas are rumored to employ magnified psionic powers on a terrifying scale, multiplying their already formidable mental abilities to crush opposition across entire battlefields.

name: Martial Trance
description: You enter a shallow trance that focuses your complete attention on psionic combat, tuning out all other distractions. While in the trance, you gain a +1 bonus on all Telepathy power scores. The trance ends whenever you choose, and you are brought out of it by any physical blow, shake, or slap. Because your attention is completely occupied, any melee attack against you hits automatically and causes maximum damage.
darkSunNotes: In the mental arenas of Athas, martial trance is the mark of a dedicated psychic combatant — one who has chosen to risk their body entirely in pursuit of mental dominance.

name: Probability Manipulation
description: The psionicist tampers with the laws of chance to affect a specific action, gaining a bonus of +4 (d20) or +20% (d100). This can be applied to saving throws, attack rolls, ability checks, thief skills, or spell failure checks—but cannot affect psionic power initiation. If no roll is made within one turn of initiating, the chance is lost. Luck is fickle: if probability manipulation is used more than once per day, there is a 50% chance the psionicist suffers a penalty equal to the intended bonus instead. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist favorably manipulates chance for the next three die rolls.
darkSunNotes: In the gambling dens and trading halls of the Athasian city-states, probability manipulation gives a psionicist an edge that merchants would kill for—though the backlash of overuse keeps even the greediest from pushing their luck.

name: Psionic Inflation
target: All psionic activity within 100 ft. radius
description: You send out a powerful ring of psionic noise extending to 100 feet. All psionic activity within this area requires twice the normal initial cost and maintenance cost. You are not affected — the noise begins about an inch beyond your aura, creating a bubble of quiet around you. Other psionicists in the area will not realize anything is wrong until they actually use a power or pay an inflated maintenance cost. If two psionicists initiate psionic inflation in the same area, they must conduct a psychic contest; the loser's inflation ceases. If both fail, both powers end.
augments: On a critical success, all psionic activity within the area requires three times the normal cost instead of two. Maintenance costs 3 PP/round.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, psionic inflation is a feared battlefield tactic, used to drain enemy psionicists of their reserves at an alarming rate while the initiator operates freely within their protective bubble.

name: Psionic Vampirism
description: The psionicist steals psionic strength from another creature to replenish his own. Unlike psychic drain, PSPs are directly drained, the victim need not be asleep, and the target need not be humanoid—only psionic. Contact must be established first. The drain rate depends on the manifestation: 3 to 15 PSPs per round. The process is painful but not permanently debilitating. Since targets are psionic by definition, they may fight back with defenses. Drained PSPs return to the victim normally. If drained to 0 PSPs, the victim must save vs. death or fall unconscious for 2d6 turns. Extensive use of psionic vampirism shifts alignment toward evil.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/round. Drain rate scales with manifestation quality (3-15 PP/round).
darkSunNotes: Psionic vampirism is one of the most feared and reviled powers on Athas. The Order hunts rogue psionicists who drain others of their mental strength, but some desperate or power-hungry mindbenders risk everything for the stolen energy.

name: Psychic Blade
description: The psionicist creates a semi-tangible manifestation of psychic energy shaped like a sword that can be wielded in physical combat. Any living creature struck suffers 1d6 damage plus 1 additional point per extra 2 PSPs spent. The victim must save vs. death or be stunned for 1d6 rounds. Damage is treated as punching damage: 25% real, 75% temporary (recovered within one turn). The blade is treated as a +2 weapon for striking creatures immune to normal weapons. Because the blade is the psionicist's own life energy, creatures that drain energy levels automatically drain a level each time they are struck. Creatures without minds (oozes, slimes, elementals, golems) cannot be harmed. If the victim has an active psionic defense, the psionicist must win a psychic contest to cause harm.
augments: Maintain for 4+ PP/round. Extra damage costs 2 PP per point.
darkSunNotes: The psychic blade is the signature weapon of Athasian psionic combatants who blend mental and physical warfare. In a world where weapons break and metal is scarce, a blade that can never be disarmed or confiscated is invaluable.

name: Psychic Drain
description: You tap into the psychic energy of other sleeping creatures to augment your own psionic strength. Up to six hosts can be tapped at once. Each subject must be asleep, and you must establish mental contact before draining. A successfully drained host falls into a trance lasting 4-9 hours. For every ability point drained from a host's Wisdom, Intelligence, or Constitution, you gain 10 power points. These points cannot be banked — they are drawn automatically as you expend your own points, unless you specify otherwise. You must remain within 30 yards of entranced hosts to draw from them. Moderate draining causes no harm, but draining more than 50% of a host's psionic potential causes increasingly severe effects: temporary amnesia (51-60%), permanent amnesia (61-70%), Intelligence reduced to 4 (71-80%), coma and daily system shock saves or death (81-90%), or save vs. death or die outright (91-100%). Psychic surgery can repair this damage.
augments: Each additional host requires 10 PP plus contact. Power points are drawn as needed rather than stored.
darkSunNotes: The templars of Athas whisper of rogue psionicists who drain entire slave populations in the night, growing bloated with stolen psychic energy. The practice is condemned by the Order but secretly employed by those desperate for power.

name: Retrospection
description: You perform a psionic seance, delving into the past to locate memories that have been loosed from other minds. You must join at least two other psionicists in a convergence before using this power. One of the participants then poses a question regarding a specific event in the past. On a successful power check, you tap into a universal, pervasive memory — accessing any information that ever existed in anyone's memory regarding the event. The detail depends on the quality of your check: a marginal success yields extremely vague and fragmentary memories, while an exceptional success provides reasonably complete and specific recollections. Memories bear the personalities of their creators: they can be polite and helpful, or cantankerous and hostile. Ancient, undisturbed memories may be angered by intrusion or delighted to air themselves out.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where written records are scarce and history has been deliberately obscured by the sorcerer-kings, retrospection is one of the only reliable ways to uncover the truth of the Green Age and the Cleansing Wars.

name: Splice
description: You splice two or more separate psionic powers into a single coordinated release. First, you make a splice check with a power score penalty of -2 per power being spliced, paying 5 PP per spliced power. Then you initiate each spliced power in succession without interruption, paying their individual costs (none takes effect yet). After all powers are initiated, you make a second splice check. If successful, you can maintain the splice at 1 PP per spliced power per round, and release all spliced powers simultaneously (or with split-second timing) at any later time. On release, you must still make individual power checks for each spliced power, but you do not pay their initial costs again. The maximum number of powers you can splice equals your manifester level. The most common use is splicing contact with another power that requires it.
augments: Each additional power spliced increases the cost by 5 PP and the power score penalty by -2. Maintenance is 1 PP per spliced power per round.
darkSunNotes: Athasian psionic combat masters use splice to devastating effect, preparing layered psychic assaults that strike with multiple powers in a single, overwhelming instant.

name: Stasis Field
target: Sphere with radius up to 1 yard per level, centered on you
description: You create a shimmering silver globe in which time slows to a crawl and energy is reduced to a fizzle. Inside the field, time passes 60 times slower — for every round inside, an hour elapses outside. Light turns murky and gray; internal light sources function at one-quarter brightness. Energy spells (fireball, magic missile, cone of cold, flamestrike) fizzle harmlessly. Movement rates are halved, and missile weapons are useless (projectiles drift lazily through the air). The field can have any radius up to 1 yard per your manifester level. While inside, maintenance costs 20 PP per round. You can transfer the focal point to anything you touch, then leave the field — reducing maintenance to 1 PP per round (from the outside perspective). You can roam up to 100 yards away while maintaining it from outside, but cannot move the field without re-entering it.
augments: The field radius scales at 1 yard per manifester level. Maintenance is 20 PP/round inside or 1 PP/round outside.
darkSunNotes: Stasis fields are used by Athasian psionicists to create sanctuaries in the desert — pockets of slowed time where a few rounds of rest inside translate to hours of passage for pursuers outside.

name: Wrench
target: One creature existing on multiple planes
description: You force a creature that exists simultaneously on the Prime Material and another plane entirely into one plane or the other. This affects most undead and similar dual-planar entities, but specifically excludes gods, demigods, and avatars. If forced off the Prime Material, the creature is trapped in its other plane for 2d6 turns before it can return. If wrenched entirely onto the Prime Material, it is trapped only as long as you maintain the power. A creature wrenched onto the Prime Material may suffer several penalties at the DM's discretion: armor class penalized by 1d6 points, undead lose the ability to drain life energy, magical weapon requirements to hit are reduced by 1, and the creature is killed permanently if reduced to 0 hit points.
augments: Maintenance costs 8 PP/round to keep a creature wrenched onto the Prime Material.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the Gray separates the living from the dead, wrench is one of the few tools capable of forcing undead wholly into the material world — making them vulnerable to permanent destruction.

name: Appraise
description: You focus your psionic awareness on a specific course of action and mentally process enormous calculations to arrive at an overall probability of success. If you successfully manifest this power, the DM reveals the percentage chance for the plan or action's success. The accuracy of the appraisal depends on how closely you and your allies adhere to the plan as conceived. Factors unknown to you at the time of appraisal can change the outcome dramatically. Like precognition, this power's usefulness hinges on the quality of information available when it is used.
darkSunNotes: Templars and noble houses of Athas prize psionicists who can appraise battle plans against the forces of rival city-states.

name: Aura Alteration
description: You can temporarily disguise a creature's alignment aura, disguise its level, or remove aura afflictions such as curses, geases, and quests. Disguising alignment or level lasts 1d6 hours and has no effect on the creature's actual alignment or class, but creatures using aura sight or similar detection will be fooled. Removing curses, geases, and quests is more difficult, requiring an additional 10 PSP expenditure (20 total) and imposing a -6 penalty to your power check. On a critical success, the patient must make a saving throw vs. spells to avoid losing one experience level as a side effect of the psychic procedure.
augments: You may spend 20 power points instead of 10 to attempt removal of curses, geases, or quests, at a -6 penalty to your manifesting check.
darkSunNotes: Under the oppressive sorcerer-kings of Athas, aura alteration is used by fugitive preservers and rogue psionicists to hide their true nature from templar inquisitors.

name: Suppress Magic
description: The psionicist creates a field that dampens magic of all types within a 20-yard radius. Within the field, no spells function, weapons lose their enchantments, creatures with magical attacks or powers cannot use them, magically animated monsters stop, and magical items cease functioning. Base maintenance is 1 PP per round, plus 1 PP per spell level suppressed each round. Effects without a spell level cost 1 PP per HD or level of the initiating creature. The field temporarily disrupts active enchantments: charmed persons are freed, polymorphed creatures revert, etc. Things permanently changed by magic (stone shaped into a bench) are unaffected. Once the power ends, all enchantments resume. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist may choose to permanently dispel any enchantment in the area.
augments: Maintain for 1+ PP/round. Each spell level suppressed adds 1 PP to maintenance cost.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the sorcerer-kings' power derives from both psionics and magic, suppress magic is one of the few powers that can level the playing field against a defiler's devastating spells—though it also neutralizes any allied magical support.

name: Empower
description: You imbue an item with rudimentary intelligence and psionic ability through an arduous, multi-day process. The item must be of exceptional quality (worth 250%-500% of normal cost) and newly manufactured (process must begin within 48 hours of creation). The empowerment proceeds in steps: First, prepare the item each day (50 PP). Second, grant the item access to one discipline you know (100 PP). Third, empower it with individual powers you know within that discipline (100 PP per power), each requiring two successful checks. Finally, seal the powers within the item (50 PP). An empowered object has 8 PP per devotion and 12 per science. It gains intelligence equal to your Intelligence minus 1d6 (minimum 12) and develops its own personality. An unsealed item loses one power per month.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, empowered items are among the rarest treasures. The few psionicists capable of creating them are jealously guarded by the sorcerer-kings, or hunted by those who covet their craft.

name: Subjective Reality
description: With the power of absolute belief, the psionicist can ignore the reality of objects, making them have no existence for him. He can only disbelieve inanimate objects or effects, not living creatures. Possible uses include: disbelieving a type of weapon (hand-held, bows, hurled—no weapon of that type can harm him); disbelieving an entire type of magic (priestly or wizardly); disbelieving a manifestation of an element (bonfire, rockslide); or disbelieving any single object regardless of size (a boulder, enemy armor, a magical item). Disbelieved objects simply do not exist for the psionicist—he cannot be harmed by them, nor can he affect them. The psionicist can alter what he is disbelieving from round to round on an exceptional manifestation.
augments: Maintain for 7 PP/round. Exceptional manifestation allows changing the disbelieved category each round.
darkSunNotes: Subjective reality represents the pinnacle of Athasian psionic philosophy—the ultimate expression of Will over the material world. A psionicist who can disbelieve a defiler's magic or a templar's weapons is nearly unstoppable.

name: Ballistic Attack
description: A special variation of telekinesis, ballistic attack allows the psionicist to hurl a small object at a target at deadly speeds. The object must weigh no more than 1 pound, must be within sight, and cannot be anchored or attached to anything. A rock is the most common projectile. The psionicist uses his regular THAC0 to determine whether the missile hits. On a successful hit, the missile inflicts 1d6 points of damage.
darkSunNotes: In the siege of Tyr's iron mines, rebel psionicists hurled shards of obsidian and bone at templar guards with nothing but the force of their minds. On a world where metal is scarce, every stone becomes a weapon in the hands of a telekinetic.

name: Control Object
target: One unattended object weighing up to 100 lb.
description: You telekinetically "bring to life" an inanimate object. Though it is not actually alive, the object moves under your control. Live vegetation cannot be controlled in this fashion, nor can already animated objects or undead. The controlled object moves like a puppet, with jerky and clumsy movements, at a speed of up to 20 feet. If its form is rigid, it makes creaking, groaning, or grating sounds as you control it. A controlled object can attack an opponent if you direct it to do so. It has one slam attack, at a base attack bonus equal to your base attack bonus plus your Intelligence modifier. If the attack hits, it deals points of damage equal to 1d6 plus your Intelligence modifier. A controlled object has its usual hardness and hit points. You can use this power on a nonmagical lock, making it move in such a way as to attempt to unlock itself. If another character makes an Open Lock check involving a lock that you are concentrating on controlling, the character gains a +4 bonus on the check.
darkSunNotes: In a world where metal is scarce and every tool is precious, the ability to animate objects with thought alone is invaluable. Kineticists in elven market camps use this power to send obsidian-bladed weapons dancing through the air as distractions while they flee. In Nibenay's winding streets, thieves animate door bolts from outside, slipping locks without a sound.

name: Control Sound
description: This power allows the psionicist to shape and alter existing sounds. He could change a woman's speech into a lion's roar or different words, disguise the sound of marching soldiers as falling rain, or layer sounds so a single singer sounds like an entire choir. The power can also dampen a specific sound—quieting a hammer, muffling someone's words, or eliminating a door's creaking—but only one sound can be affected at a time.
augments: Maintenance cost of 2 power points per round to sustain the alteration.
darkSunNotes: The Veiled Alliance uses this power to muffle the tell-tale sounds of spellcasting in crowded marketplaces, while templar interrogators employ it to ensure that a prisoner's screams never reach sympathetic ears beyond the dungeon walls.

name: Deflect
description: The psionicist can deflect a missile in flight or a weapon in mid-swing, causing it to miss its target. The psionicist may use this on a weapon targeted at himself or someone else, but must have better initiative than the attacker and hold his action until the attack is made. On a successful manifestation, the weapon misses automatically. Deflect works on any thrown, fired, or hand-held weapon, including giant-thrown boulders or ballista bolts, but has no effect on creatures attacking with natural weaponry such as teeth or claws. On an exceptional manifestation, the defense is involuntary, and the psionicist may make a separate attack in the same round.
darkSunNotes: In the gladiatorial arenas of Athas, a psychokineticist who can deflect an incoming obsidian blade or a hurled boulder is a crowd favorite—and a nightmare for opponents relying on ranged weapons.

name: Energy Ray
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You create a ray of energy of the chosen type that shoots forth from your fingertip and strikes a target within range, dealing 1d6 points of damage, if you succeed on a ranged touch attack with the ray. Cold: +1 point of damage per die. Electricity: +3 bonus on attack roll if target wears metal armor and +2 bonus on manifester level checks to overcome power resistance. Fire: +1 point of damage per die. Sonic: -1 point of damage per die and ignores an object's hardness. This power's subtype is the same as the type of energy you manifest.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6).
darkSunNotes: The most basic attack in any Athasian psion's arsenal, energy ray is often the first offensive power a young student manifests. On a world where metal armor is exceedingly rare, the electricity variant's bonus against metal is nearly useless — but when it does connect with a templar in their precious iron breastplate, the results are spectacular. Fire rays are by far the most common, drawn instinctively from the searing heat of the dark sun overhead.

name: Far Hand
target: A nonmagical, unattended object weighing up to 5 lb.
description: You can mentally lift and move an object at will from a distance. As a move action, you can propel the object as far as 15 feet in any direction, though the power ends if the distance between you and the object exceeds the power's range.
augments: You can augment this power in one or both of the following ways. 1. For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's range increases by 5 feet. 2. For every additional power point you spend, the weight limit of the target increases by 2 pounds.
darkSunNotes: The simplest expression of telekinetic talent, far hand is so common among Athasians that many don't even recognize it as a psionic power. Children in the city-states learn to pull fruit from high branches with their minds before they learn to climb. In the markets of Tyr, far hand is the pickpocket's best friend — a waterskin lifted from a belt, a ceramic coin palmed from a merchant's table, all without a hand being raised.

name: Force Screen
description: You create an invisible mobile disk of force that hovers in front of you. The force screen provides a +4 shield bonus to Armor Class (which applies against incorporeal touch attacks, since the force screen is a force effect). Since it hovers in front of you, the effect has no armor check penalty associated with it.
augments: For every 4 additional power points you spend, the shield bonus to Armor Class improves by 1.
darkSunNotes: On a world where shields are made from chitin and bone, a disk of pure telekinetic force is superior in every way — weightless, invisible, and impervious to the corrosive silt storms that destroy conventional equipment. Psychic warriors serving as bodyguards to nobles and merchants maintain force screens as a matter of course, the faint hum of displaced air the only sign of their invisible protection.

name: Hammer
description: This power charges your touch with the force of a sledgehammer. A successful melee touch attack deals 1d8 points of bludgeoning damage. This damage is not increased or decreased by your Strength modifier.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's duration increases by 1 round.
darkSunNotes: In the gladiatorial pits, where every advantage matters, hammer turns an unarmed slave into a lethal combatant. A swift-action manifestation followed by a devastating open-palm strike has ended many an arena bout. Mul gladiators are particularly fond of this power, combining their natural toughness with psionic striking force to shatter bone and chitin armor alike with a single blow.

name: Inertial Armor
description: Your mind generates a tangible field of force that provides a +4 armor bonus to Armor Class. Unlike mundane armor, inertial armor entails no armor check penalty or speed reduction. Because inertial armor is composed of psychokinetic force, incorporeal creatures can't bypass it the way they do normal armor. Your inertial armor can be invisible or can appear as a colored glow, at your option. The armor bonus provided by inertial armor does not stack with the armor bonus provided by regular armor.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, the armor bonus to Armor Class increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Perhaps the most universally valued psionic defense on Athas, inertial armor is the reason so many psions can walk the wastes wearing nothing but linen and still survive attacks that would fell an armored templar. On a world where metal armor is the exclusive province of sorcerer-kings and their most favored servants, a sheath of invisible psychokinetic force is the great equalizer. Many manifesters keep the power visible as a faint amber glow — a warning to would-be attackers.

name: Magnetize
description: The psionicist can change the magnetic orientation of a metal object, strongly magnetizing it. The initial cost is 2 PSPs per pound of metal affected. The item remains magnetized for a number of rounds equal to the psionicist's level. The magnetized item can be made attracted to or repelled by other metal objects within 20 feet. Heavier objects remain stationary while lighter objects move toward or away. A creature holding or wearing a magnetized item may attempt a Strength check to retain control. A character in metal armor can be immobilized on a failed Strength check. Small objects such as daggers can reach impressive speeds when attracted or repelled. On an exceptional manifestation, all Strength checks by opponents to keep control of metal items fail automatically.
augments: Cost scales at 2 PP per pound of metal. Duration is 1 round per level.
darkSunNotes: Metal is scarce and precious on Athas, making magnetize a uniquely devastating power—wrenching a steel sword from an enemy's grasp can be as effective as any lethal attack.

name: Matter Agitation
target: An area of 2 ft. by 2 ft. of loose matter
description: You can excite the molecules of a small area of loose matter (ite, sand, powder, and similar materials), causing them to agitate and become warm. After the power has been in effect for 2 consecutive rounds, the surface of the agitated area begins to heat, dealing 1d6 points of fire damage to any creature or object touching the area. After the power has been in effect for 4 consecutive rounds, the area is hot enough to boil water, dealing 2d6 points of fire damage to any creature or object touching the area. After 6 consecutive rounds, the area glows red-hot, dealing 3d6 points of fire damage per round.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where silt and sand are everywhere and fire is the element of destruction, matter agitation is both tool and weapon. Desert travelers use it to heat stones for cooking without the telltale smoke of a campfire that might attract raiders. In more sinister applications, assassins agitate the sand beneath a sleeping target's bedroll, slowly cooking them alive. The power is particularly effective in the Sea of Silt, where the fine particulate heats with terrible efficiency.

name: Molecular Bonding
description: The psionicist joins two surfaces together at the molecular level. An enemy's boots can be bonded to the floor, a sword to its sheath, or the joints of armor fused into a useless plug of metal. The power can also repair damaged items by joining broken pieces together. The rate of bonding is approximately 6 square inches per round. When used on armor, this is sufficient to freeze one joint or fix one boot to the ground per round. A character immobilized this way can usually wriggle free in one round, but a character wearing fully bonded plate mail is stuck for a considerable time. The power does not affect living creatures. On an exceptional manifestation, the bonding rate increases to 24 square inches per round.
augments: Maintain for 4 PP/round to continue bonding surfaces.
darkSunNotes: On metal-poor Athas, molecular bonding is one of the few ways to repair precious metal weapons and tools. In combat, bonding an enemy's bone armor joints can render even a seasoned gladiator helpless.

name: Momentum Theft
description: The psionicist drains a target of its momentum, causing it to stop moving. The stolen momentum is harmlessly dissipated in the ground. Arrows, catapult missiles, and flying creatures can be halted in mid-air (winged creatures usually break their fall after dropping 10-20 feet). Walking or fighting creatures are thrown off-balance and must make a Dexterity check or lose their action for the round. Running or charging creatures must make a Dexterity check at -4 or take a tumble for 1d4 damage and be stunned for 1-2 rounds. If used on a falling character, it stops them instantly—they begin falling again, but damage counts only from the new fall distance. The cost scales with the target's size and speed: 5 PSPs for a man-sized creature at walking speed, +5 PSPs for each doubling of speed or weight.
augments: Cost scales with target mass and speed. Each doubling of weight or speed costs an additional 5 PP.
darkSunNotes: Momentum theft can halt a charging half-giant or stop an obsidian-tipped bolt in mid-flight—invaluable in the brutal skirmishes that erupt daily across the Athasian wastes.

name: My Light
description: Your eyes beam a cone of light that illuminates a 40-foot cone. The light produced is equivalent to a torchlight. You can shut off the light as a swift action or reactivate it as a free action. You are dazzled while the light is active.
augments: If you spend 2 additional power points, you can manifest this power as an immediate action.
darkSunNotes: Beneath Athas — in the caverns of the underdark, the buried ruins of forgotten cities, and the labyrinthine tunnels dug by ankhegs and other burrowing horrors — my light is the difference between life and death. The power's distinctive eye-beam is unmistakable, and experienced explorers of the under-regions know that the sudden appearance of such light means a psion is present. Some thri-kreen find this power deeply unsettling, as their compound eyes interpret the beam as a predatory display.

name: Soften
savingThrow: Special (magical items save vs. crushing blow)
description: This power weakens an entire object, causing it to lose rigidity and strength. Effects vary by material: Metal—for each round of softening, weapons incur a cumulative -1 penalty to attack rolls and deal one less point of damage; metal armor's AC worsens by one per round; after 10 rounds any metal becomes soft and rubbery. Wood—weapons suffer a cumulative -1 attack and damage penalty per round; after six rounds wood becomes stringy and rubbery; after 10 rounds it can be easily split or punched through. Stone—noticeably soft after two rounds; after five rounds it can be worked like stiff clay (maximum softness). Magical items may save vs. crushing blow to escape the effect entirely. This power has no effect on living tissue.
augments: Maintenance cost of 3 power points per round. Effects increase cumulatively each round as described.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where obsidian and bone weapons are common, soften can quickly render an opponent's gear useless.

name: Static Discharge
description: The psionicist uses control of magnetism to accumulate a charge of static electricity and discharge it as violent electrical shocks. The longer the charge accumulates, the more damaging the discharge: 1d4+1 points of damage per round of accumulation. Maintaining the power doesn't count as an attack, but the discharge does. The psionicist must make an attack roll to hit the target, but only magical protections apply—armor and Dexterity do not. On an exceptional manifestation, damage increases to 1d6+1 per round and any metal items on the victim are magnetized.
augments: Maintain for 5 PP/round to accumulate charge. Damage is 1d4+1 per round accumulated.
darkSunNotes: In the dry, arid climate of Athas, static electricity builds naturally—a psionicist channeling this force can unleash devastating shocks, making metal-armored foes (rare as they are) particularly vulnerable.

name: Stomp
description: Your foot stomp subjects everyone within the affected area to a miniature earthquake. Creatures standing in the area must make Reflex saves or fall prone. Objects may also be toppled and fragile objects might be broken.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: The psychic warrior's stomp shakes the very earth of Athas, and in a world where the ground itself is often little more than compacted silt or crumbling sandstone, the effects can be devastating. Half-giant psychic warriors are particularly feared for their stomps, which can crack stone foundations and collapse tunnel ceilings in the underground warrens beneath the city-states. In arena combat, a well-timed stomp can send an entire group of opponents sprawling into the dust.

name: Animate Object
description: Inanimate objects can be brought to life with this devotion. The objects are not truly alive but move under the psionicist's control as if they were—chairs may walk, trees may dance, stones may waddle. The animated object must weigh 100 pounds or less. The material affects difficulty: cloth and paper are easiest, while stone is hardest. Once animated, all materials become flexible to some extent. Motion is not fluid; the item moves like a puppet with jerky, clumsy movements, producing loud creaking, groaning, or grating sounds if rigid. The object can move 60 feet per round and attack as a club with a THAC0 of 20.
augments: Maintenance cost of 3 power points per round to sustain animation.
darkSunNotes: In the workshops of Nibenay, psionicist artisans animate their own tools—ceramic chisels that carve of their own accord, looms that weave without hands. More grimly, templar executioners have been known to animate the very manacles binding a condemned prisoner, forcing them to tighten until bones crack.

name: Animate Shadow
target: One shadow, up to 100 sq. ft.
description: The psionicist can animate the shadow cast by any person or thing, making it appear to have a life of its own. The shadow can move away from its source but must remain flat against a surface—it can never be more than two-dimensional. An animated shadow cannot attack or disrupt concentration. It is similar to a cantrip's effect, useful mainly as a diversion to entertain someone or attract a guard's attention.
augments: Maintenance cost of 3 power points per round to sustain animation.
darkSunNotes: Under the dark sun's merciless glare, shadows are sharp-edged and deep. Athasian psionicists who master this power can set those shadows creeping across arena sands to trip opponents, or send them slithering beneath doors to scout templar strongholds—a darkness that moves with predatory intent.

name: Concussion Blast
description: A subject you select is pummeled with telekinetic force for 1d6 points of force damage. You can choose to have the power deal an equal amount of nonlethal damage instead. Concussion blast always affects a subject within range that you can see, even if the subject is in melee or has cover or concealment (you cannot use this power against creatures with total cover or total concealment). Nonmagical, unattended objects (including doors, walls, locks, and so on) may also be damaged by this power.
augments: You can augment this power in one or both of the following ways. 1. For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's damage increases by 1d6 points. 2. For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power can affect an additional target. Any additional target cannot be more than 15 feet from another target of the power.
darkSunNotes: In the gladiatorial pits of Tyr and Balic, psions favor concussion blast for its ability to strike past shields and defensive formations. The invisible hammer of force leaves no mark on the sand — only the sudden, brutal collapse of a foe who thought himself protected. Templars have been known to use this power to punish disobedient slaves without leaving evidence of the beating.

name: Control Air
description: You have some control over wind speed and direction. The speed of the wind within the area of this power can be increased or decreased by up to 10 miles per hour. You can spend as many as 5 additional power points to augment this power, with each point allowing you to modify the wind speed by an additional 10 miles per hour, to a maximum change in wind speed of 60 miles per hour. This power also gives you the ability to alter the direction of the wind by as much as 90 degrees. Powerful enough winds can cause creatures to be blown away, knocked down, or checked.
augments: See above. Each additional power point spent increases wind speed modification by 10 mph, up to 60 mph maximum change.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the wind carries scouring silt and choking dust across the barren wastes, mastery of air is mastery of survival itself. Kineticists who travel the Sea of Silt use this power to part the deadly clouds of particulate that would suffocate lesser travelers. In the city-states, cunning manifesters redirect desert winds to fill an enemy's lungs with grit during sandstorms.

name: Control Body
description: This devotion allows psychokinetic control of another person's body, turning the victim into a marionette. The victim is aware that someone else controls him. Before the power takes effect, the psionicist must win a psychic contest, pitting his power score against the victim's Strength. If the victim wins, he breaks free. A tie continues the contest into the next round. If successful, the psionicist gains rudimentary control over the victim's limbs—standing, sitting, walking, turning. The body can be forced to attack physically but with a -6 penalty on attack rolls. The victim retains control of his voice and speech. The victim must remain within 80 yards or control is broken automatically. If forced toward obviously suicidal actions, the victim can fight another psychic contest to regain control.
augments: Maintenance cost of 8 power points per round to sustain control.
darkSunNotes: The sorcerer-kings' most feared templars are said to employ psionicists who can seize control of a rebel's body mid-stride, forcing them to kneel before the throne they sought to overthrow. In the gladiatorial arenas, this power turns a champion's own sword arm against him.

name: Control Flames
target: One fire source, up to 10 sq. ft.
description: By controlling flames, the psionicist can make a normal fire bigger, smaller, hotter, or colder, and even make it move as if alive. A fire's size can be increased by 100% or decreased by 50%. Increased heat causes double damage; reduced heat halves damage. This affects normal fires (torches, burning oil) but not magical fires such as fireballs. An animated fire can move up to 90 feet per round, shaped like a person or animal covering no more than 10 square feet. If the fire moves away from its fuel, it survives only one more round before dying out. An animated fire can attack by engulfing an opponent using the psionicist's THAC0, dealing 1d6 points of damage on a hit.
augments: Maintenance cost of 3 power points per round to sustain control.
darkSunNotes: Under the blazing Athasian sun, campfires are less common, but controlling flames in a besieged caravan or burning ruin remains invaluable.

name: Control Light
description: The psionicist can manipulate existing light—he cannot create light from darkness, but can create darkness from light. Possible effects include: deepening existing shadows to inky black (granting a thief a 20% hiding bonus), lightening shadows (reducing a thief's hiding by 20%), brightening a light source until it hurts to look at (imposing a -2 penalty on attack rolls for those exposed), dimming a light source to twilight, extending shadows up to triple their original size into well-lit areas, or extending light to reduce shadows by up to 50%.
augments: Maintenance cost of 4 power points per round to sustain the effect.
darkSunNotes: Under Athas's crimson sun, light is both life and weapon. Psionicists in the employ of merchant houses dim the light around their caravans to avoid detection by raiders, while arena showmen bend the harsh sunlight into dazzling displays that blind opponents and thrill the crowds.

name: Control Wind
description: The psionicist gains limited control over wind speed and direction. Existing wind speed can be increased or decreased by 10 mph or 25%, whichever is greater. Wind direction can be shifted by up to 90 degrees. Changes occur within moments and dissipate within a minute when maintenance stops. Winds above 19 mph prevent small creatures from flying and impose a -4 penalty on missile fire. Winds over 32 mph cause minor structural damage and kick up dust clouds. Winds over 55 mph prevent all flight, knock down trees and wooden buildings, and threaten to swamp ships. Winds over 73 mph are hurricane-force gales.
augments: Maintenance cost of 10 power points per round to sustain wind changes.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where sandstorms can strip flesh from bone, manipulating wind is both a powerful weapon and a vital survival tool.

name: Create Sound
description: Unlike control sound, this power allows the psionicist to create sound from silence. The psionicist chooses the source or location of the sound—he can make rocks sing, weapons cast insults, or trees sound as if a battle rages within. Volume ranges from a whisper to several people shouting in unison. Once created, the sound can be controlled without additional cost beyond normal maintenance. Created sounds cannot produce magical effects; a duplicated banshee's wail would sound authentic but cause no harm.
augments: Maintenance cost of 3 power points per round to sustain the sound.
darkSunNotes: Athasian spies and smugglers prize this power for creating diversions—the sound of a templar patrol approaching from the wrong direction, or a mul's battle cry echoing through empty streets. In the eerie silence of the dead city of Bodach, any sound at all can drive lesser minds to panic.

name: Cryokinesis
description: The opposite of molecular agitation. The psionicist suppresses molecular motion to lower an object's temperature dramatically. Effects escalate with each round maintained: Round 1—embers extinguished, metal becomes cold, skin becomes clammy, water condenses. Round 2—small fires extinguished, bone and chitin become brittle, skin becomes frostbitten (1d3 damage), liquids freeze. Round 3—large fires reduced to embers, metal and wood become brittle, flesh freezes (1d8 damage per round thereafter). Round 4—stone and obsidian become brittle. Items frozen and then thawed may become more susceptible to breakage; armor and weapons that become brittle must make saving throws each time they deliver or receive blows.
augments: Maintain for 7 PP/round. Damage escalates each round as described.
darkSunNotes: On a world baked by the crimson sun, cryokinesis is a shocking and terrifying power. Freezing a thri-kreen's chitin renders it doubly vulnerable to physical blows—a tactic feared by mantis warriors across the Tablelands.

name: Energy Missile
target: Up to five creatures or objects; no two targets can be more than 15 ft. apart
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You release a powerful missile of energy of the chosen type at your foe. The missile deals 3d6 points of damage to each creature or object you target, to the maximum of five targets. You cannot hit the same target multiple times with the same manifestation of this power. Cold: +1 per die, Fortitude save. Electricity: +2 save DC and +2 to overcome PR. Fire: +1 per die. Sonic: -1 per die, ignores hardness. This power's subtype is the same as the type of energy you manifest.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6) and its save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: In the cramped, narrow streets of Raam — where gang wars and templar purges are a daily occurrence — energy missiles that can strike multiple foes simultaneously are invaluable. Kineticists serving the city's fractious noble houses learn to split their focus across several targets, raining fire or crackling lightning upon clustered enemies. The screams of the struck carry far in the dusty twilight.

name: Energy Push
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You project a solid blast of energy of the chosen type at a target, dealing it 2d6 points of damage. In addition, if a subject of up to one size category larger than you fails a Strength check (DC equal to the save DC of this power), the driving force of the energy blast pushes it back 5 feet plus another 5 feet for every 5 points of damage it takes. If a wall or other solid object prevents the subject from being pushed back, the subject instead slams into the object and takes an extra 2d6 points of damage from the impact (no save). The movement caused by energy push does not provoke attacks of opportunity. Cold: +1 per die (impact damage remains 2d6), Fortitude save. Electricity: +2 save DC and +2 to overcome PR. Fire: +1 per die (impact remains 2d6). Sonic: -1 per die (impact remains 2d6), ignores hardness.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6) and its save DC increases by 1. The damage increase applies to both the initial blast and any damage from impact with an object.
darkSunNotes: In the gladiatorial arenas, pushing an opponent into the spiked walls or off elevated platforms is often more crowd-pleasing than a simple kill. Psions who master energy push become favorites of the crowds, launching opponents through the air to crash against obsidian-studded barriers. On the open wastes, this power can send an attacker tumbling over cliff edges or into chasms that scar the Athasian landscape.

name: Energy Stun
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You release a powerful stroke of the chosen energy type that encircles all creatures in the area, dealing 1d6 points of damage to each of them. In addition, any creature that fails its save for half damage must succeed on a Will save or be stunned for 1 round. Cold: +1 per die, Fortitude save. Electricity: +2 save DC and +2 to overcome PR. Fire: +1 per die. Sonic: -1 per die, ignores hardness. This power's subtype is the same as the type of energy you manifest.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6) and its save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Slavers and templar press-gangs across the Tablelands prize energy stun for its ability to incapacitate without killing — damaged merchandise sells for less, after all. In the chaotic street battles of Raam, a well-placed stun can freeze an entire knot of rioters long enough for the guards to move in with bone manacles. The sonic variant is particularly insidious, leaving victims reeling and deaf in the dust.

name: Inertial Barrier
description: The psionicist creates a barrier of elastic air around himself and anyone within 3 yards—an unpoppable, semipermeable bubble that softens missile impacts and shields against many damage types. The barrier protects against: nonmagical missile weapons, physically created magical missiles, missiles with magical bonuses, flames, some breath weapons, acid (slows it but doesn't prevent dripping), gas (turns it aside partially, but it seeps in after a turn), and falling damage (halved). The barrier has no effect against missiles conjured from pure magic, raw heat or cold, pure energy or light, or gaze attacks. Enemies can enter the barrier but must stop when they hit it, crossing through on the following round. When a missile strikes a target inside the barrier, the defender rolls the same damage die as the attacker to determine how much damage the barrier absorbed, subtracting this from the attack's damage.
augments: Maintenance cost of 5 power points per round to sustain the barrier.
darkSunNotes: Inertial barriers are a common defense among Athasian psionicists traveling the wastes, offering protection against sandstorms and missile-wielding raiders alike.

name: Levitation
description: Levitation allows the user to float by applying telekinesis to himself. The psionicist can rise at 1 foot per second (60 feet per round) and descend as quickly as desired by letting himself fall and slowing near the ground. Levitation provides no horizontal movement—the character hovers in place and drifts with the wind. He can push off a wall or fixed object to drift up to 60 feet per round in a straight line. The powers control wind and project force can provide directional propulsion. The psionicist can always levitate his own weight, but every 25 pounds of additional weight (equipment, passengers) reduces the power score by one point.
augments: Maintenance cost of 2 power points per round to remain aloft.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where flying mounts are rare and jealously guarded by the sorcerer-kings, the ability to rise above the scorching sands is a precious gift. Psionicist scouts levitate above the dunes to spot raider bands, and escaped slaves use it to clear the walls of city-states under cover of night.

name: Mass Manipulation
description: The psionicist can alter how gravity affects an item, increasing its weight to 200% normal or reducing it to 25% normal. Creatures burdened by extra-heavy armor suffer a -2 penalty to attack rolls and AC, and move at half speed. Weapons made heavier or lighter suffer -2 to attack and damage. Light objects can be thrown twice as far. Very heavy objects can be lightened for easier transport. Dramatic effects are possible—a doubled roof may collapse its supports, a lightened anchor may not hold. Heavier objects are more difficult to affect, with increasing penalties for objects over 20 lbs. On an exceptional manifestation, the item can be reduced to 10% or increased to 400% of normal weight.
augments: Maintain for 9 PP/round to continue the mass alteration.
darkSunNotes: Athasian psionicists use mass manipulation to lighten heavy cargo for caravans crossing the desert, or to make enemy fortifications collapse under their own doubled weight.

name: Molecular Agitation
savingThrow: Special (items save vs. destruction at +10 penalty)
description: Molecular agitation excites the molecules of a substance, generating intense heat from within. Effects escalate with each round of sustained agitation: Round 1—flammable materials ignite, skin reddens (1 hp damage), wood darkens. Round 2—wood smolders, metal becomes hot, skin blisters (1d4 damage), hair smolders. Round 3—wood ignites, metal scorches (1d4 damage), skin burns (1d6 damage), water boils, lead melts. Damage continues at the Round 3 rate for subsequent rounds. Round 4—steel grows soft. Round 5—steel melts. Items may save vs. destruction but with a +10 penalty to the saving throw number, as the heat originates from inside the material.
augments: Maintenance cost of 6 power points per round. Sustained agitation increases damage over multiple rounds as described.
darkSunNotes: A terrifyingly effective power under the Athasian sun, where materials are already heat-stressed. Psionicists have been known to melt an opponent's obsidian weapon mid-swing.

name: Molecular Manipulation
target: One object, approximately 2 sq. inches per round
description: This power weakens the molecular bonds within an object or structure, creating a breaking point so that when the object is stressed or struck, it snaps. The psionicist creates a breaking point of approximately two square inches per round. Deterioration occurs across a plane (two dimensions). One round's work is enough to fatally weaken most small objects—swords, ropes, saddle straps, belts, and bows. Larger objects require more time. A partially weakened object need not be in two pieces to be rendered useless; a cracked hull is unsafe at sea, and a half-split shield offers little protection.
augments: Maintenance cost of 5 power points per round to continue weakening the object.
darkSunNotes: In a world where metal is rarer than water, the ability to weaken or reshape materials at the molecular level is devastating. Athasian saboteurs have used this power to make a templar's bronze sword brittle as dried clay, or to soften the stone walls of a slave pen just enough for desperate hands to tear through.

name: Create Object
description: The psionicist assembles matter from air and surrounding materials to create a solid object. The object remains as long as the power is maintained, then breaks apart into its constituent parts. The created object can have any shape, color, and texture desired, provided it fits within one of the following constraints: a sphere no more than 4 feet in diameter, a cylinder no more than 20 feet long and 1 foot in diameter, a cylinder no more than 2 feet high and 6 feet in diameter, or an object weighing no more than 10 pounds. Only available materials within 20 yards can be used. If the psionicist also knows molecular rearrangement, the two powers can be combined to restructure materials—manufacturing diamonds from coal dust or a sword from iron ore, for example.
augments: Maintenance cost of 3 power points per round to sustain the object.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where metal is scarce, this power is highly prized for creating temporary tools and weapons from available stone, bone, and organic matter.

name: Detonate
target: One item up to 8 cu. ft.
description: The psionicist causes latent energy inside plants or inanimate objects to be harnessed, focused, and released explosively. This power also works against animated undead such as skeletons and zombies, but has no effect on noncorporeal undead, animals, intelligent creatures, or undead with free will. The detonation inflicts 1d10 points of damage upon all vulnerable objects chosen by the psionicist within 10 feet. A saving throw vs. breath weapon reduces damage to half. The percentage of the target destroyed equals the power check result multiplied by 10. No more than 8 cubic feet of material can be destroyed at once—a portion of a wall can be blown out, but a 10-inch-thick wall would yield only about a 3-foot-square opening.
darkSunNotes: Athasian defilers sometimes seek out psychokineticists who know this power to aid in demolishing ancient ruins.

name: Dispel Psionics
target: One manifester, creature, or object; or 20-ft.-radius burst
description: You can use dispel psionics to end ongoing powers that have been manifested on a creature or object, to temporarily suppress the psionic abilities of a psionic item, or to end ongoing powers within an area. A dispelled power ends as if its duration had expired. You choose to use dispel psionics as either a targeted dispel or an area dispel. Targeted Dispel: You make a dispel check (1d20 + your manifester level, maximum +10) against each ongoing power on the target. The DC is 11 + the power's manifester level. Area Dispel: Affects everything within a 20-foot radius, making dispel checks against each creature and object. You automatically succeed against any power you manifested yourself.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, the bonus on your dispel check increases by 2 (to a maximum bonus of +20 for a 5-point expenditure).
darkSunNotes: In a world saturated with psionic energy, dispel psionics is as essential as a steel blade — more so, because psionic threats on Athas are far more common than steel. Templar enforcers use it to strip fugitives of their mental defenses, while Veiled Alliance operatives use it to neutralize the sorcerer-kings' psionic surveillance networks. Every psychic duel on Athas involves a careful calculus of when to attack and when to dispel.

name: Energy Bolt
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You release a powerful stroke of energy of the chosen type that deals 5d6 points of damage to every creature or object within the area. The beam begins at your fingertips. Cold: A bolt of this energy type deals +1 point of damage per die. The saving throw is a Fortitude save instead of a Reflex save. Electricity: Provides a +2 bonus to the save DC and a +2 bonus on manifester level checks for overcoming power resistance. Fire: Deals +1 point of damage per die. Sonic: Deals -1 point of damage per die and ignores an object's hardness. This power's subtype is the same as the type of energy you manifest.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6). For each extra two dice of damage, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: A line of searing energy punching through the dust-choked air of Athas — this is the signature strike of many arena champions. Fire bolts leave trails of fused glass in the sandy floor of the arena, drawing roaring approval from the crowds. Lightning bolts are rarer and more spectacular, arcing wildly in the dry, static-charged atmosphere of the Tablelands.

name: Energy Burst
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You create an explosion of unstable ectoplasmic energy of the chosen type that deals 5d6 points of damage to every creature or object within the area. The explosion creates almost no pressure. Since this power extends outward from you, you are not affected by the damage. Cold: Deals +1 point of damage per die. Fortitude save instead of Reflex save. Electricity: +2 bonus to save DC and +2 bonus on manifester level checks to overcome power resistance. Fire: +1 point of damage per die. Sonic: -1 point of damage per die and ignores object hardness. This power's subtype is the same as the type of energy you manifest.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6). For each extra two dice of damage, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: When surrounded by gith raiders or thri-kreen hunting packs on the open wastes, a psion who can detonate energy in every direction simultaneously becomes the most dangerous creature on the battlefield. The burst radiates outward like a shockwave, and in the parched Athasian air, fire bursts have been known to ignite entire patches of dead scrubland, creating secondary infernos that rage for hours.

name: Energy Retort
range: Personal and close (25 ft. + 5 ft./2 levels); see text
target: You and creature or object attacking you; see text
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You weave a field of potential energy of the chosen type around your body. The first successful attack made against you in each round during the power's duration prompts a response from the field without any effort on your part. The attack may be physical, the effect of a power, or the effect of a spell. An ectoburst discharges from the field, targeting the source of the attack and dealing 4d6 points of damage of the chosen energy type. To be affected, a target must be within close range, you must have line of sight and line of effect to it, and you must be able to identify the source of the attack. The ectoburst is a ranged touch attack made using your base attack bonus plus your key ability modifier for your manifesting class. Cold: +1 per die, Fortitude save. Electricity: +2 save DC and +2 to overcome PR. Fire: +1 per die. Sonic: -1 per die, ignores hardness.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's duration increases by 1 minute.
darkSunNotes: In the treacherous courts of Nibenay and Gulg, where assassination attempts are as common as sandstorms, energy retort serves as both deterrent and punishment. The shimmering field of latent energy warns would-be attackers that striking the manifester will invite an immediate and painful reprisal. Mul gladiators who face psionically gifted opponents learn quickly to recognize the telltale shimmer — and to think twice before pressing their attack.

name: Energy Wall
target: An opaque sheet of energy up to 20 ft. long/level or a ring of energy with a radius of up to 5 ft./2 levels
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You create an immobile sheet of energy of the chosen type formed out of unstable ectoplasm. One side of the wall, selected by you, sends forth waves of energy, dealing 2d6 points of damage to creatures and objects within 10 feet and 1d6 points of damage to those beyond 10 feet but within 20 feet. In addition, anyone passing through the energy wall takes 2d6 points of damage +1 point per manifester level (maximum +20). If you manifest the wall so that it appears where creatures are, each creature takes damage as if passing through the wall. If you manifest this power in the form of a ring of energy, you choose whether the waves of energy radiate inward or outward from the ring. Cold: +1 per die, Fortitude save. Electricity: +2 save DC and +2 to overcome PR. Fire: +1 per die. Sonic: -1 per die, ignores hardness.
darkSunNotes: On a world without stone castles or iron portcullises, a wall of pure energy serves as the ultimate fortification. Caravan masters hire kineticists to erect walls of fire at night, creating a perimeter that no predator — not even a tembo — will willingly cross. During the siege of Tyr, rebel psions used rings of cold energy to trap templar squads, freezing them in place while archers finished the work.

name: Eradicate Invisibility
target: You and all invisible creatures and objects in a 50-ft.-radius burst centered on you
description: You radiate a psychokinetic burst that disrupts and negates all types of invisibility (though this power can't negate the effect of cloud mind). Any creature that fails its save to avoid the effect loses its invisibility. Creatures that are naturally invisible, such as an invisible stalker, are revealed as a dim outline for 1 round (until the beginning of your next turn) and do not have total concealment during this period.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's range and the radius of the burst in which it functions both increase by 5 feet.
darkSunNotes: In a world where psionics are ubiquitous but arcane magic is reviled, invisibility is the province of the few — and those few are dangerous. Templar investigators use eradicate invisibility to flush out veiled alliance members and hidden defilers. In the Pristine Tower's shadow, where reality bends in strange ways, this power has proven essential for revealing creatures that exist partially between the planes.

name: Kinetic Control
description: The psionicist gains the ability to absorb, store, and redirect kinetic energy. While maintaining this power, the psionicist is immune to all physical impacts—sword slashes stop on contact with his skin, arrows drop harmlessly at his feet, and claws and fangs cannot harm him. The absorbed damage is stored for later use. However, the psionicist can still be harmed by attacks that inflict damage through contact, such as energy-draining undead or constriction. To release stored energy, the psionicist must make a physical attack. Absorbed damage is added to the psionicist's normal damage on a successful hit, delivered by touch, punch, or hand-held weapon. If the psionicist stops maintaining kinetic control while still holding stored energy, he immediately suffers 2 hit points of damage for every point of absorbed damage not expended. This power cannot absorb the psionicist's own kinetic energy from a fall.
augments: Maintain for 7 PP/round. Stored energy can be discharged through melee attacks.
darkSunNotes: Gladiators in the arenas of Tyr and Balic who master kinetic control become legendary—absorbing blows from obsidian greataxes and redirecting the force into devastating counterstrikes.

name: Molecular Rearrangement
description: Molecular rearrangement is the psionic equivalent of alchemy. By manipulating an object's molecular structure, the psionicist can change its fundamental nature or properties. This power cannot create matter or mass from nothing, nor change a material's state (liquid to solid, gas to liquid, etc.). It is best suited to converting one element into another but can perform complex operations such as neutralizing poison. Converting one element to another is usually simple: one ounce of material per hour. Typical conversions include steel to lead or any metal to gold. More complex rearrangement, like metal to glass or wood to ruby, takes four times longer. The power can also be used to psionically temper weapons, potentially granting a +1 on damage rolls, though the process is time-consuming (a typical short sword takes at least 40 hours). This power has no effect against living creatures weighing more than one ounce.
augments: Maintenance cost of 10 power points per hour to continue the rearrangement process.
darkSunNotes: On metal-scarce Athas, a psionicist who can transmute common materials into metal is extraordinarily valuable—and a target for every merchant house and sorcerer-king.

name: Project Force
description: The psionicist focuses a psychokinetic punch against a target up to 200 yards away. Used offensively, this punch causes damage equal to 1d6 plus the target's armor class (negative armor classes reduce the damage). A successful save vs. breath weapon reduces damage by half. This unsubtle blow can also be used to trigger traps, throw levers, open unlocked doors, break windows, and perform similar tasks.
darkSunNotes: Gladiators who manifest this power become terrors of the arena, slamming opponents across the sand with invisible battering rams of pure psychic force. The crowds of Tyr roar with delight when a half-giant warrior is hurled twenty feet by a psionicist half his size.

name: Telekinesis
description: Telekinesis, or TK, is the ability to move objects through space without touching them. Moving small, light objects is relatively easy; heavier objects become significantly more difficult. The base cost (3 PSPs initially and 1 per round) assumes the object weighs 3 pounds or less. For heavier objects: the initial cost equals the object's weight in pounds, the maintenance cost is half the initial cost (rounded down), and the power score is decreased by one-third of the object's weight in pounds (rounded down). A psionicist using TK can move an object up to 60 feet per round. The object can serve as a weapon, using the character's own THAC0 with a penalty equal to one-third the object's weight (rounded down).
augments: Maintenance cost of 1+ power points per round. Cost scales with weight of object moved: initial cost equals weight in pounds for objects over 3 lbs.
darkSunNotes: Telekinesis is the foundational psychokinetic power and prerequisite for most other powers in the discipline. On Athas, it sees widespread use in everything from gladiatorial combat to construction.

name: Telekinetic Barrier
target: Wall up to 60 ft. long, 10 ft. high (or 10 ft. radius dome)
description: The psionicist creates an immobile, invisible barrier of mental force resembling a wall of force. The barrier can be a vertical surface up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high (or any other configuration of 600 square feet or less), or a dome with a 10-foot radius centered on the psionicist. No physical object or form of energy can penetrate the barrier, though it can be circumvented by teleportation or dimensional door. Magical or psionic disintegration destroys the barrier, but the spell or power is cancelled in the process. If the psionicist moves more than 20 yards from the barrier, it collapses.
augments: Maintain for 10 PP/round to keep the barrier standing.
darkSunNotes: In the siege warfare common between Athasian city-states, telekinetic barriers can turn a narrow canyon or gate into an impenetrable chokepoint, frustrating even the most powerful templar assaults.

name: Telekinetic Flight
description: The psionicist uses her telekinetic abilities to achieve true flight at a movement rate of 18, with maneuverability class B. She can maneuver horizontally or vertically as desired. Using telekinetic flight requires as much concentration as walking, so the psionicist can initiate other powers while flying and even attempt physical attacks. When the psionicist fails to maintain the power or runs out of power points, she drops like a rock. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist flies at a rate of 24 with maneuverability class A.
augments: Maintain for 4 PP/round to continue flying.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where flying creatures are rare and dangerous, a psionicist soaring over the Tablelands is a remarkable and intimidating sight. The power is invaluable for crossing the Sea of Silt or escaping desert predators.

name: Telekinetic Force
description: You move an object by concentrating your mind upon its current position and then the position you desire, creating a sustained force. You can move an object weighing no more than 250 pounds up to 20 feet per round. A creature can negate the effect on an object it possesses with a successful Will save or with power resistance. The weight can be moved vertically, horizontally, or both. An object moved beyond your range falls. If you cease concentration, the object falls. You can drop an object on a target to deal damage (see the rules for falling objects). You can use telekinetic force for a variety of purposes, each round choosing from the options given in the power's description.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, the weight limit of the target increases by 25 pounds.
darkSunNotes: Telekinetic force is the workhorse power of Athasian construction crews, used to move massive blocks of sandstone and obsidian that no team of slaves could lift. In Urik, where Hamanu's grand monuments demand constant expansion, teams of kineticists work in shifts to levitate building materials into place. The power also sees military use — dropping boulders on approaching enemies or lifting away the barricades that protect rebel strongholds.

name: Telekinetic Thrust
target: One or more objects or creatures with a total weight of 250 lb. or less
savingThrow: Will negates or Will negates (object); see text
description: You can hurl one or more objects or creatures that are within range and within sight toward any target within the power's range. You can hurl a total weight of up to 250 pounds. You must succeed on ranged attack rolls (one per creature or object thrown) to hit the target with the items, using your base attack bonus + Intelligence modifier. Hurled weapons deal their standard damage, while other objects deal 1d6 points of damage per 25 pounds of weight. Creatures who fall within the weight capacity of the power can be hurled, but they get a Will save to negate the effect, as do targets with power resistance. A creature hurled against a solid surface takes damage as if it had fallen 10 feet (1d6 points).
augments: For every additional power point you spend, the weight limit of the target increases by 25 pounds.
darkSunNotes: The resourceful kineticists of the Tablelands have turned the very landscape into ammunition — hurling jagged chunks of obsidian, heavy sandstone blocks, or even the bleached bones of long-dead creatures at their enemies with lethal precision. During the liberation of Tyr, rebel psions used telekinetic thrust to launch ceramic pots filled with burning oil over the walls of Kalak's ziggurat, igniting the wooden scaffolding within. The power is also grimly effective in the arena, where psions have been known to hurl their own fallen opponents as projectiles.

name: Energy Ball
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You create an explosion of energy of the chosen type that deals 7d6 points of damage to every creature or object within the area. The explosion creates almost no pressure. Cold: A ball of this energy type deals +1 point of damage per die. The saving throw to reduce damage is a Fortitude save instead of a Reflex save. Electricity: Provides a +2 bonus to the save DC and a +2 bonus on manifester level checks for overcoming power resistance. Fire: Deals +1 point of damage per die. Sonic: Deals -1 point of damage per die and ignores an object's hardness. This power's subtype is the same as the type of energy you manifest.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6). For each extra two dice of damage, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: When a kineticist unleashes an energy ball across the Athasian wastes, the detonation echoes for miles across the flat, dead terrain. Fire balls are the most common — easy to draw from the crimson sun's relentless heat — but cold manifestations are feared most, as they hint at power drawn from the frigid void beyond the Gray. Sorcerer-king Hamanu's war psions devastated entire rebel encampments with these detonations during the Cleansing Wars.

name: Telekinetic Maneuver
description: You can affect a foe by concentrating your mind upon its current position and then the position you desire, moving it with telekinetic force. You can perform a bull rush, a disarm, a grapple (including pin), or a trip against the target. Resolve these attempts as normal, except that they don't provoke attacks of opportunity, you use your manifester level in place of your base attack bonus (for the purpose of grapple checks), you add your key ability modifier (Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma) in place of your Strength modifier or Dexterity modifier, and a failed attempt doesn't allow a reactive attempt by the target. No save is allowed against these attempts, but power resistance applies normally.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power grants a +1 bonus on your checks to resolve the attempted maneuver.
darkSunNotes: In the arena, telekinetic maneuver is the mark of a sophisticated combatant — one who can wrestle an opponent to the ground without ever laying a hand on them. Spectators in Balic's grand coliseum especially appreciate the artistry of a psion who trips, disarms, and pins opponents using nothing but focused will. Outside the arena, this power is invaluable against physically superior foes like half-giants and braxat, allowing a comparatively frail psion to control creatures that would otherwise overwhelm them.

name: Disintegrate
target: One item or creature, up to 8 cu. ft.
description: This science reduces an item or creature to microscopic pieces and scatters them. Anything is vulnerable unless protected by magical shielding such as a globe of invulnerability or an inertial barrier. No more than 8 cubic feet of material can be affected at once. An inanimate object must save vs. disintegration; success means it is unaffected. A living creature, character, or undead with free will must make a saving throw vs. death magic. Success means the creature feels only a slight tingle. Failure means the creature is disintegrated.
darkSunNotes: Sorcerer-kings are known to hunt down psionicists who master this terrifying power, either to recruit or eliminate them.

name: Energy Cone
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You create a cone of energy of the chosen type, extending outward from your hand, that deals 5d6 points of damage to every creature or object within the area. Cold: +1 point of damage per die. Fortitude save instead of Reflex save. Electricity: +2 bonus to save DC and +2 on manifester level checks to overcome power resistance. Fire: +1 point of damage per die. Sonic: -1 point of damage per die and ignores object hardness. This power's subtype is the same as the type of energy you manifest.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6). For each extra two dice of damage, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Kineticists of the Tablelands learn to shape their destructive will into widening fans of devastation. A cone of fire sweeping across the salt flats near Balic can melt the crystalline surface into a treacherous sheet of molten glass. Silt runners and belgoi alike have learned to fear the characteristic shriek that precedes such attacks — the sound of air being violently displaced by raw psionic force.

name: Energy Current
target: Any two creatures no more than 15 ft. apart
