savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. Your body's psionically fueled bioenergetic currents produce an arc of energy of the chosen type that targets a creature you designate as the primary foe for 9d6 points of damage in every round when the power remains in effect. Energy also arcs off the primary foe to strike one additional foe that is initially within 15 feet of the primary foe, or that subsequently moves within 15 feet. Secondary foes take half the damage that the primary foe takes in every round while the duration lasts. Should either foe fall to less than 0 hit points, the energy current's arc randomly retargets another primary and/or secondary foe while the duration lasts. Concentrating to maintain energy current is a full-round action. Cold: +1 per die, Fortitude save. Electricity: +2 save DC and +2 to overcome PR. Fire: +1 per die. Sonic: -1 per die, ignores hardness.
augments: You can augment this power in one or both of the following ways. 1. For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6). For each extra two dice of damage, this power's save DC increases by 1. 2. For every 4 additional power points you spend, this power can affect an additional secondary target. Any additional secondary target cannot be more than 15 feet from another target of the power.
darkSunNotes: An arc of psionic energy leaping between targets — this is how the sorcerer-kings' war psions broke massed infantry formations in the Cleansing Wars. On Athas, where electricity is associated with the violent storms that sweep across the Sea of Silt, lightning currents feel most natural to kineticists. The sustained concentration required makes the manifester vulnerable, but the devastation wrought on clustered enemies makes the risk worthwhile in the desperate battles of the Tablelands.

name: Megakinesis
description: The High Science of Psychokinesis. The psionicist can perform telekinesis on a truly grand scale, moving objects weighing thousands of pounds with the power of his mind. The base cost of 30 PSPs moves an object weighing up to 500 pounds. For each additional 25 pounds, the initial cost increases by 1 PSP. The maintenance cost is always one-half the initial cost. Objects can be moved at 60 feet per round—too slow for combat use, though heavy objects can be dropped on inattentive enemies. Living creatures receive a saving throw to avoid the effect. The psionicist can also use these enormous forces to open doors, pin enemies, or bend and break objects, with an effective Strength score that scales with the weight he can move (up to Strength 25 for 1,750 lbs).
augments: Maintenance cost is half the initial cost per round. Additional weight costs 1 PP per 25 lbs above 500 lbs.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, megakineticists are rumored to move massive blocks of stone for the sorcerer-kings' construction projects—or to topple the walls of rival city-states during psionic warfare.

name: Dispelling Buffer
target: You or creature or object touched
description: You create a buffer of psychokinetic energy that protects you or another creature against dispel attempts. The subject gains a +5 bonus to the DC of any dispel check made against powers affecting the subject. Multiple dispelling buffers do not stack.
darkSunNotes: When the sorcerer-kings fortify their personal defenses, dispelling buffer is among the first protections layered on. It ensures that their carefully constructed psionic shields cannot be casually stripped away by an enemy's dispel. In battle, commanders wrap their key telepathic officers in dispelling buffers to maintain battlefield communications even when facing psychic opposition.

name: Null Psionics Field
description: An invisible barrier surrounds you and moves with you. The space within this barrier is impervious to most psionic effects, including powers, psi-like abilities, and supernatural abilities. Likewise, it prevents the functioning of any psionic items or powers within its confines. A null psionics field suppresses any power or psionic effect used within, brought into, or manifested into the area, but does not negate it. Time spent within a null psionics field counts against the suppressed effect's duration. The field functions exactly like an antimagic field with respect to psionics.
darkSunNotes: On a world saturated with psionic energy, where nearly every sentient being possesses some degree of the Will, a null psionics field is the ultimate equalizer — and the ultimate terror. When the field descends, hardened psion-warriors suddenly find themselves reduced to mere physical combatants, their mental powers snuffed like candle flames. The Order uses these fields to conduct interrogations that cannot be psionically resisted, and the sorcerer-kings maintain permanent null fields in their most secure vaults.

name: Energy Wave
savingThrow: Reflex half or Fortitude half; see text
description: Upon manifesting this power, you choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You create a flood of energy of the chosen type out of unstable ectoplasm that deals 13d6 points of damage to each creature and object in the area. This power originates at your hand and extends outward in a cone. Cold: +1 per die, Fortitude save. Electricity: +2 save DC and +2 to overcome PR. Fire: +1 per die. Sonic: -1 per die, ignores hardness. This power's subtype is the same as the type of energy you manifest.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6). For each extra two dice of damage, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: The devastating energy wave is the province of master kineticists — and the sorcerer-kings themselves. When Hamanu personally took the field during the Cleansing Wars, witnesses described waves of fire rolling across the battlefield like the crimson tide of a burning ocean. Today, few psions outside the courts of the sorcerer-kings achieve the power necessary to manifest this devastating attack, but those who do are treated with the fearful reverence usually reserved for dragons and avangions.

name: Matter Manipulation
description: You can weaken or strengthen an item. You can increase or decrease an object's hardness by up to 5 from its original hardness. You can increase or decrease the number of hit points of a 10-foot-by-10-foot section of an object by a number equal to your manifester level. This power cannot reduce an object's hardness to less than 1 or its hit points to less than 1.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, you can affect an additional 10-foot-by-10-foot section of an object.
darkSunNotes: In a world where metal is rarer than water in some places, the ability to strengthen or weaken matter at the molecular level is power beyond measure. Master kineticists serving the sorcerer-kings use matter manipulation to reinforce the obsidian walls of their ziggurats or to weaken the fortifications of rival city-states before a siege. The Order — that secretive cabal of preservers of the Way — is said to use this power to maintain ancient structures that would otherwise have crumbled to dust millennia ago.

name: Telekinetic Sphere, Psionic
target: 1-ft.-diameter/level sphere, centered around creatures or objects
description: As the telekinetic sphere spell, except as noted here. You can move the sphere and its contents at a rate of 30 feet per round. You can direct the sphere's movement mentally as a move action.
darkSunNotes: The psionic telekinetic sphere is the ultimate containment — a prison of pure psychokinetic force that can hold even the most powerful creatures. The sorcerer-kings use these spheres to transport dangerous prisoners, suspending them within translucent shells of force that float eerily through the streets of their cities. In the wastes, a kineticist who can manifest this power can create a mobile shelter against the worst silt storms, carrying allies safely above the choking clouds of deadly particulate.

name: Tornado Blast
description: You induce a tornado-force blast of air and target an area you can see within the power's range. The blast deals 17d6 points of damage to every creature and object in the area. Creatures that fail their Reflex saves are blown away, taking an additional 8d6 points of damage from the impact when they slam into a solid object. If no solid surface is within the area, creatures that fail their saves are blown 100 feet into the air and fall (taking normal falling damage). The blown-away effect results in the creature being moved to the edge of the area of effect. Wind effects from the power affect the area for 1 round after the power is manifested.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by one die (d6). For each extra two dice of damage, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: The mightiest expression of a kineticist's command over the air itself, tornado blast replicates the devastating tyr-storms that periodically scour the Tablelands clean of all life. When a master kineticist unleashes this power, the resulting vortex can strip flesh from bone, rip apart buildings of sun-dried brick, and hurl half-giants like ragdolls across the desert. Legends claim that the sorcerer-king Borys used a power like this to destroy entire cities during the Cleansing Wars, leaving nothing but scoured bedrock and howling, dust-filled craters where civilizations once stood.

name: Biofeedback
description: You gain conscious control over the flow of blood through your body. This has two key effects. First, you can easily control bleeding, reducing all physical damage you take by 2 points per attack. Second, by flooding key portions of your body with blood, you cushion blows against you, improving your armor class by 1.
augments: For every 3 additional power points spent per round, you maintain biofeedback.
darkSunNotes: A staple of Athasian gladiatorial psionicists, biofeedback provides crucial damage reduction in the arena.

name: Bite of the Wolf
description: Your posture becomes stooped forward, and you grow a muzzle complete with fangs. You gain one bite attack each round, instead of or in addition to any other attacks you have, that deals 1d8 points of damage (assuming you are a Medium creature) when it hits. Your bite attack is a natural weapon, so you are considered armed when attacking with it, and it can be affected by powers, spells, and effects that enhance or improve natural weapons. You can choose to deal nonlethal damage with your bite, taking the standard -4 penalty on your attack roll. If you bite as your only attack, you use your highest base attack bonus on the attack roll, and you can apply your full Strength bonus to damage. If you bite in addition to making other attacks, the bite is a secondary attack. Based on your psychic warrior level, your bite increases in ferocity: at 5th level an extra 1d8, at 10th level an extra 2d8, at 15th level an extra 3d8, and at 20th level an extra 4d8 points of damage.
darkSunNotes: Thri-kreen psychic warriors scoff at this power—they were born with mandibles. But for the mul gladiators of Tyr and Balic, sprouting wolfen fangs mid-combat terrifies opponents and delights the bloodthirsty crowds. The transformation echoes the feral savagery that all Athasians carry within them.

name: Body Equilibrium
description: You adjust the weight of your body to correspond with the surface beneath you, allowing you to walk on water, quicksand, or even a spider's web without sinking or breaking through. If you are falling when you manifest this power, your falling speed is reduced to 120 feet per round—slow enough to avoid injury. However, due to your extreme lightness, you are vulnerable to wind gusts, which can easily sweep you up and blow you away.
augments: For every 2 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the equilibrium.
darkSunNotes: Invaluable for crossing the treacherous Sea of Silt, where a single misstep means a slow, choking death.

name: Catfall
description: You spring like a cat and always land gracefully on your feet. Falling damage is halved, and you can drop up to 30 feet without risking any injury at all.
darkSunNotes: Every child on Athas learns to climb before they can read—if they ever learn to read at all. Psionicist scouts leaping between the towering mekillot-bone scaffolds of Raam's slums, or dropping from the walls of Balic during a raid, rely on catfall to land like hunting cats among the startled guards below.

name: Cause Decay
description: Your touch causes instant decay in inanimate objects: metal rusts, wood ages and splits, cloth crumbles to dust. The targeted object receives a Fortitude save; on a failure, the item (or up to 60 pounds of it) is consumed by decay within one round. This power has no effect on living creatures.
darkSunNotes: On metal-poor Athas, this power is both feared and coveted for its ability to destroy precious metal weapons and tools.

name: Cause Sleep
description: The psionicist can cause others to fall asleep by manipulating their natural rhythms. The victim receives a saving throw to avoid the effect. If the victim fails, he remains asleep until awakened by injury, water, or violent shaking, or until the psionicist ceases maintenance. The psionicist suffers a -2 penalty if the victim is exceptionally wakeful or is a monster. Thri-kreen and other insects can be placed in a temporary torpor, but with a -4 penalty. On an exceptional manifestation, the victim does not remember being put to sleep and may continue sleeping naturally after maintenance ends.
augments: Maintain for 2 PP/round to keep the victim asleep.
darkSunNotes: Slavers and overseers on Athas frequently employ psionicists with cause sleep to pacify unruly slaves without the risk of damaging valuable property.

name: Cell Adjustment
description: You heal wounds and cure non-magical diseases through precise cellular manipulation. You can cure a disease in one round by spending 5 power points. A cure does not automatically restore hit points lost due to illness; it merely arrests the disease. In subsequent rounds, you can heal up to 4 points of damage per round by spending 5 power points per hit point recovered. You cannot cure disease and restore hit points in the same round.
augments: For every additional 5 power points spent, you heal an additional hit point in that round, up to 4 per round.
darkSunNotes: One of the most valued healing powers on Athas, where divine healing is rare and diseases are plentiful.

name: Chameleon
description: Your skin and equipment take on the color and texture of nearby objects, including floors and walls. You receive a +10 enhancement bonus on Hide checks.
darkSunNotes: In the rocky badlands and sandy wastes of Athas, a psionicist who can blend into the terrain becomes invisible to raider patrols and predatory beasts alike. Halfling scouts of the Forest Ridge are said to have perfected this power to near-art, vanishing into the canopy like ghosts.

name: Claws of the Beast
description: You call forth the aggressive nature of the beast inherent in yourself, psionically transforming your hands into deadly claws. You gain two natural claw attacks, each dealing 1d4 points of damage (1d6 if Large, 1d3 if Small) plus your Strength bonus. Your claws are natural weapons, so you are considered armed when attacking with them. You can make a full attack with two claws at your normal attack bonus, with no two-weapon fighting penalties. You can manifest this power as a swift action and cannot manifest it when it is not your turn. You can call or dismiss the claws as a swift action during the duration.
augments: If you spend additional power points, you can create larger, sharper, and more deadly claws: 3 pp for 1d6 (Medium), 5 pp for 1d8, 7 pp for 2d6, 11 pp for 3d6, 15 pp for 4d6, 19 pp for 5d6.
darkSunNotes: In the arenas of Athas, where metal weapons are the privilege of the wealthy, psychic warriors who can sprout obsidian-hard claws from their own flesh are prized combatants. The transformation is instantaneous and terrifying—one moment bare hands, the next, raking talons that can shred chitin armor.

name: Compression
description: This power causes you to become smaller, reducing your size by one category. You gain a +2 size bonus to Dexterity, a -2 size penalty to Strength, and a +1 size bonus on attack rolls and AC due to your reduced size.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power can reduce your size by an additional size category. For each additional size category, you gain an additional +2 size bonus to Dexterity and +1 size bonus on attack rolls and AC, and you take an additional -2 size penalty to Strength.
darkSunNotes: Where expansion is the brute's tool, compression is the infiltrator's art. Athasian spies use this power to slip through the narrow ventilation shafts of templar fortresses and the cramped tunnels beneath the sorcerer-kings' palaces, emerging behind enemy lines with silent purpose.

name: Enhanced Strength
description: You increase your physical Strength score to a maximum of 18. The power point cost equals twice the number of points you add to your Strength score. The maintenance cost per round equals the number of Strength points added. Your Strength cannot be raised above 18 psionically, and you do not qualify for exceptional Strength bonuses.
augments: For every additional power point spent per round equal to the Strength points added, you maintain the enhancement.
darkSunNotes: Mul gladiators with psionic talent frequently combine this power with their natural endurance for devastating combat prowess.

name: Float
description: You buoy yourself with psychometabolic power. You can float in water without making Swim checks and need not fear drowning. You can also float in other liquids and even in quicksand.
darkSunNotes: The Sea of Silt claims thousands of lives each year—travelers who stumble into its choking depths never surface. A psychic warrior who can float atop the fine gray powder drifts across it like a leaf on water, defying the deadly morass that has swallowed entire armies.

name: Grip of Iron
description: You can improve your chances in a grapple, gaining a +4 enhancement bonus on grapple checks.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, the enhancement bonus on your grapple checks increases by 2.
darkSunNotes: In the brutal close-quarters combat of the gladiatorial arenas, grip of iron transforms a wrestler's hands into unbreakable vises. Mul gladiators, already renowned for their endurance, combine this power with their natural tenacity to become nearly impossible to escape once they've seized an opponent.

name: Heightened Senses
description: You sharpen all five of your normal senses: sight, hearing, taste, touch, and smell. This grants several benefits: You have a greatly improved chance of detecting hidden or stealthy creatures, effectively halving their stealth skill. You can track by scent like a bloodhound, making Intelligence checks each round to stay on the trail (trail must be no more than 24 hours old). Your visual and auditory ranges are tripled; you can identify a person at 400 yards in daylight. You can taste poisons or impurities in quantities too small to cause harm. You can identify objects by touch, distinguish between similar items, and tell if something has been handled in the last five minutes.
augments: For every 1 additional power point spent per round, you maintain heightened senses.
darkSunNotes: Athasian scouts and rangers with psionic talent rely on heightened senses to detect predators and track prey across the desert.

name: Lend Health
description: You transfer your own hit points to another creature you are touching. Each hit point transferred heals one point of damage. You can transfer as many points as you wish in a single round. If you attempt to transfer hit points when you have fewer than five remaining, you must succeed on an additional power check or fail. You cannot transfer hit points if you have only one remaining. The recipient cannot exceed their normal maximum hit points.
darkSunNotes: A selfless power prized among Athasian preservers and their psionic allies.

name: Photosynthesis
description: The psionicist gains energy and rejuvenation from sunlight. For each turn resting in a sunny area while using photosynthesis, he heals one hit point. If maintained for an hour or more, the power satisfies his daily food requirement and reduces water needs by half. The psionicist must remain still while using the power. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist completely fulfills his daily requirement for food and water with just one turn of rest.
augments: Maintain for 2 PP/turn. Extended use reduces food and water needs.
darkSunNotes: Under the relentless crimson sun of Athas, photosynthesis is one of the most practical survival powers a psionicist can possess. Desert wanderers who master this power can survive on sunlight alone, making them invaluable caravan companions.

name: Prevenom
description: If you have a claw attack, you can use this power to secrete a mild venom on one of your claws. On your next successful melee attack with that claw, the venom deals 2 points of Constitution damage to the target on a failed Fortitude save (DC 10 + one-half your manifester level + your key ability modifier). The venom is discharged upon a successful attack whether the target makes its save or not.
augments: If you spend 6 additional power points, the venom deals 4 points of Constitution damage.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where venomous creatures are as common as sand, psychic warriors have learned to replicate their biological weapons. The secreted venom glistens on claw tips like drops of liquid obsidian—a warning that the next scratch will carry consequences far beyond a simple wound.

name: Prevenom Weapon
description: If you have a weapon, you can use this power to secrete a mild venom from your skin that coats the weapon. On your next successful melee attack with the weapon, the venom deals 2 points of Constitution damage on a failed Fortitude save (DC 10 + one-half your manifester level + your key ability modifier). The venom is discharged upon a successful attack whether the target makes its save or not.
augments: If you spend 6 additional power points, the venom deals 4 points of Constitution damage.
darkSunNotes: Applying psionic venom to a weapon requires no alchemical knowledge and no rare ingredients—just the warrior's own will. In a world where conventional poisons are expensive and unreliable, this power gives any psychic warrior with a bone dagger the lethality of an assassin.

name: Reduction
description: You reduce your body's dimensions along any or all axes: height, length, width, or thickness. The reduction is 1 foot per power point spent, until the dimension is 1 foot or less. After that, you can halve the dimension each time you spend a power point. For example, a 6-foot-tall character can reduce to 1 foot for 5 power points, then to 6 inches for 1 more, 3 inches for another, and so on. Individual dimensions can be reduced independently—you could make your arm thin enough to fit through a keyhole without altering your height.
augments: For every 1 additional power point spent per round, you maintain the reduced size.
darkSunNotes: Halfling cannibals of the Forest Ridge use this power to shrink themselves even smaller, slipping through gaps in fortifications that no other raider could navigate. In the cities, thieves employ reduction to hide in spaces no one would think to search—inside water jugs, beneath overturned bowls, within the folds of a sleeping giant's bedroll.

name: Spider Touch
description: The psionicist forms strong, sticky pads on her fingers and toes, allowing her to adhere to almost any natural surface. While maintained, the psionicist can climb or move across vertical walls and cliffs at her normal movement rate with no fear of falling. As a secondary effect, the psionicist's gripping Strength increases to 20 for purposes of holding items. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist gains spider-like agility and receives a +2 bonus to Dexterity.
augments: Maintain for 2 PP/round to continue the adhesion effect.
darkSunNotes: In the cliff-carved cities of Athas and the rocky badlands of the Tablelands, spider touch lets a psionicist scale walls that would defeat even the most skilled mundane climber.

name: Synesthete
description: You receive one type of sense when another sense is stimulated. In particular, you can feel light or sound as a tactile sensation, hear light or feel sound as an auditory sensation, or see sound or feel light as a visual sensation. This power is particularly useful if you are blinded or deafened, providing a workaround. You gain a +4 bonus on saving throws against gaze attacks and on Spot and Listen checks.
darkSunNotes: In the blinding sandstorms of Athas, where visibility drops to zero and the howling wind drowns all sound, synesthete allows a psionicist to navigate by feeling light on their skin or seeing sound as flashes of color. It transforms a helpless victim of the elements into a functional combatant.

name: Thicken Skin
description: Your skin or natural armor thickens and spreads across your body, providing a +1 enhancement bonus to your existing natural armor bonus (if you do not have a natural armor bonus, you are considered to have a natural armor bonus of +0). This enhancement bonus increases by 1 for every 3 manifester levels you have.
augments: For every 3 additional power points you spend, the enhancement bonus increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Armor is a luxury on Athas—most warriors fight in little more than leather scraps and bone plates. Thicken skin supplements or replaces physical armor entirely, causing the psionicist's hide to toughen like dried mekillot leather. For the unarmored gladiator, this power is the difference between a flesh wound and a killing cut.

name: Vigor
description: You suffuse yourself with power, gaining 5 temporary hit points. Using this power again when an earlier manifestation has not expired merely replaces the older temporary hit points (if any remain) with the newer ones.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, the number of temporary hit points you gain increases by 5.
darkSunNotes: The most fundamental survival power on Athas, vigor is the first thing most young psionicists learn. Those extra hit points are the margin between surviving a gith raider's ambush and bleeding out in the sand. Every caravan guard, every arena fighter, every wandering psion on the Tablelands knows this power by heart.

name: Absorb Disease
description: You draw a disease from another creature's body and absorb it into yourself. Presumably, you will then heal yourself using complete healing or similar means. This power can absorb magical diseases, but not curses such as lycanthropy.
darkSunNotes: With disease rampant in the slave pits and undercities of Athas, this power makes psychometabolists invaluable healers.

name: Accelerate
description: The psionicist drastically alters his metabolism, doubling his speed for short periods. While accelerated, the psionicist is effectively hasted: movement and attack rate are doubled, and he gains a -2 bonus to initiative rolls. Spellcasting and psionic use are not sped up, though the power can counter a magical slow effect. After the psionicist stops maintaining accelerate, he must rest for a number of rounds equal to the time he was accelerated, doing nothing but catching his breath. Unlike the haste spell, this power does not cause aging. On an exceptional manifestation, movement and attacks are tripled and the psionicist automatically strikes first.
augments: Maintain for 10 PP/round to continue the accelerated state. Rest period equals duration used.
darkSunNotes: Athasian gladiators and mercenary psionicists use accelerate to become blurs of lethal motion in the arena, though the exhaustion that follows makes timing crucial.

name: Adrenaline Control
description: By controlling the production and release of adrenaline in your body, you give yourself a temporary physical boost. You gain 1d6 points, which you can distribute among your Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution scores as you choose. You enjoy all normal bonuses for high physical attributes while this power is in effect.
augments: For every 4 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the adrenaline surge.
darkSunNotes: Gladiators in the arenas of Tyr and Balic prize this power for the edge it provides in deadly combat.

name: Alter Features
description: The psionicist can alter his appearance through limited control over his facial features. He can change skin coloration to any normal variation, alter hair appearance, change eye color, and adjust the bone structure of his face. The psionicist cannot pass for a member of another species. On a strong manifestation, the psionicist can mimic a particular individual's features well enough to fool anyone who relies on appearance alone. Close examination requires an additional power check to maintain the disguise. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist automatically passes any checks to protect his disguise.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/turn to keep the altered features.
darkSunNotes: In the intrigue-laden courts of the Athasian city-states, alter features allows a psionicist to impersonate nobles, templars, or even wanted fugitives—a skill prized by the Veiled Alliance and feared by the sorcerer-kings' spies.

name: Body Adjustment
description: You take control of your body's healing process, curing yourself of 1d12 points of damage. As usual, when regular damage is healed, an equal amount of nonlethal damage is also healed.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power heals an additional 1d12 points of damage.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where healing magic is scarce and clerics serve the elements rather than merciful gods, body adjustment is often the only recourse for a wounded warrior. Psychic warriors in the gladiatorial pits learn to knit their flesh between bouts, their bones cracking audibly back into place as the crowd roars.

name: Body Control
description: You adapt your body to survive in a hostile environment. The adaptation must be keyed to a specific surrounding: water, acid, extreme heat, extreme cold, an elemental plane, etc. If the power succeeds, you not only survive, you fit in like a native organism—you can breathe and move normally and take no damage simply from being in that environment. An attack in any form does not constitute an environment; for example, surviving subarctic temperatures does not grant immunity to a cone of cold.
augments: For every 5 additional power points spent per 10 minutes, you maintain the adaptation.
darkSunNotes: Essential for traversing the Sea of Silt, the obsidian wastes, and other lethal Athasian environments.

name: Body Purification
description: You restore up to 2 points of damage to a single ability score. You cannot use body purification to heal ability drain.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power heals 1 additional point of damage to the same ability score.
darkSunNotes: Athasian poisons are legion—from the venomous sting of a crystal spider to the slow-acting toxins favored by templar assassins. Body purification allows a psionicist to burn away these taints from within, a vital counter in a world where antidotes are as rare as iron.

name: Body Weaponry
description: You convert one of your arms into a weapon. Virtually any melee weapon can be imitated—except ranged weapons or any weapon you cannot normally use. Your arm actually becomes wood and/or metal and assumes the weapon's form. It behaves in every respect like a normal weapon of the chosen type, with a bonus: it can never be dropped, disarmed, or stolen.
augments: For every 4 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the weapon form.
darkSunNotes: On weapon-scarce Athas, the ability to produce a weapon from one's own body is a powerful advantage in the gladiatorial arena.

name: Chameleon Power
description: You change the coloration of your skin, clothing, and equipment to match your surroundings. The match is automatic; you do not choose the appearance. As you move, the coloration shifts to reflect changes in the surroundings. While stationary, you are extremely difficult to detect. If you move, detection becomes somewhat easier. This power is most effective in natural surroundings; in urban settings or areas without natural cover in broad daylight, its effectiveness is halved.
augments: For every 3 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the camouflage.
darkSunNotes: Desert-dwelling Athasian psionicists use chameleon power to blend with the rocky barrens and sandy wastes, evading raiders and predators.

name: Chemical Simulation
description: You cause your body to simulate the action of acid. You secrete a corrosive substance through your hand; any item you touch and hold briefly must make a Fortitude save or be dissolved. When used as a weapon against a creature, this acid inflicts 2-3 points of damage per touch and causes considerable pain.
augments: For every 6 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the acid secretion.
darkSunNotes: The poisoners of Nibenay's Shadow Court are rumored to secrete any venom they have ever tasted, their bodies becoming living laboratories of death. More than one sorcerer-king's food taster has been a psionicist using this power in reverse—neutralizing toxins by understanding them from within.

name: Displacement
description: You appear to be up to 3 feet from your actual location, similar to a displacer beast's natural ability. You decide where the false image appears. This grants you a +2 bonus to armor class. True seeing reveals your real location.
augments: For every 3 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the displacement effect.
darkSunNotes: In the gladiatorial pits, a displaced psionicist is a maddening opponent—blades slash through afterimages while the true warrior strikes from inches away. The crowd loves the spectacle, and more than one betting house has been ruined by a fighter whose image never quite matched reality.

name: Dissolving Touch
description: Your touch, body, or weapons deal 4d6 points of acid damage to a single target. You must succeed on a melee touch attack to hit.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's damage increases by 1d6 points.
darkSunNotes: In a world where armor is often made from chitin, bone, and hardened leather, dissolving touch is devastatingly effective. A psychic warrior's acid-slicked palm can eat through a braxat's carapace or dissolve the lock on a slave pen with equal ease.

name: Dissolving Weapon
description: As dissolving touch, except your weapon deals the acid damage in addition to its normal damage with each hit. The acid damage is not doubled on a critical hit.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's acid damage increases by 1d6 points.
darkSunNotes: Coating a bone longsword or obsidian axe in psionic acid gives an Athasian warrior a decisive advantage against heavily armored foes. The hissing, smoking weapon leaves trails of corroded flesh and dissolved chitin in its wake—a sight that unnerves even veteran gladiators.

name: Double Pain
description: By touching another creature, you drastically lower their pain threshold. For 1 minute, all damage scored against the target is effectively doubled. However, only half of this total represents real damage; the other half is amplified pain. When the target's combined real and pain damage reduces their hit points to zero or less, they fall unconscious, regaining consciousness 1d10 rounds later and recovering the 'pain' hit points. If the target does not fall unconscious within the duration, damage is no longer doubled, but accumulated pain damage persists for an additional 1d6 rounds.
darkSunNotes: Templars of the sorcerer-kings use this power as a tool of interrogation and punishment.

name: Ectoplasmic Form
description: You convert yourself into ectoplasm—a fine-spun, smoky substance. You become insubstantial and ghostlike, able to pass through solid material as if it did not exist. You are still visible as a wispy outline. You move at your normal speed and in the normal fashion (if you could not fly before, you cannot now). You can also convert your clothing, armor, and up to 15 pounds of equipment that you are carrying.
augments: For every 9 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the ectoplasmic form.
darkSunNotes: The Gray presses close to the material world on Athas, and psionicists who assume ectoplasmic form describe the experience as half-stepping into that realm of the dead. They become translucent, ghostly figures that can slip through prison walls—a sight that sends superstitious templars scrambling for their holy symbols.

name: Empathic Transfer
description: You heal another creature's wounds, transferring its damage to yourself. When you manifest this power, you can transfer up to 2d10 points of damage from the touched creature to yourself. You cannot take more damage than would reduce you to 0 or fewer hit points.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, you can transfer an additional 2d10 points of damage (maximum 10d10 for 7 additional power points).
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where divine healing is the jealous province of elemental clerics, empathic transfer represents the ultimate sacrifice of one mind for another. Psionicists who travel with merchant caravans often serve as battlefield medics, absorbing the wounds of fallen guards into their own flesh—a painful but vital service.

name: Energy Adaptation, Specified
description: As energy adaptation, except you must choose cold, electricity, fire, or sonic when you learn this power, and it only protects against that one energy type. You gain resistance 10 against the chosen energy type, absorbing up to 10 points of damage per manifester level before the power ends.
augments: If you spend 4 additional power points, you can manifest this power as an immediate action.
darkSunNotes: Most Athasian psionicists choose fire adaptation, given the relentless heat of the dark sun and the prevalence of defiler flame magic. But those who travel the obsidian wastes near ancient volcanic sites, or who face the lightning-wielding druids of the storm circles, may choose differently.

name: Expansion
description: You expand your body's proportions in any dimension—height, length, width, or thickness. You can increase any or all proportions by 50% of their original size per round, up to a maximum of four times your original size. This power has no effect on clothing or equipment, and your ability scores do not increase. However, being significantly larger often provides tactical advantages.
augments: For every 1 additional power point spent per round, you maintain the expanded size.
darkSunNotes: Mul gladiators who can expand their already formidable frames become living siege engines in the arena. The sight of a warrior swelling to twice his height, his obsidian-studded gauntlets now large enough to crush a man's skull in one hand, has ended more than one match before a blow was struck.

name: Flesh Armor
description: You transform your skin into armor. The transformation is invisible to others, but provides genuine protection. The type of armor your body mimics depends on the success of your manifestation: the better the result, the stronger the armor, ranging from a shield equivalent (AC 9) up to plate mail (AC 3). Because this armor is part of your body, you enjoy its benefits without suffering any penalties for weight, encumbrance, or restrictions on psionic powers. The armor weighs nothing and has no magnetic properties.
augments: For every 4 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the flesh armor.
darkSunNotes: On armor-scarce Athas, flesh armor is a highly sought power among gladiators and free psionicists alike.

name: Graft Weapon
target: You and one melee weapon touched
description: You graft any one-handed melee weapon directly onto your body. You pick up the weapon, manifest this power, and the weapon becomes an extension of your arm. If you are proficient with the weapon, you gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage. If you are not proficient, you suffer the usual nonproficiency penalties but still gain the +1 bonus.
augments: For every 1 additional power point spent per round, you maintain the graft.
darkSunNotes: In the desperate fighting pits beneath Urik, where slaves are given no weapons and expected to die for sport, psionicists who can fuse a stolen bone shard or stone fragment directly into their flesh gain an edge that no guard can confiscate. The weapon becomes the warrior.

name: Immovability
description: You anchor yourself to the fabric of the universe, becoming completely immovable. Moving you requires a combined Strength total at least 10 times greater than your power score (Constitution -5). A creature that makes a successful bend bars check can double the Strength it contributes to the effort. Even if moved once, subsequent attempts must meet the same threshold. This power has nothing to do with weight—you will not crash through floors. You do, however, need a horizontal surface as an anchor. You remain immovable until you cease maintaining the power.
augments: For every 6 additional power points spent per round, you maintain immovability.
darkSunNotes: The mul bodyguards of House Wavir are trained in this power from youth—they plant themselves before their merchant lord and become as immovable as the Ringing Mountains themselves. Charging half-giants have broken their own shoulders against a psionicist rooted to the earth by sheer will.

name: Mind Over Body
description: You suppress your body's need for food, water, and sleep. In exchange for one hour of meditation per day, all physical needs are overcome—you feel no hunger, exhaustion, or thirst, and suffer no ability reductions for privation. You can also suppress the needs of others: each additional person costs 10 extra power points and an additional hour of meditation, during which all participants must maintain physical contact. You can sustain this for a number of days equal to your manifester level or 5 days, whichever is greater. At the end, you collapse from exhaustion and must rest one day for every two days spent using this power.
augments: For every 10 additional power points spent per day, you can sustain one additional creature.
darkSunNotes: Absolutely critical for desert crossings on Athas, where water is more precious than gold.

name: Prowess
description: If you are a psychic warrior with the ability to make multiple attacks, you can manifest this power when you miss with a melee attack. You get to immediately take an additional attack at the same attack bonus as the attack you just missed with.
darkSunNotes: A psychic warrior's reflexes are honed by the relentless Athasian struggle for survival. Prowess represents that split-second correction—the blade that whiffs past a foe's guard and instantly reverses for a second strike. In the lightning-fast exchanges of gladiatorial combat, this power turns a near-miss into a killing blow.

name: Psionic Lion's Charge
description: You can make a full attack at the end of a charge, instead of the single attack normally allowed after a charge.
augments: If you spend 6 additional power points, you can also make a full attack after making a charge while mounted.
darkSunNotes: Named for the Athasian lion—a ferocious plains predator that strikes with all four paws at once—this power captures the overwhelming momentum of a desert predator's pounce. Mounted psychic warriors on crodlu-back use the augmented version to devastating effect, crashing into enemy lines with the fury of a charging inix.

name: Psionic Scent
description: You gain the scent ability. You can detect approaching enemies, sniff out hidden foes, and track by sense of smell. You can detect opponents within 30 feet by sense of smell. If the opponent is upwind, the range increases to 60 feet; if downwind, the range drops to 15 feet. You can follow tracks by smell, making a Wisdom check to find or follow a track.
darkSunNotes: The desert winds of Athas carry the scent of prey and predator alike across vast distances. A psychic warrior with psionic scent can track a fleeing slave through the warrens of Tyr's undercity or detect a lurking sand drake buried just beneath the dunes before it springs its ambush.

name: Share Pain
target: You and one willing creature, or two willing creatures; see text
description: This power creates a psychometabolic connection between you and a willing subject so that some of your wounds are transferred to the subject. You take half damage from all attacks that deal hit point damage to you, and the subject takes the remainder. If you and the subject move farther apart than close range, the power ends. You can manifest this power on two willing subjects, one of which you designate to share its damage with the other.
darkSunNotes: Gladiatorial teams in the arenas of Athas use share pain to distribute wounds among paired fighters, keeping their champion standing while a tougher companion absorbs the punishment. Outside the arena, caravan guards link themselves to their charges, ensuring that a single ambush strike cannot fell their protector in one blow.

name: Share Strength
description: You lend your physical Strength to another creature. You sacrifice two points of your own Strength ability score for every single point the recipient gains. This transfer remains in effect until you stop maintaining the power, at which point all points return in one round. If the recipient is killed before you recover your Strength, the loss is permanent.
augments: For every 2 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the strength transfer.
darkSunNotes: Slave gangs in the obsidian quarries of Urik survive by sharing what little strength they have. A psionicist who can transfer his vitality to a faltering companion may save a life today—and earn a loyal ally who will remember when the time comes to break their chains.

name: Strength of My Enemy
description: You gain the ability to siphon away your enemy's strength for your own use. One of your natural or manufactured weapons becomes the instrument of your desire. For each successful melee attack you make with this weapon against a living, corporeal creature, you drain 1 point of Strength from your target and add that amount as an enhancement bonus to your Strength score. You are not healed if the target's Strength drops to 0.
darkSunNotes: In the dog-eat-dog world of Athasian survival, strength of my enemy is disturbingly literal—every blow you land makes you stronger and your foe weaker. Mul gladiators who manifest this power in prolonged arena bouts become more devastating as the fight drags on, growing visibly more muscular as their opponent wilts.

name: Suspend Animation
description: You bring all life functions to a virtual halt, appearing dead. Only the most careful examination reveals that you are still alive. Even psionic life detection and ESP cannot detect life unless maintained for at least three minutes; only the probe power detects life immediately. You remain suspended for a number of weeks determined by your manifestation result, setting a wake time before entering the trance. Another willing creature can also be suspended for the same power point cost, with a preparation time of one hour and a range of touch (though not simultaneously with yourself).
darkSunNotes: Used by Athasian psionicists to endure long desert journeys in stasis, or to feign death when hunted by templars.

name: Sustenance
description: You can go without food and water for one day. Each time you manifest this power, your body manufactures enough nourishment to sustain yourself for 24 hours.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where water is more precious than gold and food is scarce even in the city-states, sustenance is arguably the most important survival power a psionicist can learn. Those who master it can cross the trackless wastes between Tyr and Urik without a single waterskin, their bodies generating nourishment from psionic energy alone.

name: Aging
description: By touching another creature, you cause unnatural aging. This power is inherently malevolent; only evil psionicists can use it without their alignment shifting toward evil. The victim ages 1d4 years instantly and must make a Fortitude save. Failure means the change was traumatic, causing an additional year of aging.
darkSunNotes: The sorcerer-kings of Athas are rumored to have used this power to punish disobedient servants, aging them into frailty.

name: Animal Affinity
description: When you first learn this power, you develop an affinity for a particular type of animal, determined by your psychic aura (roll 1d20: 1-Ape, 2-Barracuda, 3-Boar, 4-Bull, 5-Crocodile, 6-Giant Eagle, 7-Elephant, 8-Falcon, 9-Griffon, 10-Grizzly Bear, 11-Lion, 12-Panther, 13-Percheron, 14-Peregrine Falcon, 15-Rattlesnake, 16-Giant Scorpion, 17-Shark, 18-Stag, 19-Tiger, 20-Wolf). When you manifest this power, you claim one of the animal's attributes as your own: its armor class, movement rate and mode, attacks and damage, base attack bonus, hit points, or any special ability. Only one attribute can be used at a time. Switching to a different ability requires paying the initial cost again. Your body undergoes a partial physical transformation appropriate to the ability gained, such as growing wings for flight or fangs for bite attacks.
augments: For every additional 4 power points spent per round, you may maintain the assumed attribute.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, common animal affinities include mekillot, erdlu, kirre, and inix, reflecting the harsh desert fauna.

name: Claws of the Vampire
description: If you have a claw attack (either from an actual natural weapon or from an effect such as claws of the beast), you can use this power to change the nature of that weapon. Each time you make a successful claw attack against a living creature of Small or larger size, you are healed of some amount of damage. You heal a number of hit points equal to half the base damage dealt by your claws, rounded down (additional damage from Strength or enhancements does not count). You heal as many hit points as can be gained while the creature remains at 1 hit point or higher. You do not heal damage if your attack deals nonlethal damage.
darkSunNotes: This power embodies the brutal philosophy of Athasian survival: take what you need from the flesh of others. Gladiators who combine claws of the beast with claws of the vampire become self-sustaining killing machines, draining vitality from their opponents with every savage strike—a spectacle that drives arena crowds into a frenzy.

name: Duodimensional Claw
description: If you have a claw attack (either from a natural weapon or from a power such as claws of the beast), you can use this power to improve that weapon. One of your claw attacks becomes two-dimensional, making it razor-sharp. Your claw attack threatens a critical hit on a natural 19-20 and deals an extra 2d6 points of damage on a successful critical hit (this extra damage is in addition to the normal critical hit damage).
darkSunNotes: The thri-kreen mantis warriors of the Hinterlands speak of claws that cut between the folds of reality itself. A duodimensional claw slices through matter as if it were gossamer, ignoring the thickness of armor entirely—a terrifying refinement of the beast within.

name: Empathic Transfer, Hostile
description: You transfer your hurt to another. When you manifest this power and then make a successful touch attack, you can transfer 50 points of damage (or less, if you choose) from yourself to the touched creature. You heal yourself of the damage transferred, but you cannot heal more damage than you have taken. The target can attempt a Will save to take only half damage.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, you can transfer an additional 10 points of damage (maximum 90 at 13 additional power points).
darkSunNotes: A favorite of Athasian templar interrogators and arena psionicists, hostile empathic transfer allows the manifester to dump their accumulated agony onto a helpless victim. In the slave pits of Urik, this power is used to punish disobedient laborers without leaving visible marks—the pain simply appears from within.

name: Energy Containment
description: You have trained your body to safely absorb and assimilate harmful energy from electricity, fire, cold, heat, and sound. When subjected to an energy attack, you draw the energy into your body, transform it, and release it as visible radiance. If you make a successful power check, you double the result of your saving throw die roll against the energy attack. On a successful save, you suffer no damage; on a failed save, you suffer only half damage regardless of what the effect normally allows. After absorbing energy, you radiate soft visible light (illuminating a 2-yard radius) for a number of rounds equal to the damage you would have taken.
darkSunNotes: Under the crimson sun of Athas, this power is frequently used to endure the brutal heat of the desert and the elemental fury of defiler magic.

name: Exhalation of the Black Dragon
description: Your mouth spews forth a lesser torrent of vitriolic acid that deals 3d6 points of acid damage to any targets in the area.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's damage increases by 1d6 points.
darkSunNotes: A lesser echo of the breath of the black dragon, this power is commonly manifested by psychic warriors who need an area attack without the full power investment. In the confined tunnels beneath Athasian cities, even a small cone of acid can devastate a patrol of pursuing templars.

name: Life Draining
description: You drain hit points from another creature by touch and use them to restore your own vitality. The transfer occurs at a rate of 1d6 hit points per round. You can absorb up to 10 more hit points than your normal maximum, but these bonus hit points last only one hour. After that time, any hit points exceeding your normal maximum vanish.
augments: For every 5 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the draining link.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, this power is viewed with suspicion, as it mirrors the life-draining nature of defiler magic.

name: Nerve Manipulation
description: The psionicist can inflict terrible effects on another creature's nervous system with a touch, causing wracking pain, unconsciousness, or even death. The power works best on creatures of the psionicist's own species (no penalty), with -2 for different species and -4 for different orders (insects, mollusks). A touch attack is required. The effect depends on the manifestation result: spasms (-2 to attacks and AC for 1d3 rounds), pain (-4 penalty for 1d3 rounds), stunning (unable to act for 1d4+1 rounds), unconsciousness (out cold for 2d6 turns), or death (collapses and dies in 1d3 rounds). The victim may save vs. poison to avoid all effects. Effects can be neutralized by neutralize poison or antidote. The psionicist can choose a lesser effect than indicated.
darkSunNotes: Athasian assassins and mercenary psionicists prize nerve manipulation for its lethal efficiency. A single touch in a crowded marketplace can fell a target without anyone noticing the cause.

name: Painful Strike
description: Your natural weapons cause additional pain. Each successful attack you make with a natural weapon or unarmed strike deals an extra 1d6 points of nonlethal damage to the target.
augments: If you spend 2 additional power points, the extra nonlethal damage increases to 2d6. If you spend 6 additional power points, the extra damage is lethal.
darkSunNotes: In the arenas of Athas, where crowd-pleasing performances matter as much as kills, painful strike lets a gladiator make an opponent writhe and scream without delivering a fatal blow. The agonized cries of the target drive the mob into ecstasy—and a pleased crowd means freedom, or at least another day of life.

name: Poison Simulation
description: The psionicist can produce natural toxins from her own body. She can only simulate poisons she has experienced or tasted. If she purposely tastes a small amount of poison to learn it, she suffers half normal effects with +2 on saving throws. The toxin can be created in the bloodstream or saliva. Contact poisons affect victims by touch, injective poisons can coat weapons, and ingestive poisons in the bloodstream affect any creature that bites the psionicist. A dose remains potent for a number of rounds equal to the psionicist's level. The power also grants secondary benefits: +4 on all saving throws against poison, +8 against poisons she can manufacture, and the ability to produce antidotes for others poisoned with toxins she knows.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where venomous creatures abound and assassination is a political art, poison simulation makes a psionicist into a living weapon—coating obsidian blades with replicated giant scorpion venom before a strike.

name: Regenerate
description: The psionicist can repair permanent injuries that natural healing cannot restore. Severed limbs can be regrown, blindness or deafness repaired, and even paralyzing injuries to the brain or spinal cord can be healed. The psionicist can heal himself or others. Regeneration is slow: one full turn for minor damage (lost finger, broken bone, non-destructive sensory loss), three turns for major damage (badly broken bone, mangled hand or foot), and up to six turns for severe injuries (arm severed at the elbow, destroyed eyes). Regeneration can repair damage from disease but cannot cure the disease itself. It can restore lost ability points from disease or injury but cannot improve a person beyond their original condition.
augments: Maintain for 6 PP/turn to continue the regeneration process.
darkSunNotes: In the brutal gladiatorial arenas and savage deserts of Athas, where healers are scarce and injuries are horrific, a psionicist with regenerate is worth their weight in water.

name: Shadow-form
description: You transform yourself into living shadow, along with your clothing, armor, and up to 20 pounds of equipment. You can blend perfectly into any other shadow and travel with a movement rate of 6. You can only travel through darkness and shadow; areas of open light are impassable. While in shadow-form, you can be detected only by life detection, psychic detection, or a true seeing spell. You cannot harm anyone physically or manipulate any corporeal object, but you can still use psionic powers.
augments: For every 3 additional power points spent per round, you maintain the shadow-form.
darkSunNotes: In the shadow-haunted ruins beneath the Athasian sands, this power provides unmatched stealth for infiltration.

name: Share Pain, Forced
description: As share pain, except as noted here. You attempt to force the sharing of your wounds with an unwilling creature, and for less time. If you are immune to the type of damage dealt, or if you convert lethal damage into nonlethal damage, the target takes no damage.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: A cruel favorite of Athasian psionicists who serve as enforcers, forced share pain lets a wounded manifester dump their accumulated suffering onto an unwilling victim. Templar commanders have been known to link themselves to captured prisoners before battle, ensuring that every wound they take in combat is mirrored on their hostage.

name: Vampiric Blade
description: As claws of the vampire, except your weapon is affected rather than your claws. Each successful melee attack you make with the affected weapon heals you of half the base weapon damage dealt (not including bonuses from Strength, enhancement, or other sources).
darkSunNotes: A weapon that drinks blood and feeds it back to its wielder—such is the horror of a vampiric blade. In the arenas of Athas, where drawn-out battles of attrition determine the victor, a gladiator whose weapon heals them with every strike can outlast any opponent, growing fresher while their foe weakens.

name: Complete Healing
description: You enter a deep healing trance for 24 hours, during which your body repairs itself at an incredible rate. At the end of the trance, you awaken completely healed of all wounds, ailments, and non-magical diseases. The trance cannot be broken unless you suffer 5 or more points of damage. If your power check fails, you break the trance after only one hour, realizing the power did not take hold, expending only 5 power points instead of the full cost.
darkSunNotes: In the wastes of Athas where healing magic is scarce, complete healing is among the most prized psychometabolic powers.

name: Empathic Feedback
description: You psychically are in tune with your body so that when an opponent strikes you in melee, it also takes damage. Each time a creature hits you with a melee attack, it takes an amount of damage equal to the damage it dealt to you or your Wisdom modifier, whichever is less. Creatures that hit you with natural weapons or unarmed strikes are affected, as are creatures that hit you with handheld weapons.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, the damage the attacker receives increases by 1 point (up to the amount of damage it dealt).
darkSunNotes: In the gladiatorial pits of Draj and Gulg, egoists who manifest empathic feedback become walking traps. Every blow landed against them rebounds upon the attacker—a fact that forces even the most aggressive arena champions to reconsider their assault, turning raw aggression into a liability.

name: Energy Adaptation
description: Your body assimilates the type of energy you specify when you manifest this power (cold, electricity, fire, or sonic), then releases it harmlessly. You gain resistance 10 against that energy type. Once you have absorbed 10 points of energy damage per manifester level, the power ends.
augments: If you spend 4 additional power points, you can manifest this power as an immediate action.
darkSunNotes: The Athasian wastes are a crucible of extremes—searing heat by day, bitter cold by night, and the lightning storms of the Tyr region can strike without warning. Energy adaptation allows a psionicist to walk through a defiler's fireball or withstand the elemental fury of a cleric's invocation without flinching.

name: Keen Edge, Psionic
target: One weapon or fifty projectiles, all of which must be in contact with each other at the time of manifestation
description: As the keen edge spell. This power makes a weapon magically keen, improving its ability to deal telling blows. The power doubles the threat range of the weapon. A threat range of 20 becomes 19-20, 19-20 becomes 17-20, and 18-20 becomes 15-20. The power can be used on natural weapons or unarmed strikes.
darkSunNotes: Obsidian weapons are sharp but brittle, and bone blades dull quickly under the Athasian sun. A psychic warrior who can psionically hone any blade to supernatural sharpness is invaluable to any war party. The difference between a normal obsidian sword and a psionically keened one is the difference between a glancing blow and a killing stroke.

name: Metamorphosis
description: You can change yourself into any form with approximately the same mass as your body—a wolf, a condor, a chair, a rock, a tree. You retain your own hit points and base attack bonus, but gain the armor class of the new form. You also gain all physical attacks that the form allows, but no magical or special abilities. This transformation causes great physical stress; you must make a Fortitude save (system shock) to survive the change. On a failure, you do not die, but the power points are expended and you fall unconscious for 2d6 × 10 minutes.
augments: For every 1 additional power point spent per 10 minutes, you maintain the new form.
darkSunNotes: Athasian psionicists often use metamorphosis to take the forms of desert creatures for camouflage and survival in the wastes.

name: Psychic Vampire
description: This power allows you to drain power points from another psionic creature. The target loses up to the number of power points you designate (maximum equal to your manifester level). You gain that many power points, up to your normal maximum.
augments: For every 3 additional power points you spend, the save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: The psionicists of Athas understand that power is the ultimate currency, and psychic vampire is the most direct way to steal it. In psionic duels—formalized combat between rival mind-lords—this power can turn the tide in an instant, draining an opponent's reserves and replenishing one's own. The practice is widely condemned but secretly employed by templars and nobles alike.

name: Truevenom
description: If you have a claw attack (either from a natural weapon or from a power such as claws of the beast), you can use this power to produce a potent venom on your claw. Your next successful melee attack with the envenomed claw forces the target to make a Fortitude save or take 1d8 points of Constitution damage immediately. One minute later, the target must save again or take another 1d8 points of Constitution damage.
darkSunNotes: Truevenom replicates the deadliest poisons found on Athas—the kind secreted by crystal spiders and cistern fiends. A psychic warrior who delivers this toxin through their own claws becomes a walking death sentence. In the venomous ecology of the Tablelands, where every creature carries some form of natural poison, this power makes the manifester the apex predator.

name: Truevenom Weapon
description: As truevenom, except your weapon gains the venom coating. Your next successful melee attack with the coated weapon forces the target to make a Fortitude save or take 1d8 points of Constitution damage immediately. One minute later, the target must save again or take another 1d8 points of Constitution damage.
darkSunNotes: Where truevenom works through claws, truevenom weapon extends that lethality to any handheld weapon. An obsidian short sword or bone wrist razor coated in psionic venom becomes as deadly as a poisoned assassin's blade, without the telltale residue that conventional poisons leave behind.

name: Weapon of Energy
description: As the call weaponry power, except the weapon you create is composed of one type of energy chosen at the time of manifestation: cold, electricity, or fire. Attacks with this weapon deal normal weapon damage plus 2d6 points of energy damage of the chosen type. On a critical hit, the weapon deals an additional 1d10 points of energy damage.
darkSunNotes: A blade of crackling lightning or roaring flame needs no edge to sharpen and no material to craft—it springs fully formed from the psychic warrior's will. On Athas, where metal weapons are priceless artifacts, a weapon of pure energy is both awe-inspiring and terrifyingly effective, cutting through chitin and bone armor with equal ease.

name: Adapt Body
description: Your body automatically adapts to hostile environments. You can adapt to underwater, extremely hot, extremely cold, or airless environments, allowing you to survive as if you were a creature native to that environment. You can breathe and move (though penalties to movement and attacks, if any for a particular environment, remain), and you take no damage simply from being in that environment. You need not specify what environment you are adapting to when you manifest this power; simply activate it, and your body will instantly adapt to any hostile environment as needed throughout the duration. You can somewhat adapt to extreme environmental features such as acid, lava, fire, and electricity. Any environmental feature that normally directly deals 1 or more dice of damage per round deals you only half the usual amount of damage.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the crimson sun scorches the earth to cracking and the Sea of Silt chokes the lungs of the unwary, adapt body is the difference between survival and a bleached skeleton. Egoists who master this power cross the deadliest wastes with impunity, enduring environments that would kill even a mekillot.

name: Death Field
description: You create a life-sapping field of negative energy radiating outward from your body. Only psionicists of evil alignment can learn this power without suffering corruption; any other manifester who learns it finds their alignment gradually twisted toward evil. Before manifesting, you must decide how many hit points you will sacrifice—this loss is unavoidable and requires no saving throw. Every other living creature within the 20-yard radius must make a Fortitude save or lose the same number of hit points. Those who succeed take no damage.
darkSunNotes: Defilers and evil psionicists on Athas have been known to combine this power with defiling magic for devastating effect on the battlefield.

name: Elemental Composition
description: The High Science of Psychometabolism. The psionicist can transform his body into any element—dense materials like iron or obsidian, sand or earth, water, or even air. While in elemental form, the psionicist gains natural immunities and vulnerabilities of that element. Sword cuts cannot harm a watery or gaseous body; iron bodies need not breathe but can be rusted. Very dense bodies (stone or metal) reduce Armor Class to 0 and grant effective Strength of 21. Amorphous bodies can slither under doors and elongate limbs. Gaseous bodies drift with the winds. Normal weapons may be unable to harm some forms, but magical weapons can always harm the psionicist regardless of form. The psionicist may change form once per round as a free action; changing a second time costs a full-round action. Each elemental form also grants movement through its element at normal speed.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/round. The psionicist may change elemental form once per round.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the elemental planes are intimately connected to the world through clerical magic, elemental composition represents the ultimate fusion of psychometabolic mastery—becoming one with the primal forces that shape the dying world.

name: Fiery Discorporation
description: You use this power when you would otherwise take damage that would reduce you to 0 or fewer hit points. You dissolve your material body into a swirling column of fire. While in this fiery form, you are immune to all damage. On the following round, you reform in any unoccupied space within 1 mile of where you discorporated. When you reform, you have 1 hit point. Each item you were wearing or carrying when discorporated also reforms with you.
darkSunNotes: The crimson fire of discorporation streaks across the Athasian sky like a falling star in reverse. Egoists who master this power have cheated death in the most spectacular fashion possible—dissolving into flame at the moment of destruction and reforming miles away, battered but alive. It is said that Kalak's templars still hunt those who escape their grasp in this manner.

name: Leech Field
description: You are surrounded by a crackling field of bioenergy that siphons power from psionic effects targeted at you, giving you power points in return. Any power that is successfully manifested on you (whether it affects you or not) causes you to gain a number of power points equal to the level of the power manifested (maximum up to your normal maximum power point total). You gain the power points even if the power is one you are immune to or if you succeed on your saving throw.
darkSunNotes: In a world saturated with psionic energy, the leech field turns an egoist into a psychic parasite. Every hostile manifestation directed at them feeds the very power that sustains them. Sorcerer-king Oronis of Kurn is rumored to have taught variants of this power to his secret students, creating psionicists who grow stronger with every attack.

name: Psionic Revivify
description: You can restore life to a recently deceased creature. The dead creature can have been dead for no longer than 1 round before you manifest this power. This power functions like the raise dead spell, except that the raised creature receives no level loss, no Constitution loss, and no loss of powers. The creature is restored to full hit points. You must pay 200 XP to manifest this power.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the Gray hungrily claims the spirits of the dead, psionic revivify must work quickly—before the Gray's pull becomes irresistible. The manifester literally wrenches a departing spirit back from the boundary between life and death, an act that costs a piece of the psionicist's own life force. It is one of the few powers that can cheat Athas's merciless cycle of death.

name: Psychofeedback
description: You can readjust your body to boost one physical ability score at the expense of one or more other physical ability scores. The bonus must be drawn from one or more other physical ability scores. You can boost your Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution at the expense of one or both of the other two ability scores. The adjusted scores are treated as if they were your natural scores for the duration of the power.
darkSunNotes: Psychofeedback represents the ultimate mastery over one's own flesh—redistributing the body's physical essence like water between vessels. Athasian gladiators use it to become impossibly strong for a critical bout, or blazingly fast for an escape, accepting the trade-offs that come with pushing one attribute beyond its natural limits.

name: Restore Extremity
description: You restore a severed extremity to a creature that has lost a digit, hand, arm, leg, or other appendage. This power does not restore life to a dead creature, but it does restore an extremity to a living creature that has lost one.
darkSunNotes: Athasian punishments are brutal—templars routinely sever hands for theft and tongues for treason. An egoist who can regrow a lost limb offers hope to the maimed and the mutilated, making them invaluable to gladiators crippled in the arena and slaves whose masters have disfigured them as warnings.

name: Breath of the Black Dragon
description: Your mouth spews forth vitriolic acid that deals 11d6 points of acid damage to any targets in the area.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by 1d6 points.
darkSunNotes: True dragons are nearly mythical on Athas—only the Dragon of Tyr was widely known, and its shadow still haunts the Tablelands. A psychic warrior who vomits a cone of sizzling acid invokes that primal terror, sending enemies scrambling as the very air hisses and stone dissolves.

name: Form of Doom
description: You wreath yourself in a frightening quasi-real image of a horrid creature, becoming a fearsome, 5-foot-radius, 10-foot-tall monstrous figure. You gain a +4 bonus to Strength, damage reduction 5/-, and a fear aura. Enemies within 30 feet who view you must make a Will save or be shaken for 5d6 rounds. You gain two claw attacks (2d8 damage each) and a bite attack (4d8 damage). You can make a full attack each round.
augments: If you spend 4 additional power points, this power's duration increases to 1 min./level. If you spend 6 additional power points, the power's fear aura increases to a 60-foot range.
darkSunNotes: The form of doom transforms a warrior into something from the nightmares of the Tablelands—a towering abomination that echoes the monstrous forms of the Pristine Tower's creations. In the arenas of Raam, where chaos reigns, a gladiator who assumes this form can rout an entire opposing team through sheer terror alone.

name: Fuse Flesh
savingThrow: Fortitude negates and Fortitude partial; see text
description: You cause the target's flesh to ripple, grow together, and fuse into a nearlyite mass. The target is unable to move, attack, or use any abilities that require hands, arms, or similar limbs. The target can still breathe and speak. If the target fails its initial Fortitude save, it must make a second Fortitude save each round. If it fails this second save, the target permanently loses 1d6 points of Dexterity. A successful second save on any round ends the Dexterity loss but not the immobility.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: This horrifying power is whispered of in the slave markets of Nibenay—a punishment reserved for the most defiant. The victim's limbs melt together into a quivering lump of living flesh, fully aware but utterly helpless. Templar inquisitors have been known to use fuse flesh as a threat alone, the mere description enough to break a prisoner's will.

name: Restoration, Psionic
description: As the restoration spell. This power cures all temporary ability damage, eliminates any fatigue or exhaustion suffered by the target, and removes all negative levels the target has gained. It also eliminates all magical and psionic effects reducing one of the subject's ability scores.
darkSunNotes: True restoration is miraculous on Athas, where elemental clerics guard their healing jealously and the dying world offers no divine mercy. An egoist who can fully restore a broken body commands enormous respect—and enormous fees. Merchant houses pay fortunes for the services of such psionicists, and sorcerer-kings keep them close.

name: Suspend Life
description: You can place yourself into a state of suspended animation. While in this state, you are unconscious and immune to all harm, including physical damage, aging, and death effects. You can set a mental timer to awaken after a set period, or you can remain in suspension indefinitely until awakened by an external stimulus you designate when manifesting the power.
darkSunNotes: Legends speak of ancient psionicists who suspended themselves beneath the sands before the Cleansing Wars, waiting centuries for a time when Athas might be restored. Some ruins contain sealed chambers with figures in perfect repose—egoists who chose to sleep through the dying of their world, awaiting a trigger that may never come.

name: Energy Conversion
description: Your body assimilates energy of one type (cold, electricity, fire, or sonic) and converts it, allowing you to use that energy as a touch attack dealing the same type of energy damage. You gain immunity to the type of energy you specify when you manifest this power. Each round, you can make a touch attack that deals 4d6 points of energy damage of that type (assuming you have absorbed energy, which you are assumed to have if you are in combat). You also have the option to release the energy as a ray within close range instead of a touch attack.
darkSunNotes: The most powerful egoists of Athas can absorb a defiler's fireball and hurl the stolen energy back as a lance of concentrated flame. This power turns the manifester into a living capacitor, a terrifying prospect for any who rely on energy attacks—and a potent counter to the elemental magic that pervades the dying world.

name: Fission
description: You split into two identical copies of yourself, each with the same powers, feats, and equipment. Your duplicate appears in an adjacent space.
