You and your duplicate each have half your current hit points and power points (round down). Your duplicate has all your active powers. Equipment that is duplicated disappears when the power ends. Your duplicate acts on your turn, and you both share the same initiative count. You retain your identity and control your own actions; the duplicate acts independently but cooperatively. When the power ends, one of you—your choice—disappears, and the remaining one retains the remaining hit points and power points.
darkSunNotes: Fission is the pinnacle of egoist self-mastery—literally splitting oneself into two beings. On Athas, where a single psionicist might face an entire templar death squad, the ability to become two fighters, two manifesters, two targets, can turn an ambush into a fair fight. The philosophical implications disturb many: which half is the real you?

name: Oak Body
description: Your body transforms into living oak, granting you several abilities. You gain damage reduction 10/slashing, resistance to fire 10, immunity to ability damage, blindness, deafness, disease, drowning, poison, stunning, and all powers or effects that affect physiology or respiration (since you have no physiology or respiration while this power is in effect). You gain a +4 enhancement bonus to Strength, but take a -2 penalty to Dexterity, and your speed is reduced to half normal. You can still manifest powers while in oak body form.
darkSunNotes: Trees are sacred on Athas—the few great agafari trees that remain are guarded by druids willing to kill. To become living wood is to embody the resilience of a dying world's last forests. Psychic warriors who manifest oak body in the Forest Ridge are said to commune with the ancient trees themselves, their wooden forms blending seamlessly with the canopy.

name: Fusion
target: You and one touched willing creature of your type and your size or smaller
description: You and another willing, corporeal, living creature of the same or smaller size fuse into one being. As the manifester, you control the actions of the fused being. The fused being has your current hit points plus the other creature's current hit points, knows all powers both creatures know, has the sum of both creatures' power points, and has all feats, racial abilities, and class features pooled. For each ability score, the fused being uses the higher score. When the power ends, damage is split evenly. XP Cost: 50 XP.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where survival demands cooperation, fusion represents the ultimate trust between two beings. Thri-kreen clutch-mates have been known to fuse in battle, creating a four-armed psionic monstrosity that combines the physical prowess of one with the mental might of another. The sorcerer-kings fear this power, for it allows two lesser minds to become one greater than either.

name: Iron Body, Psionic
description: As the iron body spell. Your body transforms into living iron, granting you several powerful resistances and abilities. You gain damage reduction 15/adamantine, immunity to blindness, critical hits, ability score damage, deafness, disease, drowning, electricity, poison, stunning, and all powers, spells, or attacks that affect your physiology or respiration. You gain a +6 enhancement bonus to Strength, but take a -6 penalty to Dexterity, and your speed is reduced to half normal. You cannot drink potions, and arcane spell failure applies.
darkSunNotes: Iron is the rarest and most precious metal on Athas—to become iron is to become something almost mythical. A psychic warrior transformed into living metal is virtually indestructible by conventional Athasian weapons, as obsidian blades and bone spears shatter uselessly against a body harder than anything found in the Tablelands.

name: Shadow Body
description: Your body and all your equipment are subsumed by your shadow, changing you into a living shadow. You are visible as an unattached shadow in areas of normal light. In areas of darkness or dim illumination, you gain total concealment. You gain damage reduction 10/magic, are immune to extra damage from critical hits, and gain a +10 competence bonus on Hide checks. You can pass through solid objects, but not force effects. While in shadow form, you cannot attack or manifest powers. You can end the power at any time as a free action.
darkSunNotes: To become shadow on Athas is to brush against the Black, the plane of ultimate darkness that lurks beyond the Gray. Shadow body transforms the manifester into something neither alive nor dead—a flat, silent presence that slips beneath doors, through cracks in walls, and past the most vigilant templar guards. The sorcerer-kings' shadow spies are rumored to use this power extensively.

name: True Metabolism
description: You are filled with psionic energy that accelerates your natural healing to incredible levels. You heal 10 hit points of damage per round. This healing is in addition to any other healing you might receive. You are immune to poison while this power is in effect, and any poisons currently in your system are neutralized.
darkSunNotes: True metabolism transforms a psychic warrior into something almost unkillable—a regenerating juggernaut that heals grievous wounds in seconds. On Athas, where healers are few and the next threat is always moments away, this power represents the apex of the body's psionic potential. Warriors with true metabolism have been known to fight through injuries that would fell a giant.

name: Affinity Field
description: You create an affinity feedback loop with all creatures within the area. While the duration lasts, affected creatures take all damage (including ability damage) as you do and heal all wounds as you do. Hit points gained or lost persist after this power ends. Creatures in range are also subject to magical and psionic effects of 3rd level or lower. Creatures that have an affinity to you gain a saving throw against each new power transferred through the affinity field as if the power were manifested upon them normally. All magical and psionic effects transferred to subjects fade at the end of this power's duration, although instantaneous effects remain. If you suddenly become immune to a particular effect or power, the effect or power to which you are immune cannot be transferred to creatures that have affinity to you.
darkSunNotes: The sorcerer-kings of Athas would kill to suppress knowledge of this power—an egoist who links an entire war party's fate to their own becomes either an unstoppable force or a single point of catastrophic failure. In the gladiatorial arenas, paired fighters sometimes use this bond to share the healing of a single psion, turning two warriors into one resilient unit.

name: Assimilate
description: Your pointing finger turns black as obsidian. A creature touched by you is partially assimilated into your form and takes 20d6 points of damage. Any creature reduced to 0 or fewer hit points by this power is killed, entirely assimilated into your form, leaving behind only a trace of fine dust. An assimilated creature's equipment is unaffected. A creature that is partially assimilated grants you temporary hit points equal to half the damage you dealt for 1 hour. A creature that is completely assimilated grants you temporary hit points equal to the damage you dealt and a +4 bonus to each of your ability scores for 1 hour. If the assimilated creature knows psionic powers, you gain knowledge of one of its powers for 1 hour. You gain some semblance of a creature you completely assimilate for 1 hour, granting you a +10 bonus on Disguise checks made to appear as that creature during that time.
darkSunNotes: Defiler magic drains the life from plants; assimilate drains the life from flesh itself. The Dragon of Tyr was said to have used this power to consume entire villages during the Cleansing Wars, absorbing their knowledge and strength into its ever-growing form. On Athas, where every drop of moisture and every morsel of power is hoarded, assimilate represents the ultimate act of consumption.

name: Astral Traveler
description: This power allows you or a creature you touch to participate in an astral caravan created through use of the astral caravan power. While participating in a journey, you must hold the hand of both your fellow passenger ahead of you (or the caravan leader) and your fellow passenger behind you (unless you are the last in line). If you or any passenger breaks the hand-to-hand link for 2 consecutive rounds, the impetus through the Astral Plane provided by astral caravan fails. All those who are part of the caravan who are capable of performing purely mental actions, such as manifesting a power, may do so while maintaining contact. Each traveler is connected to the Material Plane by an insubstantial silvery cord. The last creature in the line has one hand free and can wield a weapon.
darkSunNotes: For the desperate refugees of Athas, the silvery cord that binds an astral traveler to the Material Plane is a lifeline back to a world of baking sun and endless thirst. Thri-kreen clutch-mates sometimes form astral chains dozens strong, their chitinous hands interlocking with eerie precision. Halfling witch-doctors in the Forest Ridge have been known to sever the silvery cords of unwelcome travelers, stranding them forever in the Gray.

name: Burst
description: This power increases your land speed by 10 feet. This adjustment counts as an enhancement bonus to speed. You can manifest this power with an instant thought, quickly enough to gain the benefit of the power on your turn before you move. Manifesting this power is a swift action. You cannot manifest this power when it isn't your turn.
darkSunNotes: In the gladiatorial pits of Draj and Gulg, the difference between life and death is often measured in a single desperate stride. Psychic warriors learn burst as one of their first survival tools — a sharp crack of displaced air as the manifester lunges clear of a charging mekillot or ducks beneath a half-giant's obsidian blade. Elven traders use it to outpace pursuit across the salt flats, their feet barely kissing the scorching ground.

name: Deceleration
description: You warp space around an individual, hindering the subject's ability to move. The subject's speed (in any movement mode it possesses) is halved. A subsequent manifestation of deceleration on the subject does not further decrease its speed.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power can affect a target one size category larger.
darkSunNotes: Nomad scouts in the wastes use deceleration to hobble fleeing prey — whether beast or humanoid. In the brutal arithmetic of Athasian survival, slowing a pursuer by even half is often the difference between reaching an oasis and dying of thirst. Mul gladiators have learned to recognize the telltale shimmer of warped space and will sometimes deliberately trigger the effect on a charging opponent, turning their momentum into a stumbling crawl across the arena floor.

name: Dimensional Door
description: The psionicist opens a man-sized portal leading to the edge of another dimension, which acts as a lightning-quick transit system. A second identical portal appears at a chosen point within range. Anyone stepping into either portal immediately exits through the other. Travel through the portal is disorienting; a traveler is dazed and cannot attack or move for one round afterward. Shoving only an arm through causes intense pain. Poking a head through requires a system shock roll; failure means losing 50% of current hit points and passing out. Inanimate objects pass through unaffected, and ranged attacks can be made through portals at a -4 penalty. The range can be extended: 75 yards (-2 penalty), 100 yards (-5), 150 yards (-8), 200 yards (-12).
augments: Maintenance cost of 2 PSPs per round sustains both portals. Extended range available at power score penalties.
darkSunNotes: In the gladiatorial arenas of Athas, dimensional door is a feared tactic—a psionicist can redirect attacks or reposition allies with startling speed.

name: Dissipating Touch
description: Your mere touch can disperse the surface material of a foe or object, sending a tiny portion of it far away. This effect is disruptive; thus, your successful melee touch attack deals 1d6 points of damage.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by 1d6 points.
darkSunNotes: Dissipating touch scatters matter across dimensional boundaries, leaving wounds that look like erosion rather than violence. Psychic warriors of the wastes use it to silently dispatch sentries, their victims crumbling like sun-bleached bone. Elven raiders of the Tablelands prize this power for its subtlety — a touch that leaves nothing but dust and confusion.

name: Dream Travel
description: The psionicist travels by dreaming, journeying in sleep and awakening at his destination. The psionicist must be asleep to use this power. At the start of the journey, a power check determines success; failure causes the traveler to fall short by 10% per point the roll exceeded the power score. The cost is 1 PSP per 25 miles traveled. Dream journeys take approximately eight hours regardless of success. The psionicist can bring sleeping passengers, with his power score reduced by one per passenger and the PSP cost paid individually for each. Intelligent animals can be included, but normal animals require a separate check at half the power score. If the traveler is killed in the dream, he wakes at his starting location. Upon arrival, the travelers' bodies fade from their sleeping location and materialize at the destination.
augments: Cost scales at 1 PSP per 25 miles. Each passenger reduces power score by 1 and requires full PSP cost.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, dream travelers sometimes encounter the psionic echoes of the Green Age in their nocturnal journeys—visions of lush forests and blue seas that have long since turned to dust and silt.

name: Grease, Psionic
description: As the grease spell, except as noted here. You cover a solid surface with a layer of psychoportive slippery energy, or make an item slippery. Creatures in the area must succeed on a Balance check (DC 10) or fall prone. Those entering the area must also make Balance checks. A creature that fails its save and falls can't get up until it leaves the greased area.
darkSunNotes: Psionic grease manifests as a shimmering, oily distortion in the air above sandy ground — a teleportive slick that sends limbs sliding in unexpected directions. In the cramped streets of Athasian cities, it turns narrow alleys into impassable traps. Gladiators have learned to lure heavily-armored opponents onto greased arena floors, where even a half-giant can be brought crashing down.

name: Hustle
description: You gain an additional move action in the current round. Taking a full round's worth of attacks and then using this power to move away from your foe does provoke attacks of opportunity. You can manifest this power with an instant thought, quickly enough to gain the benefit of the power before you move. Manifesting the power is a swift action. You cannot manifest this power when it isn't your turn.
darkSunNotes: Every psychic warrior on Athas knows hustle — it is perhaps the single most vital combat technique in a world where hesitation means death. In the arenas, the crowd gasps as a warrior delivers a flurry of obsidian-edged strikes and then darts away before the opponent can retaliate. Elven runners use it to maintain their legendary pace across the desert, covering distances that would kill lesser races. It is said that the mul gladiator Rikus could hustle so quickly that he appeared to be in two places at once.

name: Skate
target: You or one willing creature or one unattended object (total weight up to 100 lb./level); see text
description: You, another willing creature, or an unattended object can slide along solid ground as if on smooth ice. If you manifest skate on yourself or another creature, the subject retains equilibrium by mental desire alone, allowing her to gracefully skate along the ground, turn, or stop suddenly as desired. The skater's land speed increases by 15 feet (treated as an enhancement bonus). As with any effect that increases speed, this power affects the subject's maximum jumping distance. The subject can skate up or down any incline or decline she could normally walk upon without mishap, though skating up an incline reduces speed to normal, while skating down a decline increases speed by an additional 15 feet (treated as a circumstance bonus). If you manifest skate on an object, treat the object as having only one-tenth of its normal weight for the purpose of dragging it along the ground.
darkSunNotes: Skate transforms the endless salt flats and sandy wastes of Athas into highways. Nomad couriers glide across the blinding white surface of the Great Ivory Plain at terrifying speed, delivering messages between city-states in a fraction of the time it takes a kanks caravan. Halfling scouts in the Forest Ridge use skate to zip along the massive branches of the ancient trees, their bare feet never quite touching bark. The power is also invaluable for dragging heavy loads of scavenged metal — the rarest commodity on Athas — across the desert.

name: Teleport Trigger
description: The psionicist programs a reflexive teleport that activates when specific conditions are met, instantly transporting him to a predetermined safe location. After manifesting this power, the psionicist specifies both the destination and the triggering conditions (such as being reduced to 10 or fewer hit points, seeing a specific creature, or being attacked by a particular spell). The teleport only triggers if the psionicist is aware the conditions have been met. When triggered, the psionicist must have enough PSPs remaining to pay the normal teleport cost for the distance traveled, and must succeed on a teleport power check with standard distance penalties. No PSPs are spent when setting the trigger, but a maintenance cost is paid to keep it active.
augments: Maintenance cost of 2 PSPs per hour keeps the trigger active. When triggered, normal teleport PSP costs apply based on distance.
darkSunNotes: Many Athasian psionicists maintain a teleport trigger at all times, given the constant dangers of the desert world. A wise psionicist always has an escape route planned.

name: Time Hop
target: One Medium or smaller creature, or one object weighing 300 lb. or less
description: The subject of the power hops forward in time 1 round for every manifester level you have. In effect, the subject seems to disappear in a shimmer of silver energy, then reappear after the duration of this power expires. The subject reappears in exactly the same orientation and condition as before. From the subject's point of view, no time has passed at all. In each round of the power's duration, on what would have been the subject's turn, it can attempt a DC 15 Wisdom check. Success allows the subject to return. The subject can act normally on its next turn after this power ends. If the space from which the subject departed is occupied upon return, the subject appears in the closest unoccupied space.
augments: You can augment this power in one or both of the following ways. 1. For every 2 additional power points you spend, you can affect a creature of one size category larger, or double the weight of an object to be affected. 2. For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power can affect an additional target. Any additional target cannot be more than 15 feet from another target of the power.
darkSunNotes: Time hop is a favored technique of Athasian nomads dealing with overwhelming threats. When a tembo charges or a sorcerer-king's army approaches, the simplest solution is to remove the problem from the timeline entirely. Gladiators have been time-hopped mid-swing, reappearing rounds later to find their opponent has already been declared the victor. The technique is considered dishonorable in formal arena combat but devastatingly effective in the wild, where honor is a luxury the dead cannot afford.

name: Time/Space Anchor
description: This power protects the psionicist against unwanted teleportation. While the anchor is active, the psionicist cannot be teleported against his will unless the would-be teleporter wins a psychic contest. All creatures and objects within a 3-yard radius are also protected, though the psionicist can choose which living creatures benefit from the effect. Items are protected automatically. This power cannot prevent a creature from teleporting itself away voluntarily; it only blocks teleportation imposed from an outside source.
augments: Maintenance cost of 1 PSP per round. Each additional creature protected beyond the psionicist costs 1 extra PSP per round.
darkSunNotes: Athasian defilers and psionicists alike fear forced teleportation into the deadly wastes. A time/space anchor is considered essential preparation before any confrontation with a powerful psychoportive opponent.

name: Astral Projection
description: The psionicist projects an astral body into the astral plane, leaving his physical body behind. A translucent silvery cord connects the astral body to the physical body, stretching 10 feet visibly before becoming invisible and intangible. If the cord is severed, both bodies die. The cord can usually only be severed by the psychic wind at its most powerful or by githyanki silver swords. When the traveler reaches a destination on another plane, a temporary physical body is formed there, still connected by the cord. If either the astral body or temporary body is killed, the cord returns to the real body. The traveler cannot form a second body on the same plane as his real body; he can only observe in astral form without interacting physically. Most magical items lose their properties temporarily while on the astral plane.
augments: Maintenance cost of 2 PSPs per hour sustains the projection.
darkSunNotes: Astral projection on Athas sends the traveler into the Gray, the ashen realm of the dead. Athasian psionicists who project astrally risk encounters with the undead spirits that haunt this bleak plane.

name: Blink
description: The psionicist initiates a series of random, short-range teleportations that make him extremely difficult to hit in combat. Each round, the psionicist automatically blinks once at a random initiative point. Opponents must have a better initiative than the blink time to strike at the psionicist. Each blink carries the psionicist 3d10 feet in a random direction, but never into solid objects or danger. The psionicist may attempt to strike before he blinks or hold his action. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist automatically blinks away before enemies can strike.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/round to continue blinking.
darkSunNotes: In the chaotic melee of Athasian arena combat, a blinking psionicist is an infuriating opponent—appearing and disappearing unpredictably while delivering strikes from unexpected angles.

name: Dimension Blade
description: The psionicist makes a hand-held weapon infinitely sharp by reducing it to two dimensions. Almost any weapon can be altered, except jointed, chain, or rope weapons like whips or flails. While active, the weapon ignores any armor worn by the victim—only Dexterity and magical protection apply. The blade also gains +2 to hit and +2 to damage. The dimension blade can sever inanimate objects: any object struck must make an item saving throw vs. disintegration or be sliced in half. On an exceptional manifestation, the blade scores double damage in addition to all other benefits.
augments: Maintain for 5 PP/round to keep the dimensional edge.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where bone and obsidian weapons are common and metal rare, a dimension blade turns even a crude stone axe into a weapon that can slice through the finest armor as if it weren't there.

name: Dimension Swap
target: You and one ally in range, or any two allies in range; see text
description: You instantly swap positions between your current position and that of a designated ally in range. Alternatively, you can swap the positions of any two allies in range. This power affects creatures of Large or smaller size. You can bring along objects, but not other creatures. A psychic warrior can manifest this power to swap positions with an ally, but not to swap the positions of two allies.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power can affect a target one size category larger.
darkSunNotes: On the battlefield, dimension swap transforms Athasian psionic warfare into a deadly chess game. Paired gladiators in the arenas of Urik practice coordinated swaps to confuse opponents — a mul bruiser suddenly replaces a nimble halfling, catching the foe off guard with a bone club to the skull. Thri-kreen hunting packs use it to rotate wounded members to the rear while fresh fighters take the front, a tactic that has baffled many a human commander.

name: Dimension Walk
description: The psionicist travels from place to place in his own dimension by piercing other dimensions at right angles. Unlike dimensional door, this power is not physically traumatizing and has greater range, but only the psionicist can travel and there is a risk of getting lost. The traveler enters a gray, featureless realm and moves at a speed of 7 leagues (21 miles) per turn. A Wisdom check is required every turn; if all checks succeed, the traveler arrives at his destination. If any check fails, the traveler strays off course by several miles. Dimension walking is otherwise completely safe, as beings in the crossed dimensions cannot interact with the traveler. The character can carry as much as he can hold, but bags of holding and dimensional storage devices spill their contents immediately.
augments: Maintenance cost of 4 PSPs per turn sustains the walk through the gray realm.
darkSunNotes: Athasian dimension walkers describe the gray realm as indistinguishable from the oppressive emptiness of the Gray itself, leading some scholars to theorize Athas's planar boundaries have thinned dangerously.

name: Dimensional Screen
description: The psionicist wraps a dimensional door around his body, creating a shimmering protective cylinder. Any missile or hand-held weapon that strikes the screen is instantly transported to a corresponding point on the other side, passing through harmlessly. The protection extends to creatures attacking with tooth, claw, or grappling—they pass through the screen and emerge on the opposite side. Most energy forms like lightning bolts or flames are also diverted. However, the psionicist cannot pierce his own screen with any weapon or energy, and the screen fails if an object larger than the screen contacts it. Magical spells that project no energy (charm, sleep, cloudkill) and psionic powers can penetrate the screen.
augments: Maintain for 6 PP/round to keep the screen active.
darkSunNotes: The dimensional screen makes its user nearly untouchable in physical combat—a terrifying sight in the gladiatorial arenas of Athas, where audiences watch weapons pass harmlessly through the psionicist's body.

name: Duo-Dimension
description: The psionicist alters her dimensions to have height and width but no depth, becoming a two-dimensional image. Her third dimension is transferred to another plane. When seen from the side, she simply does not exist—completely invisible and immune to any attacks from that direction. She can fit through the thinnest cracks by edging sideways. The psionicist can move and attack normally but can only be attacked by opponents in front of or behind her. While two-dimensional, she suffers double damage from any successful attack. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist's weapon is affected by the dimension blade power at no additional cost.
augments: Maintain for 4 PP/round to remain two-dimensional.
darkSunNotes: In the narrow alleyways of Athasian cities, a duo-dimensional psionicist can slip through cracks in walls and evade pursuit by turning sideways and simply ceasing to exist from the pursuers' perspective.

name: Knock, Psionic
target: One door, box, or chest with an area of up to 10 sq. ft./level
description: As the knock spell, except as noted here. The power opens stuck, barred, locked, held, or psionically sealed doors. It opens secret doors, as well as locked or trick-opening boxes or chests. It also loosens welds, shackles, or chains.
darkSunNotes: Every thief in the Warrens of Tyr dreams of manifesting psionic knock — a power that opens any lock without tools, noise, or evidence. In a world where iron is precious and most locks are crude ceramic or bone mechanisms, the power is devastatingly effective. Templar storehouses and merchant vaults use psionic locks specifically to counter this power, creating an arms race between those who seal and those who open.

name: Levitate, Psionic
range: Personal or close (25 ft. + 5 ft./2 levels)
target: You or one willing creature or one object (total weight up to 100 lb./level)
description: As the levitate spell, except as noted here. You can mentally direct the recipient to move up or down at a speed of 20 feet per round. You cannot move the recipient horizontally, but the recipient could clamber along the face of a cliff, for example, or push against a ceiling to move laterally.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, levitation is a common sight in the markets and workshops of the city-states, where psionic laborers lift heavy stone blocks and obsidian slabs that no beast of burden could manage. The templars of Hamanu use levitation to inspect the walls of Urik from above, searching for cracks or sabotage. In the wastes, nomads levitate themselves above the burning sand to rest, hovering just high enough to escape the ground-level heat that can cook a man alive.

name: Phase
description: The psionicist shifts his body's molecules into a different frequency of motion, becoming ghostly and transparent. He can walk through solid matter, and enemies' weapons pass right through him—no physical force or energy can harm him. He can be affected by other phased objects or creatures. A phased character is not affected by gravity but retains falling momentum. Movement rates: normal speed on solid ground, half speed on water or quicksand, quarter speed through solid matter, 10 feet per round vertically. If phasing fails while inside solid matter, the psionicist takes 3d10 damage, falls into a coma for 1-6 hours, and becomes trapped in the Ethereal Plane. Psionic attacks against a phased mind suffer -4, and the psionicist saves at +2 vs. mind-affecting effects, but these same penalties apply to the psionicist's own attacks.
augments: Maintain for 6 PP/round to remain phased.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where thick stone walls and locked gates are common defenses against both raiders and sandstorms, a phasing psionicist can bypass any physical barrier—making them invaluable infiltrators and utterly terrifying to guards.

name: Phase Object
description: The psionicist extends her phasing powers to an object. A boulder blocking a tunnel can be phased to allow passage, an enemy's sword can be rendered harmless, or a building can be forced to collapse by phasing its supports. Cost scales at 1 PSP per 10 pounds, with a minimum of 10 PSPs. Phasing an object does not harm it. A phased object cannot be affected by physical force but can be affected by other phased objects. The psionicist can phase her own sword to harm another phased character. Phased objects retain their momentum. If the object is worn or carried by an unwilling subject, the victim may save to avoid the effect. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist may choose to disintegrate the phased item.
augments: Cost scales at 1 PP per 10 pounds. Maintain for 5 PP/round.
darkSunNotes: Athasian siege warfare takes on a new dimension when a psionicist can phase the supports of a watchtower, causing it to collapse, or render an enemy champion's prized obsidian sword intangible mid-swing.

name: Psionic Lock
target: Door, chest, or portal touched, up to 30 sq. ft./level in size
description: As the arcane lock spell, except as noted here. A psionic lock seals a door, chest, or portal. The sealed portal can be opened only by the manifester. Psionic knock can temporarily suppress the lock. A psionic lock increases the DC to break open or force the sealed item by 10.
darkSunNotes: In a world without abundant metal for fine locksmithing, psionic lock serves as the premier security measure for anyone who can afford a psionicist's services. The merchant houses seal their most valuable cargo with psionic locks, while templar armories use them to protect their hoarded iron and obsidian weapons. Psionic locks also seal the tomb-vaults of ancient Athasian kings, protecting treasures that have gone undisturbed for millennia.

name: Shadow Walk
description: The psionicist uses the Demiplane of Shadow to instantly cross between two shadows. The psionicist steps into any shadow larger than his own body and teleports to any other shadow within range. If he can see the destination shadow, the cost is 9 PSPs; if teleporting blindly, the cost doubles to 18 PSPs. All equipment and carried items are transported. The power works best in twilight conditions and is usually ineffective in bright daylight or very dark nights. The psionicist may use his own shadow. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist may travel to any shadow within two miles.
darkSunNotes: The long shadows cast by Athas's crimson sun provide ample stepping-stones for a shadow-walking psionicist. At dawn and dusk, when shadows stretch for dozens of yards, the power's effective range increases dramatically.

name: Spatial Distortion
description: The psionicist can affect the dimensions of an area, making it larger or smaller than it appears. The base area is a 20-foot cube; additional 20-foot cubes cost 4 PSPs each, with maintenance at half the initial cost. The area can be shaped to surroundings (e.g., a 10-foot passage could affect an 80-foot length). Within the area, distances can be doubled or halved. A chasm can be made crossable or widened for defense, a friend's fall shortened, or corridors stretched to delay pursuers. Objects must be wholly in or out of the area; the transition is unnoticeable. On an exceptional manifestation, distances can be altered from 25% to 400% normal.
augments: Each additional 20-ft. cube costs 4 PP. Maintenance is half initial cost per round.
darkSunNotes: In the labyrinthine Under-Tyr and the narrow canyons of the Ringing Mountains, spatial distortion can turn a short passage into a marathon or compress a vast distance to a single step.

name: Teleport Lock
description: The psionicist prevents another creature within range from teleporting. All forms of teleportation, both magical and psionic, are blocked. A creature attempting to teleport while locked must win a psychic contest against the psionicist. The power also prevents magical or psionic blinking and plane shifting, and blocks shadow walking. It does not prevent travel by dimensional doors or dimension walk. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist automatically wins any psychic contests with this power.
augments: Maintain for 2 PP/round to keep the lock active.
darkSunNotes: The Order uses teleport lock to prevent fugitives from escaping psionic justice. In a world where powerful psionicists can teleport vast distances, this power is essential for anyone trying to hold a mindbender accountable.

name: Time Shift
description: The psionicist travels up to three rounds into the future, observing a frozen tableau of events as they will be when that moment arrives. While time-shifted, the psionicist exists in a separate reality—invisible and undetectable, unable to affect or be affected by anything. Even two time-shifted characters cannot perceive each other. The psionicist can move freely and position himself for advantage. In combat, a time-shifted psionicist who positions for an attack gains a +4 bonus to his attack roll when time catches up. This power cannot break existing psionic contact or tangents. The cost and difficulty scale with duration: 1 round (3 PSPs, no penalty), 2 rounds (6 PSPs, -2 penalty), 3 rounds (12 PSPs, -6 penalty).
augments: Scaling cost by duration: 1 round = 3 PSPs, 2 rounds = 6 PSPs, 3 rounds = 12 PSPs, with increasing power score penalties.
darkSunNotes: Time shifting is whispered to be a fragment of the ancient psionic arts practiced during the Green Age, when Athasian psionicists could reportedly manipulate time on a far grander scale.

name: Wall Walker
description: You can walk on walls and ceilings as if they were floors. Your movement rate is the same as your normal land speed. You can switch from floor to wall to ceiling and back again as part of your movement. You retain your Dexterity bonus to AC while wall walking, and you can fight normally while doing so.
darkSunNotes: In the cramped tunnels beneath the city-states and the narrow canyons of the Ringing Mountains, wall walker transforms a psychic warrior into a three-dimensional threat. Thri-kreen psychic warriors take to this power with particular ease, their multiple limbs finding purchase on vertical surfaces as naturally as on flat ground. In the arenas of Draj, wall-walking gladiators have been known to charge across the arena walls to attack opponents from above, raining blows down from impossible angles while the crowd howls in delight.

name: Astral Caravan
description: You lead a caravan into the Astral Plane, leaving the Material Plane behind. You can travel astrally to any other plane, provided you know your way. You can bring other willing creatures who have each manifested astral traveler and are linked hand to hand with you at the time of manifestation. These travelers are dependent upon you and must accompany you at all times. If something happens to you that causes you to break hand-to-hand contact, your companions are stranded wherever you leave them. The power lasts while you maintain formation, until you reach your destination, you desire to end the power, you or anyone breaks the chain for 2 consecutive rounds, or the power is terminated by outside means. While traveling, you appear to move at 30 feet. For each 24 hours you travel, make a Knowledge (the planes) check (average DC 20). You must succeed on six checks within 12 days to arrive at your destination, appearing within 10-1,000 miles of your intended location.
augments: If you spend 2 additional power points, this power weaves a quasi-real filmy membrane around yourself and all those adjacent in the caravan (hand-linking is not required). You remain visible within the translucent enclosure. Attacks through the enclosure have a 25% miss chance. Your apparent speed becomes 40 feet, and you can make a Knowledge (the planes) check once every 12 hours.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, the Astral Plane is a harrowing wasteland, choked with the psychic residue of millennia of defiler magic and the oppressive presence of the Gray. Nomads who lead astral caravans are among the most valued guides in the Tablelands, for they can bypass the vast, impassable Sea of Silt and the Ringing Mountains entirely. The sorcerer-kings are said to maintain their own astral highways, guarded by undead templar sentinels who patrol the silvery void.

name: Dimension Slide
description: You instantly transfer yourself from your current location to any other spot within range to which you have line of sight. You can bring along possessions that amount to as much as a medium load, including living creatures that weigh as much as 20 pounds. Movement caused by the use of dimension slide does not provoke attacks of opportunity. If you somehow attempt to transfer yourself to a location occupied by a solid body or a location you can't see, the power simply fails to function.
augments: If you spend 4 additional power points, you can manifest this power as a move action.
darkSunNotes: Psychic warriors prize dimension slide above almost any other combat technique. In the gladiatorial arenas, a fighter who can blink across the sand to flank an opponent or dodge a lethal blow commands higher prices and greater fame. The mul champion Rikus was rumored to have mastered a variant that let him slide behind a charging crodlu mid-charge, striking the beast's unprotected flank before it could turn.

name: Pocket Dimension
description: The psionicist creates a small storage area in extradimensional space—a 5-foot cube accessed through a glowing 2-foot square doorway that follows the psionicist. Objects stored inside have no weight or bulk; the psionicist could carry 1,000 pounds in his belt pouch. The access can be resized from fully closed to 5 feet square at will. When the psionicist stops maintaining the power, the contents appear at the access location and drop to the ground. Living creatures inside exhaust the air in about 15 minutes, but the access can be left partially open for ventilation. The psionicist cannot enter his own pocket dimension but can reach in to retrieve items. On an exceptional manifestation, the pocket dimension can be up to 10 feet on a side.
augments: Maintain for 3 PP/turn to keep the pocket dimension accessible.
darkSunNotes: On trade-dependent Athas, a pocket dimension is priceless for smuggling contraband past templar checkpoints or carrying water reserves that would otherwise be impossible to transport across the desert.

name: Probability Travel
description: The psionicist physically traverses the astral plane as if in astral form, bringing his physical body along. Unlike magical astral travel, this power creates no silvery cord, so the traveler cannot die from a severed cord. Travel through the astral plane proceeds at 10 yards per minute per point of Intelligence. After 1d6+6 hours of travel, the traveler reaches the desired color pool, with a 75% chance it leads to the exact destination. The psionicist can bring willing passengers along at an additional cost of 12 PSPs initially plus 4 PSPs per hour each, with each passenger adding half an hour to travel time. Most magical items do not function on the astral plane. If the power is deactivated while on the astral plane, the traveler becomes stranded.
augments: Maintenance cost of 8 PSPs per hour sustains travel. Additional passengers cost 12 PSPs initially plus 4 PSPs per hour each.
darkSunNotes: The Gray surrounding Athas makes probability travel extremely dangerous. Travelers risk becoming lost in Athas's unique astral void, and color pools leading to the Outer Planes are exceedingly rare near the dying world.

name: Retrieve
target: One object you can hold or carry in one hand, weighing up to 10 lb./level
description: You automatically teleport an item you can see within range directly to your hand. If the object is in the possession of an opponent, it comes to your hand if your opponent fails a Will save.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, the weight limit of the target increases by 10 pounds.
darkSunNotes: In the cutthroat bazaars of Tyr and Raam, retrieve is the most feared power among merchants and thieves alike. A nomad can pluck a purse from a noble's belt, a key from a templar's chain, or a weapon from an enemy's hand — all without crossing the crowded street. The elven market tribes have been known to employ nomads specifically for this purpose, running elaborate distraction schemes while the psionicist snatches the prize from across the market square.

name: Teleport
description: The psionicist teleports to a familiar location he can picture mentally, even if he has never physically been there. The destination must be a fixed location; teleporting to a moving object brings the traveler to where the object was last known to be. Teleportation is instantaneous, producing an audible pop at both ends as air is displaced. Restraints do not affect teleportation and remain behind, but clothing and equipment up to one-fifth of body mass travel along. By doubling the PSP cost, the psionicist can carry up to three times his body mass or bring one or two other willing characters. Cost and difficulty increase with distance: 10 yards (20 PSPs, +1), 100 yards (20 PSPs, +0), 1,000 yards (30 PSPs, -1), 10 miles (40 PSPs, -2), 100 miles (50 PSPs, -3), 1,000 miles (60 PSPs, -4), 10,000 miles (70 PSPs, -5), planet to planet (100 PSPs, -6).
augments: By doubling the PSP cost, the psionicist can carry up to three times his body mass or bring one or two additional willing characters.
darkSunNotes: Teleportation is prized on Athas, where overland travel across the scorching wastes is deadly. Many psionicists guard knowledge of safe teleport destinations jealously.

name: Teleport Other
description: This power functions identically to Teleport, except the psionicist teleports another character instead of himself, remaining at his current location. The target must be willing or unconscious. PSP costs and power score modifications follow the same distance-based scaling as Teleport. By doubling the PSP cost, the psionicist can teleport up to three characters, provided they are firmly grasping one another.
augments: By doubling the PSP cost, the psionicist can teleport up to three characters simultaneously if they are grasping one another.
darkSunNotes: Sorcerer-kings have been known to use this power to exile enemies to remote desert locations, stranding them without supplies in the trackless wastes.

name: Time Dilation
description: The psionicist affects the timestream, speeding or slowing its flow relative to herself. Hasting (slowing time): x2 costs 18 PP (-3 check), x3 costs 36 PP (-5), x4 costs 54 PP (-7). When hasted, movement, attack rates, spellcasting, and psionic powers all increase, but spells or powers that target non-dilated subjects require a save to function across timestreams. Duration is a number of rounds equal to the psionicist's level. Slowing (speeding time): x1/10 costs 18 PP, x1/20 costs 36 PP, x1/50 costs 54 PP. The psionicist's turns stretch enormously—6 turns at x1/20 dilation equals 120 turns (20 hours) of outside time. This can reduce water consumption to near zero or slow poison. Maintained powers cost only on subjective rounds.
augments: Rate of dilation can be increased one step on an exceptional manifestation at no additional cost.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where water is life, a psionicist using time dilation to slow herself can survive on a single waterskin for days of outside time—an incredible survival advantage in the deep desert.

name: Banishment
description: The psionicist teleports a creature against its will into a pocket dimension and holds it there. The pocket dimension is a featureless area with a benign environment. If the psionicist ceases concentration, the banished creature automatically returns to its original location. The creature cannot reappear in a space that is now occupied by solid matter; it instead returns to the nearest open space. While the psionicist maintains the power, the creature cannot return unless it has access to planar travel, in which case it must win a psychic contest against the psionicist to escape.
augments: Maintenance cost of 10 PSPs per round sustains the banishment. If the manifester stops paying, the creature returns automatically.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, banishment is sometimes used by templar-psionicists to temporarily remove dangerous threats from the battlefield, sending foes to a barren pocket dimension not unlike the wastelands beyond the Tablelands.

name: Dimension Door, Psionic
target: You and touched objects or other touched willing creatures
description: As the dimension door spell, except as noted here. You instantly transfer yourself from your current location to any other spot within range. You always arrive at exactly the spot desired. After using this power, you can't take any other actions until your next turn. You can bring along objects as long as their weight doesn't exceed your maximum load. You may also bring one additional willing Medium or smaller creature or its equivalent per three caster levels. A Large creature counts as two Medium creatures, and so on.
augments: If you spend 6 additional power points, you can manifest this power as a move action.
darkSunNotes: In the labyrinthine undercities beneath Nibenay and Raam, psionic dimension door is the preferred means of escape for thieves, spies, and assassins who must navigate trapped corridors and sealed vaults. The templarate has learned to ward key chambers with dimensional anchors, but a skilled nomad can still slip through the gaps. Escaped slaves have used this power to bypass the walls of city-states entirely, vanishing from slave pens and reappearing in the desert beyond.

name: Dimensional Anchor, Psionic
description: As the dimensional anchor spell, except as noted here. A green ray springs from your outstretched hand. You must make a ranged touch attack to hit the target. Any creature or object struck by the ray is covered with a shimmering emerald field that completely blocks extradimensional travel. Forms of movement barred by dimensional anchor include astral projection, blink, dimension door, ethereal jaunt, etherealness, gate, maze, plane shift, shadow walk, teleport, and similar powers and spells. The power also prevents the use of a gate or teleportation circle for the duration.
darkSunNotes: The sorcerer-kings employ psionic dimensional anchors as a cornerstone of their tyrannical security. The obsidian walls of Kalak's ziggurat in Tyr were said to be laced with permanent anchor effects, preventing any would-be assassin from teleporting inside. Templars carry specially crafted focus items — usually shards of green obsidian — that help them anchor fleeing criminals. In a world where even beggars might know a psionic trick or two, the ability to prevent dimensional escape is power itself.

name: Dismissal, Psionic
description: As the dismissal spell, except as noted here. This power forces an extraplanar creature back to its proper plane if it fails a Will save.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, the boundaries between planes are tattered and thin, weakened by millennia of defiling magic. Creatures from the elemental planes, the Gray, and the Black occasionally slip through the cracks unbidden. Psionic dismissal is the psionicist's answer to these intrusions — a focused burst of teleportive energy that shoves the interloper back where it belongs and seals the tear behind it.

name: Fly, Psionic
description: As the fly spell, except as noted here. You gain a fly speed of 60 feet (or 40 feet if you wear medium or heavy armor, or if you carry a medium or heavy load). You can ascend at half speed and descend at double speed, and your maneuverability is good. Using the power requires only as much concentration as walking, so you can attack or manifest powers normally. You can charge but not run, and you cannot carry aloft more weight than your maximum load, plus any armor you wear.
darkSunNotes: In a world without casual arcane flight, psionic fly grants a staggering tactical advantage. Nomads soar above the searing sands while their enemies trudge below, scouting water sources and enemy positions alike. The Oba of Gulg employs flying psychic warriors as her personal Sky Hunters, who descend upon poachers in the Crescent Forest like raptors. To fly above the Sea of Silt is to court disaster, however — the silt horrors sense vibrations even in the air above their domain.

name: Freedom of Movement, Psionic
description: As the freedom of movement spell, except as noted here. You can move and attack normally, even under the influence of psionics or magic that usually impedes movement, such as paralysis, solid fog, slow, and web. You automatically succeed on any grapple check made to resist a grapple attempt, as well as on grapple checks or Escape Artist checks made to escape a grapple or a pin. The power also allows you to move and attack normally while underwater.
darkSunNotes: Freedom of movement is essential for navigating the countless hazards of Athas. Whether trapped in the clutches of a silt horror, ensnared by the living vines of the Forest Ridge, or pinned beneath a collapsing ruin in the desert, this power has saved more lives than any healing art. Gladiators prize it against net-wielders and grapplers, while escaped slaves use it to slip from chains and manacles as if they were made of smoke.

name: Summon Object
description: The psionicist brings a distant object to him. The item must be a single piece weighing no more than 100 pounds. Metal costs double, obsidian triples. Cost varies with distance: 10 yards (30 PP), 100 yards (35 PP), 1,000 yards (40 PP), 10 miles (50 PP), 100 miles (60 PP), 1,000 miles (70 PP), 10,000 miles (100 PP). The psionicist must either see the object or know exactly where it is. If the object has been moved from its expected location, it cannot be summoned without visual confirmation (e.g., via clairvoyance). If removing an object from another person, the victim receives a saving throw. Summoned objects appear in the psionicist's hand or at his feet.
augments: Cost scales with distance. Metal objects cost double, obsidian triple.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where a steel sword left behind can mean the difference between life and death, summon object lets a psionicist recall his prized metal weapon from across the Tablelands.

name: Summon Planar Energies
description: The psionicist unlocks alien energies from other planes and uses them to attack enemies. Available energy types and their effects: Fire—2d8 damage in 10-ft. cube, 1d8 additional next round; Heat—1d12 twice, two targets; Magma—1d12 twice plus 2d8 next round and 1d8 third round; Radiance—3d8 incandescent strikes twice; Lightning—1d20 twice in 5-ft. wide path; Salt—3d8 dehydrating in 10-ft. cube plus enfeeblement; Ash—4d4 cold in 20-ft. cone; Positive Energy—5d4, destroys undead on failed save; Negative Energy—3d12, drains a level on failed save. Victims may save for half damage. Using this power more than twice per day requires a save or the psionicist falls unconscious for 1d6 turns.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the Inner Planes press close to the material world, summoning planar energies is a terrifyingly effective combat power. The para-elemental planes of Silt and Magma offer particularly devastating attack options unique to the Athasian cosmology.

name: Time Duplicate
description: The psionicist travels forward in time to borrow a future self, creating an identical duplicate. Round 1: The power is initiated. Round 2: The future self from round 3 arrives, creating two psionicists. Both act normally. The future self gains advantages: automatic initiative, -4 AC bonus, +4 saving throw bonus, and +2 to attack rolls (having already lived through the round). Round 3: The psionicist travels back to round 2 to help himself—no psionicist is present. Round 4: The future self returns in the same condition as when round 2 ended. Injuries to the future self are felt by the present self when he reaches that point. Injuries to the present self are immediately duplicated on the future self.
darkSunNotes: Time duplicate is one of the most unsettling powers in the Athasian psionicist's arsenal—witnesses report seeing the same person fighting alongside himself before vanishing, only to reappear moments later bearing wounds not yet inflicted.

name: Trace Teleport
target: Spread with a radius of 25 ft. + 5 ft./2 levels, centered on you
description: As detect teleportation, except you can trace the destination of any psionic or magical teleportation made by others within this power's area within the last minute. You know the direction and distance the individuals traveled and could teleport to the location yourself if you so desired (and if you know the psionic teleport power), as if you had 'seen casually' the location. This power does not grant you any information on the conditions at the other end of the trace beyond the mental coordinates of the location.
augments: If you spend 2 additional power points, this power's range increases to Medium (100 ft. + 10 ft./level).
darkSunNotes: Trace teleport is an indispensable tool for the templar hunting squads that enforce the sorcerer-kings' will. When a fugitive psionicist teleports away, a skilled tracer can follow the psychic residue like a bloodhound follows a scent, pinpointing the destination within moments. The Veiled Alliance has developed counter-techniques involving rapid sequential teleports to different locations, creating a web of false trails that can confuse even an experienced tracer. In the shadow wars between the city-states, trace teleport has turned many a clean escape into a deadly trap.

name: Wormhole
target: Portal up to 10 ft. x 10 ft.
description: The psionicist opens a door between two points, regardless of distance. One end must be within 20 yards; the portal can be any size up to 10 ft. square (doubling size costs -2 to power check). The far end can be placed at any point the psionicist has seen or can reference from his current position. Distance affects cost: 100 yards (24 PP, 8/round), 1,000 yards (36 PP, 12/round), 10 miles (48 PP, 16/round), 100 miles (60 PP, 20/round), 1,000 miles (72 PP, 24/round), 10,000 miles (100 PP, 30/round), interplanetary (150 PP, 50/round). Once created, the entrances are motionless and anything can pass through for instant transport. Unwilling creatures receive a saving throw. Objects larger than the portal cannot be transported.
augments: Cost and maintenance scale with distance. Portal size can be doubled at -2 per doubling to power check.
darkSunNotes: Wormholes could revolutionize Athasian trade and warfare—instantly connecting distant city-states or transporting entire units of soldiers across the Tablelands. The sorcerer-kings would pay dearly for this power, or kill to suppress it.

name: Baleful Teleport
description: You psychoportively disperse minuscule portions of the subject, dealing 9d6 points of damage. Targets can be protected from the effects of baleful teleport by dimensional anchor.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by 1d6 points. For each extra 2d6 points of damage, this power's save DC increases by 1 and your manifester level increases by 1 for the purpose of overcoming power resistance.
darkSunNotes: Arena crowds in Tyr and Balic roar with savage delight when a nomad gladiator uses baleful teleport, scattering fragments of an opponent across the sand in a shower of gore. Templar interrogators have found more insidious applications, using the power at reduced intensity to slowly unmake a prisoner piece by piece. The Veiled Alliance whispers that Hamanu himself once used a variant of this power to erase an entire rebel cell from existence, leaving nothing but bloodstains on the obsidian floor.

name: Catapsi
description: By manifesting this power, you generate psychic static, interfering with the ability of other psionic characters to manifest their powers or use psi-like abilities (you are not affected by your own catapsi manifestation). All psionic activity within the area requires 4 more power points to manifest than normal, unless a character makes a Will save each time he attempts to manifest a power. Using a psi-like ability becomes a full-round action, instead of a standard action, in a catapsi field. If two or more fields of catapsi overlap, the effects are not cumulative. The limit on the number of power points a subject can spend on a power remains in effect; thus, a subject may not be able to manifest its highest-level powers. If manifesting a power would cause the manifester to exceed available power points or spending limits, the manifestation fails automatically, but no power points are expended.
augments: For every 4 additional power points you spend, this power's range and the radius of its area both increase by 5 feet.
darkSunNotes: On a world where psionics pervade every stratum of society, catapsi is the great equalizer — and the great terror. Templar inquisitors deploy catapsi fields when raiding Veiled Alliance cells, smothering the psionics of their targets like sand smothering a flame. The Order, that secretive cabal of psionic preservers, considers catapsi a war crime against the mind itself. During the siege of Kalidnay, it is said that a single nomad's catapsi field silenced an entire battalion of psion-soldiers, leaving them helpless before the advancing undead.

name: Planar Transposition
description: The High Science of Psychoportation. The psionicist can switch a portion of the Prime Material Plane with a portion of another plane. Inner (Elemental) Planes, Ethereal, or Astral transpositions cost 65 PSPs; Outer Plane transpositions cost 130 PSPs. The psionicist can affect larger areas at -1 to the power score per additional 30-foot cube. A donut-shaped ring (10 feet inner diameter, 10 feet wide) is also possible. The transposed areas remain switched as long as maintenance is paid. Living creatures are either wholly in or wholly out. Creatures that wander away from the transposed area don't return when the power drops. Possible uses include creating temporary oases (transposing desert for Elemental Water), opening passages through mountains (Ethereal), or sending enemy-filled towers to the Abyss.
augments: Maintain for 1 PP/turn. Each additional 30-ft. cube costs -1 to power score. Outer Planes cost 130 PP initial.
darkSunNotes: On water-starved Athas, transposing a patch of desert for a section of the Elemental Plane of Water can create a temporary oasis—an act that can save an entire tribe or make a psionicist fabulously wealthy.

name: Plane Shift, Psionic
target: Willing creature touched, or up to eight willing creatures joining hands
description: As the plane shift spell, except as noted here. You move yourself or some other creature to another plane of existence or alternate dimension. If several willing persons link hands in a circle, as many as eight can be affected by the power at the same time. You may attempt to send an unwilling target to another plane, but this requires a successful melee touch attack.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, the planes are not the orderly cosmology known to other worlds. The Gray — that bleak realm of the dead — presses against reality like a smothering blanket, and the Black lies beyond even that. Plane shifting from Athas is notoriously difficult and unpredictable; the sorcerer-kings have spent millennia reinforcing the planar barriers to prevent outside interference. Those who do manage to shift away often find it nearly impossible to return, as Athas seems to resist reconnection like a wound refusing to reopen.

name: Summon Planar Creature
description: The psionicist reaches into another plane, grabs a random creature, and teleports it to his own plane. The victim is disoriented for one round after arriving, suffering a -2 penalty on initiative, attacks, and saving throws. Unlike a magical summons, this power provides no control over the creature and does not return it home automatically. To be rid of the creature, the psionicist must banish it, teleport it, kill it, or reason with it. The creature materializes anywhere within 200 yards of the psionicist's position. The psionicist chooses the plane of origin but not the specific creature summoned. It costs 45 PSPs to summon from the astral or ethereal plane, or 90 PSPs from an inner or outer plane.
augments: Summoning from inner or outer planes costs 90 PSPs instead of the base 45.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, the sealed planar boundaries make this power unreliable. Creatures pulled through the Gray are often twisted or maddened by the experience, arriving in an even more hostile state than usual.

name: Time Travel
description: The psionicist extends teleportive powers into the time stream, traveling to different times. Cost and difficulty scale with temporal distance: 1 day (20 PP), 1 week (30 PP, -1), 1 month (40 PP, -2), 1 year (50 PP, -3), 10 years (60 PP, -4), 100 years (70 PP, -5), 1,000 years (80 PP, -6), 10,000+ years (100 PP, -8). If the psionicist knows teleport other, he may bring one companion per level for an additional 20 PSPs each. While gone, time continues normally—8 hours spent in the past means returning 8 hours after departure. Travel to the past allows altering events, though historical inertia resists changes to important events. Once changed, an event cannot be changed again. Travel to the future reveals possible timelines, but the act of observation creates historical inertia toward the revealed future.
augments: Cost scales with temporal distance. Companions cost 20 PP each.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where the sorcerer-kings have deliberately erased history, time travel is one of the only ways to witness the truth of the Green Age and the Cleansing Wars firsthand—though the paradoxes it creates can be as dangerous as any defiler's magic.

name: Overland Flight, Psionic
description: As the overland flight spell, except as noted here. You fly at a speed of 40 feet with average maneuverability. When using this power for long-distance movement, you can hustle without taking nonlethal damage (a forced march still requires Constitution checks). You can cover 64 miles in an 8-hour period of flight (or 32 miles at a speed of 20 feet).
darkSunNotes: Overland flight is the power that makes the great trade routes of Athas possible for those who can afford a nomad guide. Soaring above the Sea of Silt, the Ringing Mountains, or the endless Tablelands, a manifester can cover in hours what a caravan takes tendays to traverse. The sorcerer-kings view unauthorized overland flight with deep suspicion — a psionicist who can bypass their borders at will is a threat to their iron grip. Nibenay's shadow consorts patrol the skies above the city, hunting for unauthorized flyers.

name: Temporal Acceleration
description: You enter another time frame, speeding up so greatly that all other creatures seem frozen, though they are actually still moving at normal speed. You are free to act for 1 round of apparent time. You can manifest powers, cast spells, move, or perform other types of actions. While your temporal acceleration is in effect, other creatures are invulnerable to your attacks and powers. You cannot target a creature with any attack or power. However, a power you manifest that affects an area and has a duration longer than the remaining duration of your temporal acceleration has its normal effect on creatures in the area once this power ends. You can affect an unattended object but not an object held, carried, or worn by another creature. You are undetectable by any means while your temporal acceleration lasts. When your temporal acceleration expires, you resume acting during your current turn in the standard time frame. You are shaken for 1 round upon your return. Manifesting this power is a swift action. You cannot manifest this power when it isn't your turn.
augments: For every 4 additional power points you spend, this power's duration (in apparent time) increases by 1 round.
darkSunNotes: Temporal acceleration is whispered about in the psionics academies of Athas as the ultimate expression of mental dominance over reality itself. To step outside the flow of time, even for a heartbeat, is to touch something older and more fundamental than defiler magic or elemental power. The Order teaches that time manipulation draws attention from entities beyond the Gray — ancient things that predate the sorcerer-kings and hunger for minds bold enough to disturb the temporal stream.

name: Divert Teleport
target: A circle, centered on you, with a radius of 50 ft. + 10 ft./level
description: Similar to detect teleportation, except that you know the intended destination, and you can divert the final destination of any teleportation attempt made by others within the area. This is an immediate action, like manifesting a quickened power, and counts toward the limit of one quickened power per round. You can manifest this power even if it is not your turn. You can divert the destination of both incoming and outgoing teleportations, psionic and magical. You must overcome the power resistance of creatures that possess it to make a successful diversion, and the teleporting creature can make a Will save to foil the diversion as well. For the purpose of this power, 'divert' means you choose the actual destination of any teleportation attempt you can affect, as if you yourself were teleporting to that location, regardless of the teleportation range. The destination you choose must be a location with which you are very familiar or that you have studied carefully.
darkSunNotes: Divert teleport is the ultimate expression of nomad dominance over spatial travel. The Shadow King of Nibenay is rumored to maintain a permanent diversion field around his naggaramakam, redirecting any unauthorized teleporter into a sealed pit filled with hungry silk wyrms. During the wars between the city-states, nomad generals used this power to redirect enemy reinforcements into ambush zones — entire squads of soldiers materializing inside kill boxes instead of their intended rally points.

name: Ethereal Jaunt, Psionic
description: As the ethereal jaunt spell, except as noted here. You become ethereal, along with your equipment. For the duration of the power, you are in the Ethereal Plane, which overlaps the Material Plane. When the power expires, you return to material existence.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, the border between the material world and the Gray is thinner than on most worlds, making ethereal jaunts a harrowing experience. Nomads who jaunt describe a landscape of ash-colored mist populated by the restless dead — spirits of those consumed by defiler magic or slain in the endless wars of the sorcerer-kings. The Gray presses against the jaunt like a living thing, hungry and cold, and those who linger too long sometimes return... changed.

name: Phase Door, Psionic
target: Ethereal 5-ft.-by-8-ft. opening, 10 ft. deep + 5 ft. deep per three levels
description: As the phase door spell, except as noted here. This power creates an ethereal passage through wooden, plaster, or stone walls, but not other materials. The phase door is invisible and inaccessible to all creatures except you, and only you can use the passage. The power disappears after a number of uses equal to one per two manifester levels, or after one day per manifester level.
darkSunNotes: The ancient ruins beneath the Tablelands are riddled with phase doors left behind by psionicists of the Green Age, passages that have persisted for millennia through stone that predates the sorcerer-kings. Explorers who stumble upon these ghostly portals sometimes find themselves in sealed chambers containing treasures — or horrors — that have been locked away since before the Cleansing Wars. The Veiled Alliance maintains a network of phase doors through the walls of Tyr, enabling members to pass through the city unseen.

name: Teleport, Psionic Greater
target: You and touched objects or other touched willing creatures
description: As the greater teleport spell, except as noted here. You instantly transport yourself and up to 50 additional pounds per level to any spot you can visualize. There is no range limit and no chance of error. You need not have seen the destination, but in that case you must have at least a reliable description of the place.
darkSunNotes: Greater psionic teleport is the province of the most powerful nomads on Athas, and those who can manifest it reliably are courted by sorcerer-kings and merchant houses alike. To step from the shadow of Urik's walls to the shores of the Sea of Silt, or from the Forest Ridge to the obsidian wastes, without error or risk — this is power that reshapes the politics of the Tablelands. Hamanu is said to have placed a death mark on any nomad who teleports into Urik without his explicit permission.

name: Time Hop, Mass
description: As time hop, except you can affect any number of willing subjects in range, including yourself. You can choose which creatures are affected by the power. The subjects hop forward in time a number of hours equal to your manifester level, or some shorter number of hours; you decide how many hours the mass time hop lasts when you manifest the power.
augments: If you spend 6 additional power points, you can manifest this power as an immediate action.
darkSunNotes: Mass time hop is the last resort of a desperate army. During the fall of Yaramuke, it is said that a cadre of nomads time-hopped the entire civilian population forward by a day, hoping the battle would be over when they returned. It was — but the city was nothing but ash and ruin when the survivors materialized. The Veiled Alliance has contingency plans involving mass time hop to preserve its members in the event of a sorcerer-king's purge, leaping forward past the danger in a single silver flash.

name: Etherealness, Psionic
target: You and one other touched willing creature/three levels
description: As the etherealness spell, except as noted here. You and other willing creatures joined by linked hands become ethereal. Besides yourself, you can bring one creature per three manifester levels to the Ethereal Plane. Once ethereal, the subjects need not stay together.
darkSunNotes: To lead an entire party into the Gray is an act of supreme confidence or supreme desperation. The ancient psionicists of Kalidnay reportedly used mass etherealness to evacuate their entire city before its destruction, though the spirits never emerged on the other side. Some say they wander the Gray still, a ghost city of thousands, forever searching for a way back to a home that no longer exists.

name: Teleportation Circle, Psionic
target: 5-ft.-radius circle that teleports those who activate it
description: As the teleportation circle spell, except as noted here. You create a circle on the floor or other horizontal surface that teleports any creature who stands on it to a designated spot. The circle is a glowing, silvery ring. Once you designate the destination for the circle, you can't change it. The destination must be very familiar to you. When a creature stands in the circle, it is instantly teleported to the destination.
darkSunNotes: The legendary teleportation circles of the Green Age once connected every major city on Athas in a web of instantaneous travel. Most were destroyed during the Cleansing Wars or deliberately dismantled by the sorcerer-kings to prevent rivals from using them. The few that remain are among the most closely guarded secrets on the planet. Beneath the ruins of Giustenal, explorers have reported finding a circle that still glows with faint silver light — but none who have stepped upon it have ever returned to tell where it leads.

name: Time Regression
description: You can regress apparent time 1 round into the past. In effect, you 'replay' the previous round of activity. The power regresses time to the point along the time stream just prior to your previous turn, undoing the effects of everyone else's actions in the meantime. Once you have used time regression, only you retain knowledge of what happened during the round that is being replayed; however, you can communicate that knowledge verbally to your companions, if desired. During the round that you live through a second time, you can act on knowledge you previously gained by already living through the immediate future. In all likelihood, you'll probably not choose to manifest time regression during your second pass through the time stream, instead taking completely new actions, but you pay the XP cost all the same. XP Cost: 1,000 XP.
darkSunNotes: Time regression is the rarest and most feared power in the nomad's arsenal, for it violates the most fundamental law of existence. On Athas, where the world itself seems to be dying and entropy is a palpable force, rewinding time even by a single round sends shockwaves through the Gray that can be felt by the undead and the sorcerer-kings alike. Borys the Dragon was said to have sensed every use of time regression on the planet, hunting down those who dared to tamper with the timeline. It is the ultimate second chance in a world that offers none.

name: Timeless Body
description: Your body ignores all harmful (and helpful) effects, beginning when you finish manifesting this power and ending at the end of your next turn. While timeless body is in effect, you are invulnerable to all attacks and powers. This power cannot be quickened.
darkSunNotes: Timeless body is the ultimate defensive technique, removing the manifester from the flow of cause and effect entirely. For one precious round, not even a sorcerer-king's fury can touch you. The power is rumored to have been developed by an ancient psionicist who sought to survive the moment of the planet's death — to stand at the end of all things and remain untouched. On Athas, where every living thing is one breath away from oblivion, the ability to simply refuse to be harmed, even for a single heartbeat, is the closest thing to divinity a mortal can achieve.

name: Call to Mind
description: By meditating on a subject, you can recall natural memories and knowledge otherwise inaccessible to you. On a failed Knowledge check, you can manifest this power to gain a new check with a +4 competence bonus. If successful, you instantly recall what was previously buried in your subconscious.
darkSunNotes: Athasian scholars in the libraries of Balic use call to mind to recover fragments of pre-Cleansing Wars history, teasing ancient knowledge from deep memory. Wandering psionicists in the wastes find it invaluable for recalling half-forgotten survival lore — which plants hold water, which ruins hide shade, and which paths the silk wyrms no longer patrol.

name: Charm, Psionic
description: As the charm person spell, except as noted here. This charm makes a humanoid creature regard you as its trusted friend and ally.
augments: You can augment this power in one or more of the following ways. 1. If you spend 2 additional power points, this power can also affect an animal, fey, giant, magical beast, or monstrous humanoid. 2. If you spend 4 additional power points, this power can also affect an aberration, dragon, elemental, or outsider in addition to the creature types mentioned above. 3. If you spend 4 additional power points, this power's duration increases to one day per level. In addition, for every 2 additional power points you spend to achieve any of these effects, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: In the merchant houses of Athas, psionic charm is the currency of influence. House Stel and House Wavir employ entire cadres of telepaths to smooth negotiations and ensure favorable trade agreements. The practice is so widespread that high-ranking merchants routinely employ psionic bodyguards specifically trained to detect and counter such manipulations.

name: Conceal Thoughts
description: This defensive devotion protects you against psionic or magical ESP, probes, mindlink, life detection, and other powers or spells which read or detect thoughts. To overcome thought concealment, an attacker must wage and win a psychic contest against you.
augments: Maintenance cost is 3 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: Conceal thoughts is essential for any psionicist navigating the political intrigues of the city-states.

name: Contact
