description: Contact must be established before virtually any telepathic power can be used on another creature. It is the conduit for other telepathic exchanges. Contact does not allow communication by itself. The initial cost depends on the target's level or HD: 1–5 HD costs 3 PSPs, 6–10 HD costs 8, 11–15 HD costs 13, 16–20 HD costs 18. You must either have the subject in sight or know specifically who or what you are looking for. Distance modifiers apply when the target is not in line of sight: 1 mile (−1), 10 miles (−3), 100 miles (−5), 1,000 miles (−7), 10,000 miles (−9). Non-mammalian minds are harder to contact, with modifiers ranging from −1 (mammals) to −8 (plants). Psionicists can resist contact naturally; only the five telepathic attack modes can force contact on a resisting psionicist.
augments: Maintenance cost is 1 PSP per round per contact maintained.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, contact is the foundation of all telepathic activity and the first power most telepaths master.

name: Daydream
description: By using this power, you cause someone's mind to wander. This is only effective against characters with Intelligence 14 or less who are not concentrating hard on the task before them, but are going about their business in a casual, relaxed manner. Once affected, the daydreamer pays little attention to his surroundings. Thieves gain a 20% bonus on their pick pockets, move silently, and hide in shadows checks. Other characters can perform these tasks as an average 4th level thief. The DM can assign chances for success to other types of skulking and skullduggery.
augments: Maintenance cost is 3 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: In the crushing tedium of Athasian slave labor—hauling stone under the dark sun for sixteen hours without rest—daydream is both mercy and cruelty. It grants a moment's escape into pleasant fantasy, but the return to reality is all the more bitter. Templars despise this power, viewing any mental escape as a form of rebellion.

name: Daze, Psionic
target: One humanoid creature that has 4 HD or less
description: As the daze spell, except as noted here. This power clouds the mind of a humanoid creature with 4 or fewer Hit Dice so that it takes no actions. Humanoids of 5 or more HD are not affected.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power can affect a target that has Hit Dice equal to 4 + the additional points spent.
darkSunNotes: Street psions in the Warrens of Tyr use psionic daze to stun marks before lifting their purses. It is the first power many wild talents manifest, often unconsciously — a child freezing a bully mid-swing, a slave momentarily stunning an overseer. On Athas, even the smallest psionic gift can mean the difference between chains and freedom.

name: Demoralize
description: You fill your target with self-doubt. The target takes a -2 morale penalty on attack rolls, checks, saves, and damage rolls. Demoralize counters and dispels charm, psionic.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's penalty increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Athasian arena masters use demoralize to soften up gladiators before important matches, ensuring the favored combatant wins. Beyond the arenas, it is a tool of psychological warfare — caravaneers manifesting waves of despair toward pursuing raiders, or templar psions breaking the will of besieged villages before the soldiers even arrive.

name: Destiny Dissonance
description: Your mere touch grants your foe an impending sense of doom. The subject suffers a -1 penalty on attack rolls and saving throws. If the target is psionically focused, it loses its focus.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where survival often hinges on split-second reactions, destiny dissonance can be a death sentence. The power floods the victim's mind with a creeping certainty that every choice they make will be the wrong one. Jhakaran dune traders call it 'the sand-doubt' — a paralysis of the soul more crippling than any physical wound.

name: Disable
target: One humanoid of Medium or smaller size
description: You broadcast a telepathic static that disrupts the subject's ability to act. If the subject fails its Will save, it drops to 0 hit points (disabled). The subject is not knocked unconscious and can take a single action each round. At the beginning of the target's next turn, its hit points return to normal.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power can affect a target one size category larger.
darkSunNotes: Slave catchers in the employ of the sorcerer-kings favor disable for capturing runaways alive — a disabled slave can still walk, still be chained, still be sold. The power leaves no marks on the body, making it impossible to prove the slave was harmed. Veiled Alliance rescuers have turned this same power against the catchers, dropping them mid-pursuit without spilling blood.

name: Distract
description: You cause your target's mind to wander, dulling its senses and perceptions. While you concentrate on this power, the subject takes a -4 penalty on Listen, Search, Sense Motive, and Spot checks.
darkSunNotes: In the crowded bazaars of Nibenay, distract is the pickpocket's best friend — clouding a merchant's attention just long enough to lift a ceramic piece or a waterskin. More dangerously, it sees use in the wilderness to dull a sentry's awareness, ensuring that a war party of gith or elven raiders can creep within striking distance unnoticed.

name: Ego Whip
description: Ego whip is one of the five telepathic attack modes used to establish contact with another psionicist's mind. The power assaults the victim's ego, leaving him with feelings of inferiority and worthlessness. If used against a contacted mind, the target is dazed for 1d4 rounds, during which all of his die rolls (psionic attacks, melee attacks, saving throws, etc.) are penalized 5 points (or 25%) and he cannot cast any spells above third level. This power has three ranges: at medium range the power score is reduced by 2, at long range by 5.
darkSunNotes: Templar inquisitors wield ego whip like a psychic lash, stripping away a prisoner's sense of self until nothing remains but a broken, compliant husk. In the arenas, a psionicist who can shatter an opponent's confidence mid-charge turns the crowd's cheers into the weapon that finishes the fight.

name: Empathy
description: By using empathy, you can sense the basic needs, drives, and emotions generated by any mind. Thirst, hunger, fear, fatigue, pain, rage, hatred, uncertainty, curiosity, hostility, friendliness, love—all these and more can be sensed. When used against non-psionic targets, contact is not required. The initial cost is 1 PSP when contact is not needed.
augments: Maintenance cost is 1 PSP per round.
darkSunNotes: Empathy is widely used by Athasian merchants to gauge the mood of trading partners.

name: Empty Mind
description: You empty your mind of all transient and distracting thoughts, improving your self-control. You gain a +2 bonus on Will saves until your next action.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, the bonus on your Will save increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: The psionicists of the Order teach empty mind as the first line of mental defense. In a world where telepathic intrusion is a constant threat — from sorcerer-kings, templars, and wild psions alike — the ability to instantly clear one's thoughts can mean the difference between free will and enslavement. Thri-kreen masters achieve a state they call 'wind-mind,' emptying their consciousness so completely that telepaths find nothing to grasp.

name: Id Insinuation
description: Id insinuation is one of the five telepathic attack modes. It seeks to unleash the uncontrolled subconscious of the defender, pitting it against his superego. The attack leaves the victim in a state of moral uproar—his id (the seat of primitive needs, animal drives, cruelty, and ferocity) seeks to launch him into a rage of violence and desire. His superego (the seat of moral conscience) struggles to maintain the upper hand. If used against a contacted mind, the target can do nothing for 1d4 rounds. This power has three ranges: at medium range the power score is reduced by 2, at long range by 5. On a critical success, the victim's id overpowers his superego and he turns against his allies for 1d4 rounds.
darkSunNotes: The id insinuation is whispered about in the slave quarters of every city-state—a power that plants seeds of primal chaos in a target's subconscious, unleashing their darkest impulses. Templars have used it to provoke riots among slaves they wished to cull, while Veiled Alliance agents turn it against the templars themselves.

name: Intellect Fortress
description: Intellect fortress is one of the five telepathic defense modes against unwanted contact. It calls forth the powers of the ego and superego to stop attacks. Unlike most other defenses, intellect fortress has an area of effect beyond your own mind, offering protection to other minds within a 3-yard radius. Every mind within that area defends against telepathic attack with your intellect fortress power score. You can initiate one other psionic power in the same round that you use intellect fortress.
darkSunNotes: When psionic warfare erupts in the courts of the sorcerer-kings, the intellect fortress is the first defense raised. Athasian psionicists describe it as a crystalline shell of pure thought, and those who have weathered a telepathic assault behind one speak of hearing enemy probes shatter against its walls like obsidian on stone.

name: Life Detection
description: You can detect the presence of living, thinking creatures within a limited area, scanning like radar. At short range you can cover a large angle: 180 degrees to 40 yards, 90 degrees to 60 yards, or 30 degrees to 100 yards. One round of scanning detects humans, demihumans, humanoids, mammals, and monsters with 8 or more hit dice. A second round detects birds, reptiles, amphibians, fish, and monsters with fewer than 8 hit dice. In either case you get an accurate count of creatures. The DM should make this power check secretly. On a critical failure (roll of 1), you get faulty information—detecting what is there but with numbers wrong by ±50%. On a roll of 20, you detect 1d6 creatures that aren't there.
augments: Maintenance cost is 3 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: Life detection is invaluable for navigating the dangerous wastelands of Athas.

name: Mental Barrier
description: Mental barrier is one of the five telepathic defense modes against unwanted contact. It is a carefully built wall of thought repetition which exposes only one small area of the mind at a time. You can initiate one other psionic power in the same round that you use mental barrier. On a critical success, contact during this round and the next is impossible.
darkSunNotes: Every Athasian psionicist learns mental barrier before they learn to attack—survival demands it. In a world where any stranger in a crowded marketplace might be a telepath probing for secrets, the ability to slam shut the doors of the mind is as essential as carrying water in the wastes.

name: Mind Blank
description: Mind blank is one of the five telepathic defense modes against unwanted contact. It attempts to hide the mind from attack, making its parts unidentifiable. This defense is particularly effective against psionic blast and id insinuation. Mind blank is unique among defense modes: it costs nothing to maintain. A character can still recover PSPs while using this power. Mind blank is nearly instinctual; if a character knows it, it is almost always active, even while sleeping or meditating. The power is inactive only if you announce it, or you use another defense mode. You can use one other psionic power (but not another defense mode) in the same round you use mind blank. Even though mind blank has no PSP cost, it still constitutes psionic activity and may attract detection.
darkSunNotes: The Veiled Alliance teaches mind blank to every operative before their first mission. In cities where the sorcerer-kings' telepaths scan crowds for seditious thoughts, a blank mind is the only safe mind. The power leaves a psionicist's thoughts as featureless and unreadable as the endless Sea of Silt.

name: Mind Thrust
description: Mind thrust is one of the five telepathic attack modes used to establish contact with another psionicist's mind. It is a stabbing attack which seeks to short the synapses of the defender. If the subject's mind has already been contacted and he has psionic powers, this power causes damage: the subject loses the use of one power, chosen randomly, for 2d6 days. Beyond establishing contact, mind thrust has no effect on creatures or characters without psionic powers. This power has three ranges: at medium range the power score is reduced by 2, at long range by 5.
darkSunNotes: The most basic telepathic attack on Athas, mind thrust is learned by nearly every psionicist who survives their first year of training. In the gladiatorial arenas, a well-timed thrust can drop an opponent before a blade is drawn—a spike of pure psychic force that leaves no wound for the crowd to see.

name: Missive
description: You send a telepathic message of up to ten words to any living creature within range. Missive is strictly a one-way exchange from you to the subject. If you do not share a common language, the subject 'hears' meaningless mental syllables.
darkSunNotes: The simplest and most common telepathic power on Athas, missive is used by everyone from street vendors in the Warrens to templar commanders on the battlefield. In the gladiatorial arenas, psionically gifted trainers whisper last-second instructions to their fighters. On the open desert, scouts use missive to relay sightings of predators or water sources without alerting nearby enemies.

name: Passive Contact
description: The psionicist establishes a constant yet passive mental link with a willing subject who must be present during initiation. After establishment, the subject can go anywhere while maintaining a tenuous link with the psionicist. The link allows the subject to send a mental call to get the psionicist's attention—the psionicist receives a mental tingle indicating the passive contact wants attention. The psionicist can then respond with normal contact and mindlink. This power is useful for giving nonpsionic allies a way to reach the psionicist when they need help. On an exceptional manifestation, the psionicist knows if the subject is in trouble even without a deliberate call.
augments: Maintain for 1 PP/hour to keep the passive link active.
darkSunNotes: Merchant house agents across the Tyr region use passive contact to maintain emergency links with their house psionicist—when a caravan is ambushed in the desert, a single mental call can summon help.

name: Psychic Messenger
description: This power allows you to create an insubstantial, three-dimensional image of yourself which can appear anywhere within 200 miles and deliver a message. Everyone present can see and hear the messenger. Communication is one-way—you have no idea what is happening around your messenger unless you're using some other power. On a critical success, you can make the messenger's appearance differ from your own.
augments: Maintenance cost is 3 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: Psychic messengers are used across Athas to communicate between distant city-states.

name: Send Thoughts
description: This is one-way communication, allowing you to send your own thoughts to someone else's mind. You can send information or simply distract the target. If the target is a wizard casting a spell, he must make a saving throw vs. spells (modified by the difference between his Intelligence and your power score). Failure means the wizard's concentration is broken and the spell is spoiled. If the target is someone in melee, he suffers a 2-point penalty on all attack rolls (but not damage rolls). On a critical success, wizards cannot make the saving throw at all.
augments: Maintenance cost is 2 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: Before the destruction of Giustenal, the Order is said to have maintained a network of psionicists linked by send thoughts, passing intelligence across the Tablelands faster than any messenger could ride. Today, merchant houses pay handsomely for psionicists who can relay trade information between city-states without risking raiders.

name: Sense Link
description: You perceive what the subject creature perceives using its sight, hearing, taste, or smell. Only one sense is linked, and you cannot switch between senses with the same manifestation. You make any skill checks involving that sense as the creature, rather than as yourself. Once you have linked the senses, the link persists even if the subject creature moves out of range of the original manifestation.
augments: You can augment this power in one of the following ways. 1. If you spend 2 additional power points, you can have the subject perceive one of your senses instead of you perceiving its sense.
darkSunNotes: Scouts and rangers across the Tablelands forge sense links with trained psionic animals — inix, erdlu, and even the occasional tamed jhakar — to survey dangerous terrain from safety. Thri-kreen clutch-scouts routinely link senses to coordinate their hunting packs across miles of open desert, sharing the sight of prey spotted from a high dune or the scent of water carried on an evening breeze.

name: Suppress Fear
description: The psionicist can bolster a character's courage or remove fear effects. When used on a frightened character, the power automatically dispels the fear, including effects from cause fear, emotion, eyebite, fear, scare, spook, symbol, and the psionic powers invincible foes and phobia amplification. When used prophylactically on an unfrightened character, the subject gains +4 to morale checks and saving throws against magical or psionic fear. If the fear effect normally allows no saving throw, the psionicist may attempt a second power check to defeat it anyway. Protection lasts one turn plus a number of rounds equal to the psionicist's level.
darkSunNotes: In the terror-filled wastes of Athas, where fear-inducing monsters and psionic intimidation are commonplace, suppress fear keeps allies fighting when lesser minds would flee in panic.

name: Taste Link
description: Taste link allows you to tap into the flavor senses of another person or creature. You taste whatever your link tastes. If the linked creature takes poison orally, you must make a saving throw vs. poison to avoid passing out and severing the link. You can't actually be poisoned this way, however. On a critical success, scent link is also gained.
augments: Maintenance cost is 4 PSPs per turn.
darkSunNotes: Paranoid nobles of Nibenay employ psionicists with taste link to monitor their food tasters from a safe distance—experiencing every flavor the taster does, alert for the telltale bitterness of poison. Some Athasian gourmands use it simply to experience meals they could never afford, living vicariously through the feasts of the rich.

name: Thought Shield
description: Thought shield is one of the five telepathic defense modes against unwanted contact. It clouds the mind so as to hide first one part, then another, making it difficult for attackers to find a target. You can initiate one other psionic power during the same round in which you use thought shield.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where casual telepathic intrusion is a fact of daily life, the thought shield is as common and necessary as a cloak against the wind. Merchants raise it during negotiations, lovers maintain it against jealous rivals, and escaped slaves cling to it like armor against the templar mind-hunters on their trail.

name: Truthear
description: When you use truthear, you can tell whether other people intentionally lie. You do not hear their words translated into truth; you merely know whether or not the speakers believe they are lying. On a critical success, you recognize a falsehood even when the speaker does not know he is speaking falsely.
augments: Maintenance cost is 2 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: Truthear is prized by Athasian merchants and nobles who must navigate webs of deception daily.

name: Amnesia
description: The psionicist interferes with a contacted creature's memory. While amnesia is maintained, the subject cannot remember anything that occurred before the psionicist's contact. The victim remembers events that occur during the amnesiac state. The victim retains physical skills (walking, talking, eating, weapon proficiency, tumbling) but temporarily loses knowledge-based skills (spellcasting, psionics use, lockpicking). General personality traits remain despite memory loss—making a monster forget doesn't make it friendly. On an exceptional manifestation, the victim remains amnesiac for 1d10 days after the psionicist ceases maintenance.
augments: Maintain for 5 PP/round per creature to sustain the amnesia.
darkSunNotes: Templar interrogators on Athas use amnesia to strip prisoners of their sense of identity before questioning, creating confused and compliant subjects. The Veiled Alliance also uses it to protect its members—better to forget than to betray.

name: Attraction
description: This power creates an overwhelming attraction to a particular person, item, creature, action, or event. A victim will do whatever seems reasonable to get close to the object of his attraction. The key word is reasonable—the victim is completely fascinated but does not suffer from blind obsession. He won't leap into a fire, over a cliff, or into the arms of a tarrasque. He can still recognize danger but will not flee unless the threat is strong and immediate. If the danger is not apparent (such as poison in a goblet of wine), the character could easily destroy himself in pursuit of the attraction.
augments: Maintenance cost is 8 PSPs per round. For every 2 additional power points spent, the effect borders on obsession.
darkSunNotes: The merchant princes of Balic use attraction to ensure that customers return to their stalls, while templar recruiters employ it to draw promising young psionicists into service before the Veiled Alliance can reach them. On Athas, where loyalty is bought with water and fear, a psionic pull toward a person or idea is just another form of currency.

name: Aversion
description: The victim of this power gains an aversion to a particular person, place, action, or event. He will do everything he can to avoid the object of his aversion, refusing to approach within 20 yards. If he is already within 20 yards, he will back away at the first opportunity. The aversion is planted in the victim's brain for one turn. It cannot be maintained for another turn unless you maintain contact throughout that turn.
augments: Maintenance cost is 8 PSPs per turn. On a critical success, the object of aversion affects the victim like a fear spell.
darkSunNotes: Templars use aversion to keep slaves away from restricted areas without posting guards—a psionically implanted revulsion toward a doorway or corridor that turns the stomach of anyone who approaches. The power is also favored by hermit preservers who wish to be left alone in the wastes.

name: Awe
description: You can use this power to make others hold you in awe. Each contacted character must make a saving throw vs. spells. Characters who fail are mentally cowed; they sense your awesome might. They have no desire to serve or befriend you, but they won't attack you unless forced to do so. They'll do whatever they can to avoid angering or upsetting you. If possible, they'll avoid you altogether and take the first opportunity to escape.
augments: Maintenance cost is 4 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: The sorcerer-kings radiate a natural awe that may itself be psionic in origin, but lesser psionicists can produce a similar effect. A templar high priest manifesting awe before a crowd of slaves can quell a riot without raising a hand—the sheer weight of projected authority crushing defiance before it forms.

name: Bestow Power
description: You link your mind with another psionic creature's mind, creating a brief conduit through which mental energy can be shared. When you manifest this power, the subject gains up to 2 power points. You can transfer only as many power points to a subject as it has manifester levels. Because of the intimate nature of this power, it cannot be fabricated into a psionic item — only power points generated by a psionic creature in the moment can be shared using bestow power.
augments: For every 3 additional power points you spend, the subject gains 2 additional power points.
darkSunNotes: In the psionic academies of Nibenay and Raam, bestow power is the foundation of cooperative manifesting. Students learn to share their mental reserves freely, forging bonds of trust that can mean survival in the wastes. Some mul gladiators secretly channel power to wounded allies through the chaos of arena combat, a practice the templars would punish severely if discovered.

name: Brain Lock
description: The subject's higher mind is locked away. He is dazed and cannot take psionic actions for the duration of the power. A brain locked subject is not stunned, so attackers get no special advantage against him.
augments: You can augment this power in one or both of the following ways. 1. If you spend 2 additional power points, this power can also affect an animal, fey, giant, magical beast, or monstrous humanoid. 2. If you spend 4 additional power points, this power can also affect an aberration, dragon, elemental, or outsider in addition to the creature types mentioned above.
darkSunNotes: Templar interrogators rely on brain lock to subdue prisoners without physical violence — at least initially. In the gladiatorial arenas, a psion who brain locks an opponent before the killing blow is considered unsporting, but the crowd's displeasure matters little to the survivor. Thri-kreen clutch-minds have developed a variant that can freeze the hive-thoughts of their own kind, used only as punishment for the gravest betrayals.

name: Cloud Mind
description: You make yourself completely undetectable to the subject by erasing all awareness of your presence from its mind. First, you are invisible and inaudible to the creature. It cannot even detect your presence by means of blindsense, blindsight, scent, or tremorsense. It cannot pinpoint your location by any means. Second, the subject remains unaware of your actions, provided you do not make any attacks or cause any obvious or directly threatening changes in the subject's environment. If you attack the subject creature, the effect ends. If you take an action that creates a sustained and obvious change in the subject's environment, the subject immediately gains a new saving throw against the power.
darkSunNotes: Veiled Alliance operatives in Urik and Nibenay depend on cloud mind to move unseen past templar patrols. The power leaves no trace in the environment — no shimmer, no footprints unaccounted for — only a gap in the target's awareness. Halfling headhunters of the Forest Ridge are said to combine this power with their natural stealth to become truly invisible predators.

name: Elfsight
description: You gain low-light vision (as an elf) for the duration of the power, and you gain a +2 bonus on Search and Spot checks. In addition, you gain the Martial Weapon Proficiency feat for the longbow and the longsword.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where elves are lanky desert runners rather than woodland fae, this power taps into the ancestral predator instincts of the elven race. Manifesting elfsight grants the keen vision of an elf born to spot distant dust plumes on the horizon. Non-elven desert travelers prize this power for navigating by starlight across the trackless wastes between city-states.

name: ESP
description: Extrasensory perception allows you to read someone else's mind. You can only perceive surface or active thoughts. You cannot use ESP to explore someone's memories or delve into their subconscious. Most intelligent creatures tend to think in words, so language is a barrier to understanding. Unintelligent creatures think in pictures. Magical thought, such as a wizard uses in casting spells, is unintelligible, though you can easily recognize such thoughts as part of the spell-casting process.
augments: Maintenance cost is 6 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: In the paranoid courts of the sorcerer-kings, ESP is both the most coveted and most feared power. Every noble assumes their thoughts are being read at all times, leading to elaborate mental disciplines, coded thinking, and the ever-present arms race between telepaths and those who employ them.

name: False Sensory Input
description: This devotion allows you to falsify someone's sensory input—making the victim think he sees, hears, smells, tastes, or feels something other than what he really does. The falsehoods are somewhat limited. Nothing can be completely hidden or made to disappear, and everything must retain its general size and intensity. For example, a human could be made to look like a dwarf, but not like a parrot. A slamming door could be made to sound like a thunderclap or a cough, but not like rustling paper. Oil could be made to taste like garlic, but not like water.
augments: Maintenance cost is 4 PSPs per round. On a critical success, almost any false perception can be achieved.
darkSunNotes: Illusionists are rare on Athas, but psionicists who can feed false sensory input directly into a target's mind are far more dangerous—their deceptions cannot be disbelieved, only resisted. Veiled Alliance operatives have walked through templar checkpoints disguised as fellow templars, their victims seeing and hearing only what the psionicist wished them to perceive.

name: Feat Leech
description: You psychically steal one of the subject's psionic or metapsionic feats for the duration of this power. You are treated as if you have that feat (even if you do not meet the prerequisites), and the target loses access to the feat for the duration. If the subject has no psionic or metapsionic feats, you instead gain a +1 psionic bonus on attack rolls and saving throws.
darkSunNotes: In the cutthroat world of Athasian psionic academies, feat leech is both a training tool and a weapon. Masters use it to temporarily strip students of their hard-won techniques, forcing them to fight without the mental crutches they've developed. In actual combat, it turns an opponent's own expertise against them — a karmic reversal that resonates deeply in a world where everything of value must be taken from someone else.

name: Identify, Psionic
description: As the identify spell, except as noted here. This power determines all powers and properties of a single psionic item, including how to activate those functions (if appropriate), and how many charges are left (if any).
darkSunNotes: Psionic items are rare treasures on Athas, often relics of a more civilized age. When one surfaces in the markets of Tyr or the ruins beneath the Tablelands, psionic identify is essential to determine whether the find is a boon or a curse. Many 'artifacts' sold by elven traders turn out to be mundane trinkets, while genuine items are sometimes disguised as worthless junk to avoid templar confiscation.

name: Identity Penetration
description: Identity penetration allows you to determine the target's true identity in spite of polymorphs, illusions, disguises, and other forms of concealment. You see through all forms of physical and magical deception to perceive the creature's actual nature.
augments: Maintenance cost is 6 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: When shape-shifters, dopplegangers, or psionically disguised agents threaten the stability of a city-state, the sorcerer-kings deploy their most skilled telepaths to perform identity penetration—peeling back layers of false identity to expose the true mind beneath. No mask, mundane or psionic, survives this scrutiny for long.

name: Incarnation Awareness
description: By applying this power to another character, you can gain knowledge about their past lives. One past life can be explored per round, starting with the most powerful personalities (usually the most famous as well). You also know immediately how many times this particular personality has been killed and raised from the dead. It is up to you to put this information to use. On a critical success, all past life information is gained in one quick mental blast.
augments: Maintenance cost is 13 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, incarnation awareness can reveal connections to the ancient Green Age.

name: Inflict Pain
description: This is a particularly nasty form of torture. It causes no actual harm to the subject and leaves no marks or scars, but causes excruciating pain of any sort you desire. Only evil characters can learn this power freely; others find their alignments twisting toward evil if they wish to inflict pain. If the victim is an NPC being questioned, he must make a saving throw vs. paralyzation to withstand the agony. In combat, the victim must also save; failure imposes a 4-point penalty on attack rolls that round, or disrupts and ruins a spell being cast. On a critical success, the pain is so excruciating the victim passes out for 1d10 rounds.
augments: Maintenance cost is 2 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: Inflict pain is a favored tool of templar interrogators in the city-states of Athas.

name: Invincible Foes
description: The victim of this devotion believes that any blow struck against him will cripple or kill him. Even if the blow causes just one point of damage, the victim thinks he's dying and can no longer fight. He falls to the ground in horrible pain and won't recover for 10 rounds, even if you stop concentrating. Even if never actually struck, the victim may stop attacking and simply parry, flee, or surrender. This power can also work in reverse: an attacker can be made to believe every blow he strikes is fatal, creating an illusion of defeated foes lying on the ground. Characters who have been 'killed' can simply walk away or reposition for a +2 attack bonus. After this happens three times, the affected character must save vs. spells or become convinced his foes are unkillable.
augments: Maintenance cost is 5 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: A cruel power favored by arena handlers, invincible foes makes a gladiator perceive his opponents as unstoppable juggernauts—titans of impossible strength and skill. Many a promising fighter has been broken not by a superior enemy, but by the psychic certainty that he could never win, implanted by a psionicist in the stands.

name: Invisibility
description: Psionic invisibility differs significantly from magical invisibility. To make yourself invisible, you must individually contact each mind that you want to deceive. Once they are all contacted, you make yourself invisible to them only. You can still see yourself, as can anyone who was not contacted. Only characters within 100 yards can be affected. Invisibility is a delusion affecting one or more select characters, not an illusion affecting everyone. You can make anyone invisible, not just yourself, as long as the 'invisible' being is approximately man-sized or smaller. A separate power check and maintenance cost must be paid for each delusion. This power affects vision only; observers may still hear or smell 'invisible' creatures.
augments: Maintenance cost is 2 PSPs per round per creature deceived.
darkSunNotes: Psionic invisibility on Athas serves a grimmer purpose than mere stealth. Escaped slaves use it to flee city-states undetected, assassins employ it to reach their targets through crowded streets, and the Veiled Alliance relies on it to move preservers to safety when templar raids grow too close.

name: Mental Disruption
target: All creatures in a 10-ft.-radius burst centered on you
description: You generate a burst of telepathic static that dazes all creatures within 10 feet of you for 1 round. Creatures that fail their Will save are dazed and unable to take actions. Psionic creatures that fail their save also lose 1d4 power points.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, the radius of the burst increases by 5 feet.
darkSunNotes: Mental disruption is the telepath's panic button — a psychic shockwave that stuns everyone nearby when the manifester is cornered. In the cramped tunnels beneath Athasian cities, where Veiled Alliance cells meet in secret, the power can buy precious seconds for an escape. Gladiators have been known to use it in melee to stun clustered opponents, creating an opening for a devastating follow-up strike.

name: Mind Bar
description: Mind bar gives you 75% magic resistance against charm, confusion, ESP, fear, feeblemind, magic jar, sleep, and suggestion spells. It also offers complete protection against possession of any sort. In addition, mind bar protects against all telepathic powers except the five contact-establishing attack modes. No telepathic power can affect a barred mind unless the telepathic attacker prevails in a psychic contest. On a critical success, you gain a +5 bonus to your power score when defending in a psychic contest.
augments: Maintenance cost is 4 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: Mind bar is considered essential protection against the mental assaults common in Athasian politics.

name: Missive, Mass
target: All creatures in a 400 ft. + 40 ft./level radius, centered on you
description: As missive, except you can send a short message of twenty-five words or less to all creatures within range.
darkSunNotes: When a caravan comes under attack across a broad front, mass missive ensures every guard and drover receives the same warning simultaneously. The armies of the sorcerer-kings use mass missive for battlefield coordination, their telepathic officers broadcasting orders that cannot be intercepted by mundane scouts. The Veiled Alliance has developed coded mental shorthand that makes their mass missives unintelligible to outsiders.

name: Phobia Amplification
description: This power allows you to reach into someone's mind and discover his greatest fear, then amplify it to the point of irrationality. A victim who fails a saving throw vs. spells believes he is imminently threatened by the object of his fear, even if the situation is completely absurd. A character with a fear of falling, for example, is convinced he could pitch over a cliff at any moment—even while on an endless prairie—unless he stands absolutely still. The victim will defend himself if attacked but all actions will be geared toward protecting against the fearsome object. This fear lasts as long as you maintain the effect. On a critical success, the target's save automatically fails.
augments: Maintenance cost is 4 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: Every creature on Athas fears something—the dark, the silt, fire, the undead, the sorcerer-kings. A telepath who can find that buried fear and drag it screaming to the surface becomes a weapon of psychological destruction. Templar interrogators consider it their gentlest tool; their victims disagree.

name: Plant Mind
description: The psionicist attunes her mind to plant life, gaining a better understanding of plant existence and improved interaction with intelligent or psionic plants. Normally there is a -8 penalty for mental contact with plants, but while plant mind is active, this penalty is eliminated. Instead, the psionicist suffers a -8 penalty for contact with nonplant minds. Plant mind does not allow communication with plants but grants a +2 bonus to Charisma when dealing with plants. On an exceptional manifestation, the benefits last for 24 hours without maintenance cost.
augments: Maintain for 2 PP/round to keep the plant mind attuned.
darkSunNotes: In the Crescent Forest near Gulg, where intelligent trees and psionic plant life abound, plant mind is essential for any psionicist who wishes to negotiate with—rather than be consumed by—the forest's guardians.

name: Post-Hypnotic Suggestion
description: Any creature with Intelligence between 7 and 17 can receive a post-hypnotic suggestion. Very dim or very brilliant creatures are not susceptible. You plant a suggestion of some reasonable course of action in the creature's subconscious, along with the situation that will trigger it. When that situation arises, a power check is made with a −1 penalty for each day that has passed since the suggestion was planted. A reasonable course of action is one that does not violate the creature's alignment or class restrictions. It can be something the creature would not normally do, but if it is too strange, the creature simply won't do it. The maintenance cost is a one-time payment made when the suggestion is implanted.
augments: One-time maintenance cost of 1 PSP per level or HD of the subject.
darkSunNotes: The Shadow King of Nibenay is rumored to employ an entire cadre of telepaths who implant post-hypnotic suggestions in visiting dignitaries—sleeper commands that can be triggered years later with a whispered phrase. No ambassador who has visited the Naggaramakam can ever be fully trusted again.

name: Psychic Crush
description: Psychic crush is one of the five telepathic attack modes used to establish contact with another psionicist's mind. It is a massive assault upon all neurons in the brain, attempting to destroy them by a massive overload of signals. If used against any mind that you have contacted, the victim must make a saving throw vs. paralyzation. Failure costs the target 1d8 hit points of actual damage. On a critical success, the save automatically fails.
darkSunNotes: The most devastating telepathic attack in an Athasian psionicist's arsenal, psychic crush can stop a heart and shatter a mind in a single overwhelming assault. Arena psionicists who manifest it are both celebrated and feared—the crowd loves a dramatic kill, but no gladiator wants to face an opponent who can end the fight with a thought.

name: Psychic Impersonation
description: This power enables you to mask your own aura and thought patterns, making them match someone else's perfectly. To accomplish this, you must first probe the subject you wish to impersonate. Psychic impersonation does not alter any of your other features—it only changes your identity to other creatures with psionic powers. Even then, a psychic contest with identity penetration or probe can penetrate the disguise. While psychic impersonation is in effect, all of your psionic power scores are reduced by one.
augments: Maintenance cost is 3 PSPs per hour.
darkSunNotes: In the cutthroat politics of Athasian city-states, psychic impersonation is the ultimate tool of infiltration. A telepath who can project another person's psychic signature can fool even mind-reading security measures, walking into templar strongholds wearing the mental face of someone with clearance.

name: Recall Agony
description: The fabric of time parts to your will, revealing wounds your subject has previously received. That past pain overwhelms the subject in the present, dealing 2d6 points of damage.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage increases by 1d6 points.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, everyone carries scars — physical and psychic alike. Recall agony weaponizes a lifetime of suffering, dredging up old wounds and making them bleed anew. In the arena, telepaths who use recall agony against battle-scarred gladiators can inflict devastating damage, each recalled blow a phantom echo of obsidian blades and bone clubs that struck years ago.

name: Repugnance
description: With this power, you make something—a particular person, place, or object—completely repugnant to another character. That character is overwhelmed with loathing for the thing and will seek to destroy it as completely and quickly as possible. If this destruction is strongly against the character's alignment (such as making a temple repugnant to its cleric), the character gets a saving throw vs. spells to shake off the effect. On a critical success, no save is allowed.
augments: Maintenance cost is 8 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: The opposite of attraction, repugnance is used by Athasian hermits and outcasts to ensure they are left alone in the wastes. More sinisterly, it has been used to turn communities against specific individuals—psionically ensuring that a targeted merchant or noble becomes universally despised, their allies turning away in inexplicable disgust.

name: Sense Link, Forced
description: As sense link, except the target does not have to be willing and you can perceive any one sense the target creature currently uses. If the target fails its Will save, you are linked to one of its senses (your choice). The target can make a new Will save each round to break the link.
darkSunNotes: Templar inquisitors use forced sense links to experience a prisoner's perspective during interrogation — seeing through their eyes, feeling the tremor in their voice, tasting the dust in their mouth. It is an intimate violation that many prisoners find more disturbing than physical torture. Veiled Alliance operatives use the same power offensively, riding a templar's senses to observe patrols and security arrangements.

name: Sensory Suppression
description: The psionicist temporarily blinds or deafens an opponent by interfering with the brain's reception of sensory information. The victim's eyes or ears still function, but the mind cannot process the information. The psionicist must individually contact each target and make separate power checks. Blinded characters suffer -4 to all attacks, saves, and AC. Deafened characters suffer -1, with increased penalties in situations where hearing is critical (such as blind fighting in total darkness). On an exceptional manifestation, the victim loses all sensory information while the power is maintained.
augments: Maintain for 4 PP/round per creature to sustain the suppression.
darkSunNotes: Athasian assassins employ sensory suppression to blind guards and sentries, allowing silent infiltration of templar compounds and noble estates.

name: Sight Link
description: Sight link allows you to tap into another character's optical system. You see whatever your link sees, while your own vision is unaffected. If the linked creature is subjected to a gaze attack, you must make the appropriate saving throw or also be affected by the gaze. On a critical success, sound link is also gained for free.
augments: Maintenance cost is 5 PSPs per turn.
darkSunNotes: Thri-kreen hunt-leaders use sight link to coordinate pack tactics across vast distances, seeing through each clutch-member's eyes in rapid succession. Human merchant lords employ it to monitor their agents in distant markets, watching negotiations unfold through borrowed eyes while sitting safely in their counting houses.

name: Sound Link
description: By means of a sound link, you tap into the auditory system of another person or creature. You hear whatever your link hears. If the linked creature is subjected to an auditory attack (by sirens, shriekers, etc.), you must make the appropriate saving throw or also be affected. On a critical success, sight link is also gained for free.
augments: Maintenance cost is 4 PSPs per turn.
darkSunNotes: Paired with sight link, sound link completes the remote surveillance that Athasian spymasters depend upon. A templar handler listening through an operative's ears can coach them through a delicate negotiation or warn them of approaching danger—a whispered lifeline spanning miles of hostile desert.

name: Suggestion, Psionic
description: As the suggestion spell, except as noted here. You influence the actions of the target creature by suggesting a course of activity (limited to a sentence or two). The suggestion must be worded in such a manner as to make the activity sound reasonable.
augments: You can augment this power in one or both of the following ways. 1. If you spend 2 additional power points, this power can also affect an animal, fey, giant, magical beast, or monstrous humanoid. 2. If you spend 4 additional power points, this power can also affect an aberration, dragon, elemental, or outsider in addition to the creature types mentioned above. In addition, for every 2 additional power points you spend to achieve any of these effects, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Psionic suggestion is the most commonly litigated power in Athasian commerce — if 'litigated' is even the right word for disputes settled by templars who are themselves susceptible to the same power. Merchant princes use it to close deals; nobles use it to sway council votes; and everyone uses it to get out of trouble with the law. The result is a society where no agreement is truly trusted unless sealed with psionic verification.

name: Telempathic Projection
description: Telempathic projection allows you to send emotions to everyone who has been contacted within a common 10-yard diameter. This power cannot radically change a character's emotional state, however. A character who is very angry could be made only slightly angry, for example, but not happy. Empathic changes are a matter of degrees, no more. On a critical success, emotions can be drastically altered (love to hatred, etc.) while this power is maintained.
augments: Maintenance cost is 4 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where survival often depends on the cooperation of strangers, telempathic projection can forge instant trust between suspicious parties—or false trust, which is far more useful. Merchant caravans employ psionicists to project calm and goodwill during tense trade negotiations with raider clans.

name: Tongues, Psionic
description: As the tongues spell, except as noted here. You can speak and understand the spoken or signed language of any intelligent creature, whether it is a racial tongue or a regional dialect.
augments: If you spend 2 additional power points, this power can be manifested on a willing creature touched instead.
darkSunNotes: Athas is a world of a thousand tongues — the clicking speech of the thri-kreen, the guttural dialects of the dwarven villages, the ancient trade-tongue of the elven tribes, and the bastardized Common spoken in each city-state with its own local flavor. Psionic tongues is essential for any traveler who ventures beyond their home walls, and for the merchant houses whose profits depend on clear communication across cultural boundaries.

name: Crisis of Breath
savingThrow: Will negates, Fortitude partial; see text
description: You compel the subject to purge its entire store of air in one explosive exhalation, and thereby disrupt the subject's autonomic breathing cycle. The subject's lungs do not automatically function again while the power's duration lasts. If the target succeeds on a Will save, it is unaffected. If it fails, it can still continue to breathe by taking a standard action in each round to gasp for breath. An affected creature can attempt to take actions normally instead of consciously controlling its breathing, but each round it does so, beginning in the round when it failed its Will save, the subject risks blacking out from lack of oxygen. It must succeed on a Fortitude save at the end of any of its turns in which it did not consciously take a breath. The DC of this save increases by 1 in every consecutive round after the first one that goes by without a breath. If a subject fails a Fortitude save, it is disabled (0 hp). In the following round, it drops to -1 hit points and is dying.
augments: You can augment this power in one or more of the following ways. 1. If you spend 2 additional power points, this power can also affect an animal, fey, giant, magical beast, or monstrous humanoid. 2. If you spend 4 additional power points, this power can also affect an aberration, dragon, or outsider. 3. If you spend 6 additional power points, this power can affect up to four creatures all within a 20-ft.-radius burst. In addition, for every 2 additional power points you spend to achieve any of these effects, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Under the crimson sun of Athas, where the air itself is a weapon — thin, superheated, and scarce — crisis of breath is among the most feared telepathic attacks. In the gladiatorial pits of Draj, telepaths who specialize in this power are called 'dust lungs.' Their victims gasp and claw at their own throats while the crowd roars approval, the dry Athasian air offering no relief.

name: Domination
description: You project signals from your own mind into the mind of one other person or creature. As a result, the subject is forced to do nearly anything you wish. The dominated subject knows what is happening but cannot resist your will. Commands are given mentally and automatically. The victim's abilities are neither diminished nor enhanced by this power. The subject can be forced to use any power or ability he normally possesses. If the victim is later forced to do something completely abhorrent or against his alignment, he receives another saving throw to regain his free will. The cost to establish and maintain this power equals twice the cost to contact the victim.
augments: Maintenance cost equals twice the victim's contact cost per round. For every 2 additional power points spent, the save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: Domination is a feared tool of Athasian sorcerer-kings, who use it to control templars and slaves alike.

name: Ejection
description: Ejection is the final defense against unwanted contact. If another psionicist has forced contact with your mind, or was granted contact and is now abusing it, you can eject the intruder. The cost of ejection is twice the opponent's contact power score. Ejection is risky: on low power check results, the user may suffer side effects including temporary loss of access to sciences, disciplines, PSPs, Constitution, or hit points.
darkSunNotes: When a foreign mind has taken root—a dominating telepath, a possessing undead from the Gray, or the insidious tendrils of a psionic parasite—ejection is the violent solution. Athasian psychic healers describe it as reaching into the patient's consciousness and physically hurling the intruder out, a brutal but necessary purging.

name: Fate Link
description: You intertwine your own fate with that of another creature. If either being experiences pain, both feel it. When one loses hit points, both lose the same amount. If either dies, the other must immediately make a saving throw vs. death to avoid the same fate. This creates a powerful mutual bond of shared suffering.
augments: Maintenance cost is 5 PSPs per turn. For every 4 additional power points spent, the range doubles.
darkSunNotes: Fate link is sometimes used by Athasian gladiators to ensure mutual destruction if betrayed.

name: Hallucination
description: The psionicist projects visions or images into a contacted victim's mind—a psionic phantasmal force. The psionicist can make the victim believe he is seeing, hearing, or feeling almost anything: hallucinatory monsters, soldiers disguised as a grove of palms, or sand that tastes like cool water. If carefully constructed to match the victim's expectations, the hallucination may not allow a saving throw. Physical effects are psychosomatic—the victim believes the damage but is not truly harmed. Maintaining a hallucination requires full concentration; making a physical attack or initiating another power grants the victim a saving throw with +4 bonus. On an exceptional manifestation, the victim saves at -4 and suffers 20% of any damage as real.
augments: Maintain for 6 PP/round to continue the hallucination.
darkSunNotes: In a world of brutal pragmatism, Athasian telepaths use hallucination to devastating effect—making enemies believe they are surrounded by tembo, that the oasis ahead is real, or that the merchant's water is fresh and clean.

name: Mind Trap
description: You set up a trap in your mind against psionic intruders. Anyone who targets you with a telepathic power or attempts to read your mind must make a Will save or lose 1d6 power points. The power also deals 1d6 points of damage to non-psionic creatures that attempt to use mind-affecting effects on you.
darkSunNotes: The Order's most valuable lesson: never enter a mind uninvited. Mind trap turns a psionicist's own consciousness into a weapon, punishing those who would pry into their thoughts. In the court of every sorcerer-king, high-ranking officials maintain mind traps at all times, creating a web of psychic defenses that makes telepathic espionage a dangerous gamble.

name: Mindlink
description: Mindlink allows you to communicate wordlessly with any intelligent creature you can contact (Intelligence 5 or greater). This is two-way communication. It is not the same as mind-reading because you only receive thoughts which the other party wants to send. Language is not a barrier. Distance affects your ability to make contact, but it has no other effect on the link once established.
augments: Maintenance cost is 8 PSPs per round. For every 2 additional power points spent, you may link one additional creature into the communication.
darkSunNotes: Mindlink is essential for communication across the vast Athasian wastes where distances make speech impossible.

name: Psionic Blast
description: Psionic blast is a wave of brain force which can jolt a subject's mind like a piece of shocking news. This is one of the five telepathic attack modes used to establish contact with another psionicist's mind. If used against a mind that has already been contacted, the subject must save vs. death. Failure means he loses 80% of his remaining hit points in his mind—he only thinks they are gone. He will pass out when the remaining 20% are lost in his mind, but won't die unless all hit points are actually lost. This effect wears off after six turns. This power has three ranges: at medium range the power score is reduced by 2, at long range by 5.
darkSunNotes: Psionic blast echoes across the Gray on Athas, sometimes alerting undead to the psionicist's presence.

name: Solicit Psicrystal
description: Your psicrystal takes over concentrating on a power you have already manifested and are maintaining through concentration. While the psicrystal concentrates on the power, you are free to manifest another power or take other actions normally. The psicrystal makes Concentration checks as you would.
darkSunNotes: The bond between an Athasian psionicist and their psicrystal runs deeper than on most worlds — in a land where trusting another person can get you killed, a crystalline fragment of your own psyche is the only confidant you can truly rely on. Delegating concentration to a psicrystal is like lending your focus to a part of yourself, a seamless mental handoff perfected over countless desert meditations.

name: Synaptic Static
description: Synaptic static interferes with all psionic activity within a given area. Even you are affected—when you create static, you cannot use any other power simultaneously. However, you may prevent others from using their powers. Anyone who tries to use a psionic power within the area of effect must fight you in a psychic contest. If the opponent succeeds, his power functions normally; if not, his power fails. Exposure to synaptic static for more than five rounds gives anyone a splitting headache (no game effect, but NPCs and animals become irritable). This power has three ranges: at medium range the power score is reduced by 2, at long range by 5.
augments: Maintenance cost is 10 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: Synaptic static is used by the Order to create psionic dead zones around sensitive locations.

name: Tower of Iron Will
description: Tower of iron will is one of the five telepathic defense modes against unwanted contact. It relies upon the superego to build an unassailable haven for the brain. Like intellect fortress, tower of iron will has an area of effect beyond your own mind, though at 3 feet it is very limited. You can initiate one other psionic power during the round in which you use tower of iron will.
darkSunNotes: The most powerful group telepathic defense known on Athas, the tower of iron will shelters allied minds behind a fortress of shared psychic resolve. When the Order's agents face a sorcerer-king's telepathic corps, the tower is what keeps them alive—a communal bastion where individual will becomes collective, unbreakable determination.

name: Correspond
target: You and one other creature that has an Intelligence score of 3 or higher
description: You forge a telepathic bond with your target. You can communicate telepathically through the bond even if you do not share a common language. No special power or influence is established as a result of the bond. Once the bond is formed, it works over any distance (although not from one plane to another).
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's duration increases by 1 round/level.
darkSunNotes: Across the vast emptiness of the Tablelands, correspond is the lifeline of the merchant caravans. House agents stationed days apart maintain telepathic bonds to relay warnings of gith raiders, approaching sandstorms, or shifting silt currents. The Veiled Alliance uses correspond to coordinate operations across multiple city-states, its messages untraceable by templar scryers.

name: Death Urge
description: You plant a hidden death-Loss in the subject's subconscious. On the subject's next turn, it looks for the quickest method to end its life and attempts to do so. The subject takes whatever action is most expedient to kill itself, attempting to do so on its next turn after you manifest this power. If armed, the subject attacks itself with its own weapon, using its full attack bonus. If the subject has no weapon or other means of easily killing itself, it will provoke attacks of opportunity from the nearest opponent, and will not try to defend itself against those attacks.
augments: For every 4 additional power points you spend, this power's save DC increases by 2 and its duration increases by 1 round.
darkSunNotes: The templars of Gulg whisper of telepaths who can compel enemies to fall upon their own obsidian blades. Death urge is the signature power of the Shadow King's most feared inquisitors, who use it to make heretics and spies destroy themselves in public as a warning to others. In the arena, a gladiator suddenly turning his weapon on himself draws gasps even from the most jaded crowd.

name: Dominate, Psionic
description: As the dominate person spell, except as noted here. You can control the actions of any humanoid creature through a telepathic link that you establish with the subject's mind.
augments: You can augment this power in one or more of the following ways. 1. If you spend 2 additional power points, this power can also affect an animal, fey, giant, magical beast, or monstrous humanoid. 2. If you spend 4 additional power points, this power can also affect an aberration, dragon, elemental, or outsider in addition to the creature types mentioned above. In addition, for every 2 additional power points you spend to achieve any of these effects, this power's save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: The sorcerer-kings' most terrifying tool of governance is not their armies or their defiler magic — it is psionic domination. Lalali-Puy of Gulg is said to maintain dozens of dominated agents throughout the Tablelands, their own personalities suppressed beneath her iron will. In the arenas, unscrupulous owners have been caught dominating their gladiators mid-fight, puppeting them through impossible feats to win wagers.

name: Mindflame
target: 5 ft. by 20 ft. cone (up to 6 creatures)
description: The psionicist projects a wave of deadly mental force that can destroy lesser intellects. The attack affects up to six creatures in a 20-foot cone. Psionicists can protect themselves with mind blank or other defenses, but undefended creatures suffer effects based on their level: less than 1 HD—comatose 1d6 days, lose 1d3 Intelligence; 1-2 HD—unconscious 2d4 turns, lose 1 Intelligence; 2+1-4 HD—paralyzed 1d4 turns; 4+1-8 HD—stunned 2d6 rounds; 8+1+ HD—dazed 1d3 rounds. All victims may save vs. death to avoid effects. Comatose creatures must save again or die; unconscious creatures must save or lapse into coma. Stunned creatures cannot act and suffer -2 AC. Using mindflame more than once per day imposes a -4 penalty, and frequent use shifts alignment toward evil.
darkSunNotes: Mindflame is considered one of the most terrifying powers on Athas—a telepathic weapon of mass destruction that can leave entire guard patrols braindead. The Order strictly monitors its use, and psionicists who employ it recklessly are hunted down.

name: Mindwipe
description: Mindwiping is a crude form of psychic surgery which affects the subject's intellect, functioning as a temporary lobotomy. You systematically seal off portions of the subject's mind, making whatever knowledge was contained there inaccessible. Each round of mindwiping reduces the victim's Intelligence and Wisdom scores by 1 point and reduces his experience level or hit dice by 1 point. These reductions affect spellcasting ability, languages, proficiencies, and magical defense adjustment. Mindwipe only seals off information; it does not erase it. All losses can be restored through psychic surgery.
augments: Maintenance cost is 8 PSPs per round. For every 4 additional power points spent, the save DC increases by 1.
darkSunNotes: The Order uses mindwipe as a tool of last resort against rogue psionicists who threaten the balance of power.

name: Modify Memory, Psionic
description: As the modify memory spell, except as noted here. You reach into the subject's mind and modify up to 5 minutes of its memories in one of several ways: eliminate all memory of an event, allow the subject to recall with perfect clarity an event it actually experienced, change the details of an event, or implant a memory of an event that never happened.
darkSunNotes: The templars of Athas consider psionic modify memory their most valuable intelligence tool. Agents sent into the Veiled Alliance can have their true loyalties hidden even from themselves, activated only by a predetermined mental trigger. Conversely, captured Alliance members have their memories of safe houses and contacts erased, leaving gaps they know exist but can never fill — a torment worse than the information itself.

name: Personality Parasite
description: You plant a foreign personality fragment in the subject's mind. This fragment competes with the subject for control, causing the target to lose 1 power point per round while the parasite is active. The subject is also dazed for 1 round upon initial manifestation. The subject can expel the parasite by succeeding on a Concentration check (DC equal to the save DC of this power).
augments: For every additional power point you spend, the personality parasite drains 1 additional power point per round from the subject.
darkSunNotes: Personality parasite is disturbingly common among the psionicists of Raam, where factional infighting has elevated psychic sabotage to an art form. Victims describe the sensation as having 'someone else thinking behind their eyes' — an alien presence that devours their mental reserves. The thri-kreen find this power particularly abhorrent, as their hive-mind culture views parasitic consciousness as the ultimate violation.

name: Power Leech
description: Your brow erupts with an arc of crackling psychic energy. For each round you maintain concentration on this power, the subject loses 1d6 power points, and you gain 1 of those points (the rest are lost). The drained creature can attempt a Will save each round to break free.
darkSunNotes: Power leech mirrors the defiler's relationship with the land — one being thriving at the expense of another. Telepaths who specialize in this power are called 'mind-defilers' by their peers, a comparison that carries the full weight of Athasian hatred. Yet in a world where power points can mean the difference between life and death, few psionicists can afford the luxury of moral objections.

name: Probe
description: A probe is similar to ESP, but allows you to dig much deeper into a subject's subconscious. If the subject fails a saving throw, all his memories and knowledge are accessible to you—from memories deep below the surface to those still fresh in the subject's mind. The information gained is not necessarily true, but it is true as far as the subject knows. You can learn the answer to one question per round. A probe can be carried out against a conscious, resisting subject, or even during melee. If a probed wizard or cleric tries casting a spell, you know both that a spell is being cast and its general effects.
augments: Maintenance cost is 9 PSPs per round. For every 4 additional power points spent, you may ask one additional question per round.
darkSunNotes: Athasian templars sometimes employ psionicists to probe suspected defilers and preservers.

name: Psychic Reformation
description: When manifesting this power, the subject can choose to spend its most recently gained skill ranks differently, choose a different feat, or choose a different power known (all subject to the usual requirements for those choices). XP Cost: 50 XP per level or Hit Die of the target.
darkSunNotes: Psychic reformation offers something almost unheard of on brutal Athas: a second chance. A gladiator who chose the wrong fighting style, a preserver who selected an impractical power — all can be remade. The psionicists of the Order sometimes offer reformation as payment for services rendered, reshaping a warrior's training to meet new threats. It is said that Hamanu of Urik underwent psychic reformation dozens of times over the millennia, endlessly optimizing himself for war.

name: Psychic Surgery
description: Psychic surgery allows you to repair psychic damage. You can even operate on yourself, though your power score is reduced by 5 in this case. Phobias, aversions, idiocy, comas, seizures—all these psychic ailments can be treated and cured. Curses or magical conditions such as geases and charms cannot be cured. This power cannot cure possession, but it can confirm that the problem is possession and force the possessing entity into psionic combat. Most psychic ailments can be cured in one turn or less. Psychic surgery has two special uses: (1) it can help non-psionicists unleash their wild powers, granting a bonus to their wild talent roll, and (2) it can make the effect of any maintainable telepathic power permanent on a non-psionicist subject with no maintenance cost.
augments: Maintenance cost is 10 PSPs per turn during the procedure.
darkSunNotes: Psychic surgeons are highly valued on Athas and often serve the sorcerer-kings directly.

name: Schism
description: Your mind cleaves into two independent parts. Each part functions in complete autonomy, like two characters in one body. Your new 'second mind' does not control your body physically but can take one standard action per round that is purely mental (such as manifesting a power). Your second mind uses your statistics but can only manifest powers of 6th level or lower that have a manifesting time of 1 standard action or less. Both minds share your power point reserve.
darkSunNotes: Schism is both a miracle and a warning on Athas. The power to split one's consciousness in two grants extraordinary multitasking — one mind fights while the other manifests powers. But the Order warns that prolonged use can lead to genuine psychological fracture, the two halves of the mind developing distinct personalities that war for dominance. More than one Athasian telepath has ended their days as two people trapped in one skull.

name: Superior Invisibility
description: Superior invisibility is very much like Invisibility, but it masks you completely. You make no sound and have no smell, though you can still be felt if touched. If you attack someone physically, you automatically break contact with that character. Each creature to be affected must be individually contacted.
augments: Maintenance cost is 5 PSPs per round per creature affected.
darkSunNotes: The ultimate expression of psionic concealment on Athas, superior invisibility renders its user utterly undetectable—invisible, inaudible, scentless. The Shadow King's legendary shadow consorts are whispered to walk permanently shrouded by this power, unseen guardians who could be standing at your shoulder right now.

name: Mass Contact
description: The High Science of Telepathy. The psionicist can establish contact with many minds simultaneously instead of one at a time. Range and life order modifiers from the contact power apply normally. Initial cost varies by subject level: 1-5 HD (3 PP each), 6-10 HD (8 PP each), 11-15 HD (13 PP each), 16-20 HD (18 PP each), 21+ HD (25 PP each). Maintenance is 1 PP per round per subject. All targets must be within 100 yards of each other, though range can extend by linking distant subjects. The psionicist chooses which minds to contact. Power checks can be statistically averaged for large groups. Once mass contact is established, the psionicist can follow up with any telepathic power, paying its cost for each individual. Mass contact combined with domination is more effective than mass domination alone.
augments: Maintain for 1 PP/round per subject. Initial cost varies by target level.
darkSunNotes: Mass contact is the ultimate tool of Athasian psionic warfare. A telepath who can simultaneously contact an entire squad of enemy soldiers, then follow up with domination or invincible foes, can turn the tide of battle in a single round.

name: Mass Domination
description: This power is identical to domination except you can control up to five creatures simultaneously. Each creature must be contacted and dominated individually. The maintenance cost—twice the victim's level or hit dice—must be paid for each dominated figure.
augments: Maintenance cost is twice each victim's level or HD per round. For every 6 additional power points spent, you may dominate one additional creature.
darkSunNotes: Sorcerer-kings have been known to use mass domination to control entire squads of soldiers.

name: Mind Probe
description: All the target's memories and knowledge are accessible to you, from memories deep below the surface to those still within the creature's active awareness. You can learn the answer to one question per round, to the best of the subject's knowledge. If the subject succeeds on a Will save, it is not required to answer the question; however, making a save does not end the power. You can ask the subject a new question (or the same question) in subsequent rounds. On a failed Will save, the subject must truthfully and fully answer the question.
darkSunNotes: The templar inquisitors of every city-state maintain chambers specifically designed for mind probe interrogations. No secret is safe — the locations of hidden water caches, Veiled Alliance contacts, or buried treasure can all be torn from a prisoner's mind. The Order teaches resistance techniques, but against a sufficiently powerful telepath, even the most disciplined mind eventually breaks.

name: Power Resistance
description: The creature touched gains power resistance equal to 12 + your manifester level.
darkSunNotes: In a world where nearly every sentient being has some degree of psionic ability, power resistance is the equivalent of wearing armor against an invisible sword. The sorcerer-kings' elite guard are routinely granted this protection before any public appearance. Gladiators blessed with power resistance command premium prices, as they can shrug off the mental assaults that would fell lesser fighters.

name: Psychic Clone
description: When this power is initiated, a clone of you steps out of your body. It is in every way identical to your original form, except that it has no substance. You can see and hear what the clone sees and hears. The clone will do anything you wish, and your psyche goes with it, enabling the clone to perform all of your psionic powers. However, as long as the clone exists, your real body is practically inert—retaining only smell, taste, and touch. You cannot move, see, or hear. All your psionic powers are transferred to the clone. Your immobile body can be slain without combat rolls. The clone can travel up to 60 feet from your body and is impervious to all forms of attack except psionic or mental attacks, which affect you directly.
augments: Maintenance cost is 5 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: On Athas, where death comes swiftly and the Gray hungers for departing souls, psychic clone represents the ultimate insurance policy. A psionicist implants a copy of their consciousness in another body—a willing host or an unsuspecting victim—ensuring that even if the sorcerer-kings destroy their flesh, their mind endures.

name: Shatter Mind Blank
target: One creature within range protected by mind blank
description: You shatter a mind blank (psionic or otherwise) currently active on the target. If the target fails its Will save, the mind blank is dispelled, leaving the target vulnerable to telepathic powers and effects.
darkSunNotes: Among the psionicists of Athas, mind blank is considered the ultimate defense — and shatter mind blank is the skeleton key that defeats it. When the sorcerer-kings' agents discover a mind-blanked individual, it triggers an immediate alert: only someone with dangerous secrets requires such protection. The ensuing psychic confrontation — one mind trying to pry open defenses while the other fights to maintain them — is the stuff of Athasian legend.

name: Split Personality
description: This is the power to divide your mind into two independent parts. Each part functions in complete autonomy, like two characters in one body. Both parts communicate fully and both can use psionic powers simultaneously. This means you can use twice as many psionic powers per round, though your total PSPs remain the same with both personalities drawing from the pool. Alternatively, one personality can use psionic powers while the other does something else—converse, ponder a puzzle, or control the body in melee. Mental attacks affect only half of the mind, and contact must be established separately with each half. If one half is destroyed, controlled, or subdued, the other half continues fighting independently. Requires 10th level.
augments: Maintenance cost is 6 PSPs per round.
darkSunNotes: The mental discipline required to maintain two independent trains of thought simultaneously is grueling even by Athasian standards. Those who master split personality can manifest powers while carrying on conversation, or maintain concentration on two effects at once—a terrifying advantage in the psychic dueling rings of Raam.

name: Switch Personality
description: You can literally put yourself in another person's body. This science allows you to switch your own mind with someone else's—in effect exchanging bodies. The switch is permanent and lasts until you use this power to reverse it. Each character gains the other's physical attributes (Strength, Constitution, Dexterity, hit points). However, both minds retain all their own knowledge and knowledge-based abilities such as THAC0, proficiencies, and psionic powers. The switch takes a turn to complete, and both characters must make system shock saving throws using their new Constitution scores. Failure results in a coma for 1d100 hours. Bodies that have undergone a personality switch degenerate: both characters must make a Constitution check each day or lose 1 point of Constitution.
