This loss reverses when personalities are restored to their proper bodies, recovering 1 point per day.
darkSunNotes: Some Athasian nobles are rumored to extend their lives by switching bodies with younger hosts.

name: Ultrablast
description: You can overwhelm and damage nearby psyches by casting thought waves in all directions. You 'grumble' psychically for three rounds, then your consciousness bursts forth and a horrid psychic scream penetrates all minds within 50 feet. All characters within the area must save vs. paralyzation. Failure means they pass out for 2d6 turns. Those who pass out must immediately save again; failing a second time means they lose all psionic power permanently (only psychic surgery can restore it). Although the blast does not affect you, if the power check fails, you become comatose for 1d10 days. Requires 10th level. On a critical success, creatures of 3 HD or less who fail their save die instantly.
darkSunNotes: Ultrablast is a devastating weapon in the psychic arena battles of Athas.

name: Cloud Mind, Mass
description: As cloud mind, except as noted above. Each creature is affected individually.
darkSunNotes: When the Veiled Alliance must move an entire cell through a city undetected, mass cloud mind is the power of choice. During the fall of Kalak, Alliance psionicists reportedly used this power to smuggle dozens of freedom fighters past the templar cordons surrounding the Golden Tower. The sorcerer-kings have since demanded their defilers develop specific countermeasures.

name: Co-Opt Concentration
description: You take over control of a power that was manifested by the subject and that must be maintained through concentration. Once you wrest control of the power from the subject, you have several options: Allow the power to function as normal; keep the power targeted on the subject (if a personal power) but decide how the power fulfills its function each round; retarget the power on yourself (if a personal power); or choose not to concentrate on the co-opted power in the next round, ending the power at that point. When the duration of co-opt concentration expires, the power you took control of ends (even if this would mean that the power ends earlier than normal).
darkSunNotes: In the psychic duels between the sorcerer-kings' champions, co-opt concentration is the ultimate expression of mental dominance — seizing control of an opponent's own power and turning it against them. The Order's masters teach this technique only to their most disciplined students, for the mental intimacy required to hijack another's concentration can leave the manifester vulnerable to psychic backlash.

name: Mind Switch
description: You attempt to take control of a nearby living creature, temporarily shifting your mind (and soul) into its body, and its mind into your body. You can target any creature whose Hit Dice are equal to or less than your manifester level. You possess the target's body and force the creature's mind into your body. You can't choose to activate the body's extraordinary or supernatural abilities. The creature's mental ability scores are replaced by your own. You can freely access your own powers. The target creature, in your body, can use your powers if it knows how. You can use your mind switch to transfer back into your original body whenever you desire, which also returns the target creature's mind to its own body. Otherwise, the switch lasts until the duration expires.
darkSunNotes: Mind switch is the ultimate infiltration tool in a world where paranoia is a survival trait. Veiled Alliance agents have used it to temporarily inhabit the bodies of templar officials, gaining access to sealed vaults and forbidden knowledge. The sorcerer-kings are said to use a permanent version of this power to escape old, failing bodies and inhabit young, vital ones — an obscene form of immortality that makes every citizen of their cities a potential vessel.

name: Crisis of Life
description: You interrupt the subject's autonomic heart rhythm, extinguishing the spark of life. The subject must make a Fortitude save or die. Even on a successful save, the subject takes 7d6 points of damage.
augments: For every additional power point you spend, this power's damage on a successful save increases by 1d6 points.
darkSunNotes: Hamanu of Urik is said to have demonstrated this power before his assembled armies, stopping the hearts of a dozen captured rebels with nothing more than a thought. The victim simply collapses, unmarked, their life-force snuffed like a candle in a sandstorm. Templar executioners consider it the cleanest method of capital punishment — no mess, no spectacle, just silence.

name: Decerebrate
description: With decerebrate, you selectively destroy a portion of the subject's brain stem. If the target fails its Fortitude save, it becomes a permanent vegetable — alive but with no higher brain function. The subject drops to a state equivalent to Intelligence 0 and is permanently helpless. Only psychic chirurgery, reality revision, or similar effects can restore the victim.
darkSunNotes: Nibenay's Shadow Guard uses decerebrate as the ultimate punishment for traitors — leaving the body alive while erasing the mind within. The victim is kept alive indefinitely, a hollow shell displayed in the palace as an object lesson. Some say the Zwuun of the Pristine Tower was created through a similar process performed on a massive scale, an entire civilization's minds wiped clean.

name: Insanity
description: As the insanity spell, except as noted here. The affected creature suffers from a continuous confusion effect, as the spell.
augments: For every 2 additional power points you spend, this power's save DC increases by 1, and the power can affect an additional target. Any additional target cannot be more than 15 feet from another target of the power.
darkSunNotes: The Gray — that bleak dimension between life and death — is said to drive those who glimpse it to madness. Psionicists who manifest insanity claim to channel a fraction of that existential horror directly into the victim's mind. Borys the Dragon left entire cities in the grip of psionic insanity during the Cleansing Wars, the survivors babbling and clawing at shadows for generations afterward.

name: Metaconcert
target: You and up to nine other willing psionic creatures in range
description: You link your psychic might with other psionic creatures, creating a composite mind. When you manifest metaconcert, a number of power points you designate flow from each participant into a collective pool. One individual is chosen as the conductor. The pool can be used to manifest powers, with the conductor choosing the power and paying the cost from the collective pool. Metaconcert grants a +1 bonus to the DC of powers manifested from the pool for every two participants beyond the first.
darkSunNotes: The great psionic collectives of Athas — the Order, the Hidden Council, the Thri-Kreen clutch-minds — all practice metaconcert as their highest art. When Kalak attempted his transformation into a dragon, it was a metaconcert of freed psions that helped shatter his defenses. The sorcerer-kings fear nothing more than organized psionic resistance, for a metaconcert of even modest psions can rival their individual might.

name: Reddopsi
description: When you manifest reddopsi, powers targeted against you are reflected back at the original manifester. This power affects only powers that have you as a target (it does not affect area powers). The power is reflected back at the original manifester, who becomes the target of the power. Only powers of up to 6th level can be reflected.
darkSunNotes: Reddopsi is the mirror-shield of the mind, beloved by kineticists who tire of being targets. In psychic duels between the champions of rival city-states, manifesting reddopsi transforms the defender into a living trap — every attack rebounds upon the attacker. The most famous use of reddopsi occurred when a nameless psion of Tyr reflected the Shadow King's own domination attempt back upon him, buying precious seconds for the city's defenders.

name: Sequester, Psionic
target: One willing creature or one object (up to a 2-ft. cube/level) touched
description: As the sequester spell, except as noted here. The subject becomes invisible to sight and undetectable by psionic means. The subject is also rendered comatose for the duration. If a living creature is sequestered, it does not age, breathe, grow hungry, sleep, or regain power points while sequestered.
darkSunNotes: The vaults beneath the sorcerer-kings' palaces are said to contain sequestered prisoners from millennia past — ancient enemies preserved in ageless stasis, hidden from all detection. Some are kept as trophies; others as insurance, their unique knowledge preserved for the day it might be needed. Rumors persist that entire caches of Blue Age artifacts have been sequestered beneath the Sea of Silt, invisible and waiting.

name: Mind Seed
target: One touched Medium or smaller humanoid
description: You impress the totality of your psyche into a subject's subconscious. If the subject fails to resist the power, you implant a copy of your personality within its mind. Over the course of the next seven days, the copy slowly displaces the subject's own personality and memories. The subject's own personality effectively dies, replaced by your duplicate. The duplicate has your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores, but retains the subject's physical scores. It gains your class levels, feats, and skill ranks (to the extent the subject's body allows).
darkSunNotes: The sorcerer-kings' path to immortality may have begun with mind seed. By implanting copies of their psyche into dozens of hosts, they ensured that some fragment of themselves would survive any catastrophe. Dregoth the Undead King is rumored to have seeded hundreds of subjects over the millennia, creating a network of sleeper agents who awaken at his will. On Athas, where identity itself can be stolen, trust is the rarest commodity of all.

name: Recall Death
description: As recall agony, but far more potent. The fabric of time parts to your will, revealing the subject's own death — its terrified final moments, its agony, and its last breath. The subject must succeed on a Will save or die from the shock of the experience. Even on a successful save, the subject takes 5d6 points of damage.
darkSunNotes: Recall death reaches through time itself to show the victim their own inevitable end — a final, gasping moment under the crimson sun, alone, forgotten, with the last of their water spent. On Athas, where death stalks every shadow, this power resonates with a primal terror that even the bravest cannot wholly resist. The Gray itself seems to reach through the power, eager to claim its due.

name: Apopsi
description: You delete 1d4 powers permanently from the subject's mind. You specify the level of each power, and the DM randomly determines which of the subject's powers is actually deleted. Psychic chirurgery or reality revision can be used to restore the lost powers, but it must be performed within 1 week of losing the powers. XP Cost: 50 XP per level of the deleted powers.
darkSunNotes: The Order has long forbidden apopsi, calling it 'the unmaking.' Yet the sorcerer-kings perfected it centuries ago to cripple rival psionicists. In the arenas of Tyr, condemned psions were once stripped of their powers before execution — not as mercy, but as entertainment, the crowd jeering as the victim's mind was hollowed out one talent at a time.

name: Microcosm
target: One creature; or one or more creatures within a 15-ft.-radius sphere
description: This power enables you to warp the subject's sense of reality, trapping it within an imaginary world of its own creation. The subject's body lies in a catatonic state, but its mind lives in a dreamscape where it believes it is living a normal life. Creatures with 100 or fewer hit points are affected automatically; creatures with more than 100 hit points can make a Will save to resist. An affected creature can be freed only by psychic chirurgery, reality revision, or similar effects.
darkSunNotes: Legend speaks of a great psionicist of the Green Age who, horrified by the devastation of the Cleansing Wars, imprisoned himself within a microcosm rather than witness the death of his world. His body supposedly lies in stasis beneath the ruins of a forgotten city, dreaming of an Athas covered in oceans and forests. Some wonder whether all of Athas might be the microcosm of some dreaming god.

name: Mind Switch, True
description: As mind switch, except the switch is permanent. You permanently move your mind into the target's body, and the target's mind is permanently moved into your body. You cannot switch back unless you manifest true mind switch again. XP Cost: 10,000 XP.
darkSunNotes: The ultimate act of psychic parasitism. True mind switch is how the immortal tyrants of Athas have ruled for millennia — when one body fails, they simply take another. The Dragon of Tyr was said to have worn a thousand faces over the centuries. Every person you meet on Athas could be someone else entirely, their true self screaming inside a stolen body somewhere, or simply dead.

name: Psychic Chirurgery
description: You can repair psychic damage or alter the mental functioning of a creature. Psychic chirurgery can restore a drained or damaged ability score to its original value, remove any compulsion or charm effect, restore sanity, or even implant a power in the subject's mind (the subject must pay the XP cost for learning the power). The power can also be used to undo the effects of apopsi, decerebrate, or similar powers. XP Cost: 50 XP per manifester level of the effect to be reversed, or 1,000 XP for implanting a power.
darkSunNotes: The supreme healing art of the mind. On Athas, where psychic damage is as common as physical injury, psychic chirurgery is performed only by the most skilled and trusted telepaths. The Order maintains a sacred roster of chirurgeons who travel the Tablelands, offering their services to the desperate. The sorcerer-kings keep their own chirurgeons in gilded cages, using them to repair favored servants — and to reshape the minds of those who displease them.
